topic: limited-samadhi / how much concentration the lower fruits require

key_loci: AN 3.86 (Sikkha / Pathama-sikkhattaya) — the mattaso-kari vs paripurakari gradient assigning lower fruits to partial-concentration trainee
AN 3.87 / AN 3.88 (the sikkha trilogy continuation) — fruit-by-training-degree matrix
AN 9.12 Saupadisesa — even the mattaso-kari (graded trainee) does not die un-attained; nine persons with remainder
Puggalapannatti — the seven noble persons (kayasakkhi, pannavimutta, ubhatobhagavimutta, ditthippatta, saddhavimutta, dhammanusari, saddhanusari) and the sikkha-degree puggalas
MN 70 Kitagiri — pannavimutta vs kayasakkhi vs ubhatobhagavimutta; the eight liberations 'touched with the body'; gradual instruction not from the start
AN 4.41 Samadhibhavana — four developments of concentration graded by fruit (pleasant abiding ... destruction of taints)
AN 4.170 Yuganaddha — samatha-first / vipassana-first / yoked / dhammuddhacca routes to arahantship (seed of vipassanayanika)
SN 12.23 Upanisa — samahito yathabhutam janati passati; samadhi as proximate cause of knowing-and-seeing
SN 45.8 / DN 22 — samma-samadhi defined as the four jhanas (the maximalist definition)
Dhammapada 372 — natthi jhanam apannassa, panna natthi ajhayato (no jhana/no wisdom couplet)
SN 56.11 Dhammacakkappavattana + the dhammacakkhu-arose narratives — on-the-spot stream-entry during discourse with no jhana mention
Sona Sutta (AN 6.55) — the lute simile; over-taut/over-slack effort, concentration has a correct non-maximal level
Visuddhimagga — the seven purifications; citta-visuddhi defined as upacara OR appana (the commentarial floor set at access); khanikasamadhi; tividha samadhi (parikamma/upacara/appana)
Dhammasangani / Vibhanga — samadhi definitional pericope (thiti, santhiti, avikkhepo, ekaggata); samadhindriya as ekaggata; paritta/mahaggata/appamana object-grading
Anguttara 'four samadhi-developments' commentary + the lokiya/lokuttara-samadhi sorting in atthakatha
Sallekha / anupubbikatha pericope (DN 2 Samannaphala gradual training) — placement of jhana before insight in the training sequence
Susima Sutta (SN 12.70) — the pannavimutta arahants who deny the formless attainments yet are liberated (the single most-cited dry-insight proof-text)
Anenjasappaya / Mahamalunkya (MN 64) — abandoning lower fetters via jhana-based contemplation (jhana-leaning fruit-attainment)

AN 3.86 (Paṭhama-sikkhā / 'first on the trainings') — the sekha grading: stream-enterer & once-returner = sīla-fulfiller, samādhi & paññā doer-to-a-measure; non-returner = samādhi-fulfiller; arahant = paññā-fulfiller
AN 3.87 (Dutiya-sikkhā) — companion grading with the same mattaso-kārī / paripūrakārī structure
AN 9.12 (Saupādisesa) — the nine persons with remainder; couples the gradient to fruits, anāgāmī as concentration-class
AN 4.85-90 / AN 4.123-126 — jhāna/brahmavihāra → anāgāmī rebirth (the contrastive control where jhāna IS the named cause of a fruit, the THIRD)
MN 70 (Kīṭāgiri) — the seven persons: ubhatobhāgavimutta & paññāvimutta & kāyasakkhī (jhāna-typed) vs diṭṭhippatta, saddhāvimutta, dhammānusārī, saddhānusārī (faith/wisdom-typed, no jhāna predicate)
SN 48.12-18 (Indriya-saṃyutta, Saṃkhitta/Vitthāra) — the five-faculty gradient: faculties fulfilled=arahant ... weak=stream-enterer ... weaker=Dhamma/faith-follower; the partial-samādhi-faculty floor
SN 25 (Okkantika-saṃyutta) — saddhānusārī enters by faith, dhammānusārī by wisdom-reflection on impermanence of the senses; the entry-to-the-path persons, no jhāna
SN 55.1-x (Sotāpatti-saṃyutta) generally — the four aveccappasāda factors, the four streams-of-merit
SN 55.5 — the four factors-FOR stream-entry: sappurisasaṃseva, saddhammassavana, yonisomanasikāra, dhammānudhammapaṭipatti (causal list, no jhāna)
SN 55.8-10 (Giñjakāvasatha / dhammādāsa) — the 'mirror of the Dhamma', self-declaration of stream-entry by the four factors
SN 55.3 (Dīghāvu) — dying lay disciple: four pasāda + six factors (add aniccasaññā etc.), guided upward with no jhāna
SN 55.21-22 (Mahānāma) — the frightened dying disciple assured of non-decline on faith/virtue/learning/generosity/wisdom; the deathbed control isolating the first fruit from jhāna
MN 117 (Mahācattārīsaka) — ariya sammāsamādhi defined functionally as cittekaggatā equipped with the seven path-supports (the sub-absorption reading of right-concentration)
DN 22 / SN 45.8 — the standard sammāsamādhi = four jhānas definition (the pro-jhāna structural premise to confront)
AN 11.1-2 / AN 10.1-2 (the 'no need for an act of will' / natural-concentration chain) — sīla→avippaṭisāra→pāmojja→pīti→passaddhi→sukha→samādhi (concentration as the FRUIT of virtue, downstream)
Puggalapaññatti (Abhidhamma) — the persons-classification including the mattaso-kārī grading and the seven/nine ariya persons; bridges canon to commentarial systematization
Dhammasaṅgaṇi / Atthasālinī + Visuddhimagga (commentary) — locus of upacāra-samādhi, khaṇika-samādhi, sukkhavipassaka (the post-canonical 'measure'-naming to date against the canon's silence)
Visuddhimagga XVIII-XXII (the vipassanā-ñāṇa sequence and sukkhavipassaka discussion) — where the dry-insight practitioner is named
MN 64 (Mahāmālukya) — uses jhāna as a vehicle for cutting the five lower fetters (anāgāmī-adjacent); test whether it conditions the LOWER fetters/first fruit on jhāna or the higher cut

AN 6.10 / AN 11.11-12 (Mahānāma, the six recollections) — the stream-enterer's practice given as anussati (recollection of Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha/sīla/cāga/devatā), an access-level, non-jhānic samādhi practice for a confirmed sotāpanna
MN 70 Kīṭāgiri Sutta (the seven ariya-puggala defined by with/without 'santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā'; 'work-to-be-done vs done'; paññāya nijjhānaṃ khamanti for dhammānusārī)

AN 9.12 Saupādisesa (nine grades who escape lower destinies despite being mattaso-kārī; the five antarā-/upahacca-/asaṅkhāra-/sasaṅkhāra-/uddhaṃsota non-returners crosswalked with ekabījī/kolaṅkola/sattakkhattuparama)
AN 3.86 / AN 3.87 / AN 3.88 (sikkhā-suttas: 'sīlesu paripūrakārī, samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī, paññāya mattaso kārī' = stream-enterer & once-returner; samādhi-fulfilment first at the non-returner)

Puggalapaññatti (Abhidhamma piṭaka — independent restatement of the seven/nine with the kāyasakkhī 'aṭṭha vimokkhe kāyena phusitvā' and paññāvimutta 'na kāyena phusitvā' clauses; samasīsī; non-returner fivefold)
SN 25 Okkantasaṃyutta (saddhānusārī = saddhā/pema-led; dhammānusārī = teachings 'paññāya mattaso nijjhānaṃ khamanti'; the verge-of-stream-entry definitions with no samādhi clause)
SN 48 Indriyasaṃyutta (the five faculties; indriya-vemattatā sorting the ariyas; faculties as the variance-theory)
MN 64 Mahāmālukya Sutta (the five lower fetters cut 'jhānaṃ nissāya', first jhāna sufficient as platform)
AN 9.36 Jhānasutta (taint-destruction in dependence on each jhāna up through nothingness; 'yāvatā saññāsamāpatti tāvatā aññāpaṭivedho' — top two attainments excluded)
AN 4.94 / AN 4.170 (cetosamatha vs adhipaññā-dhammavipassanā deficiency types; the four samatha-vipassanā yoke-orders incl. vipassanā-pubbaṅgama and dhamma-uddhacca)
SN 45.8 / DN 22 / MN 141 (sammā-samādhi DEFINED as the four jhānas — the magga-factor anchor)
MN 117 Mahācattārīsaka (ariyo sammā-samādhi sa-upaniso saparikkhāro — samādhi as cittekaggatā girded by the seven factors)

DN 2 Sāmaññaphala / the anupubbikathā conversion pericope (pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya → kallacitta muducitta vinīvaraṇacitta → dhammacakkhu; the hindrance-free composure as practice-floor for stream-entry)
AN 6.62 / Pp non-returner subdivisions; AN 10.64 + SN 55 (avecca-pasāda) for sotāpanna sub-grades ekabījī/kolaṅkola/sattakkhattuparama
Visuddhimagga / Aṭṭhakathā (upacāra-samādhi, sukkhavipassaka, parikamma/uggaha/paṭibhāga-nimitta — the commentarial gap-fillers to test as canon-zero)

AN 3.86 (Sekha / first Sikkhā sutta) — 'sīlesu paripūrakārī samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī' three-grade gradient; FETCH + read the Manorathapūraṇī commentary's gloss of mattaso kārī (highest-value commentarial datum)
AN 3.87 (second Sikkhā / Dutiyasikkhā) — companion gradient mapping fruits to partial vs full samādhi (sattakkhattuparama / kolaṅkola / ekabījī / sakadāgāmī / antarāparinibbāyī etc.)
AN 9.12 Saupādisesa — the nine-grade map of trainees who die before fulfilling all three trainings yet are non-returners/lower; ties mattaso kārī to specific fruit-grades
Puggalapaññatti (Abhidhamma) — formal definitions of the seven/nine noble persons and the paripūra/mattaso grades; the Abhidhamma layer's own gradation
DN 22 Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna & SN 45.8 Vibhaṅga — sammāsamādhi DEFINED as the four jhānas verbatim (the definitional backbone)
MN 141 Saccavibhaṅga — sammāsamādhi = four jhānas (corroborating definition)
AN 9.36 Jhānasutta — 'paṭhamampi jhānaṃ nissāya āsavānaṃ khayaṃ'; insight performed on jhāna-factors up to ākiñcaññāyatana
MN 52 Aṭṭhakanāgara — parallel jhāna-as-insight-base scheme; non-return as fallback to arahantship
MN 64 Mahāmālukya — abandoning the five LOWER fetters via jhāna bases; the 'na taṃ ṭhānaṃ vijjati' modal-necessity clause (strongest jhāna-requirement grammar)

SN 12.70 Susīma — paññāvimutta arahants deny the five abhiññā + the formless deliverances; 'pubbe dhammaṭṭhitiñāṇaṃ, pacchā nibbāne ñāṇaṃ'; FETCH the Sāratthappakāsinī commentary (does it call them sukkhavipassaka?)

MN 70 Kīṭāgiri — seven-persons typology; kāyasakkhī/ubhatobhāgavimutta defined BY touching the eight deliverances, diṭṭhippatta/saddhāvimutta/dhammānusārī/saddhānusārī defined WITHOUT; FETCH Papañcasūdanī gloss (does it admit a sukkhavipassaka diṭṭhippatta?)
AN 4.170 Yuganaddha — the four routes to arahantship (samatha-first, vipassanā-first, yoked, dhamma-uddhacca); every route still terminates in samādhiyati
SN 12.23 Upanisā — the pāmojja→pīti→passaddhi→sukha→samādhi→yathābhūtañāṇadassana causal cascade (samādhi as effect, not prerequisite)
AN 11.1-5 Kimatthiya chain — sīla→avippaṭisāra→...→vimuttiñāṇadassana virtue-cascade variant
Dhammapada 75 (Appamādavagga? actually Bālavagga) — 'aññā hi lābhūpanisā, aññā nibbānagāminī' two-paths verse
AN 11.16 = MN 52 parallel (Aṭṭhakanāgara recurs in AN) — corroborating jhāna-insight base list
MN 108 Gopaka-Moggallāna — the praised vs not-praised jhāna distinction (hindrance-ridden brooding called jhāyati pejoratively); the disambiguation locus
Vinaya Mahāvagga (Dhammacakkappavattana + the conversion narratives) — virajaṃ vītamalaṃ dhammacakkhuṃ udapādi; stream-entry on hearing, preceded by kallacitta/mudu-citta/vinīvaraṇacitta
Visuddhimagga (commentary layer) — upacāra-samādhi / appanā-samādhi / khaṇika-samādhi terminology + sukkhavipassaka + lokuttara-jhāna; the gap-filling machinery's home
Abhidhammatthasaṅgaha / Aṭṭhasālinī — lokuttara-jhāna and maggacitta as jhāna-factored path-moment (the reconciliation device); samādhindriya defined as ekaggatā in Abhidhamma-bhājanīya
MN 36/26 (Ariyapariyesanā / Mahāsaccaka) — Āḷāra Kālāma & Uddaka Rāmaputta: formless mastered yet 'na nibbidāya... saṃvattati'; concentration insufficient
AN 3.86 (Sikkha 1 / Sekha) — the mattaso-kari vs paripuri-kari grading; stream-enterer & once-returner are 'doers to a measure' in samadhi
AN 3.87 (Sikkha 2) — companion grading; the seven persons keyed to fulfilment of the three trainings
AN 9.12 (Saupadisesa) — nine persons with-residue; the measure-doer attains the lower fruits, fulfiller-in-samadhi = non-returner
Puggalapannatti (esp. the silesu/samadhismim/pannaya paripurakari sections; fetter-definitions of the noble persons)
SN 12.70 (Susima) — pannavimutta arahants deny iddhi, formless/santa-vimokkha, kaya-sakkhi; 'pubbe dhammatthitiñanam atha nibbane ñanam'
SN 12.68 (Kosambi / Musila-Narada-Savittha) — Narada sees nibbana by wisdom but 'na ca kayena phusitva', not yet arahant; well-water simile
AN 9.44 (Pannavimutta) + the parallel AN 9.43 (Kayasakkhi) / 9.45 (Ubhatobhagavimutta) — defining the liberation-types by presence/absence of the formless samapatti
MN 70 (Kitagiri) — the seven persons (ubhatobhagavimutta, pannavimutta, kayasakkhi, ditthippatta, saddhavimutta, dhammanusari, saddhanusari); the 'na ca ... atthi imesam dhammanam samapatti' clauses
AN 4.87-90 (the four samana: macala/pundarika/paduma/sukhumala) — non-returner without the eight liberations vs with
SN 48.12-18 (Indriyasamyutta) — the seven noble persons derived from the degree (vemattata) of completion of the five faculties; samadhindriya as gradable
SN 25 (Okkantasamyutta) — saddhanusari & dhammanusari enter sammattaniyama on faith/acceptance alone; guaranteed stream-entry, no samadhi clause
SN 55.24-25 (Saranani the Sakyan) — drinker declared stream-enterer/heaven-bound on 'saddhamatta pemamatta'; the hard low-concentration case
AN 4.170 (Yuganaddha, Ananda) — four routes to arahantship incl. vipassana-pubbangama and the dhammuddhacca-route
AN 6.46 (Mahacunda) — jhayi vs dhammayogi dispute; both validated; dhammayogi penetrate the deep by wisdom
AN 5.26 (Vimuttayatana) — five liberation-occasions; 'sukhino cittam samadhiyati', concentration as late term after hearing
SN 12.23 (Upanisa) — supramundane chain: samadhi → yathabhutañanadassana → nibbida...; samadhi derived from sukha/passaddhi
DN 16 / DN 18 (Mahaparinibbana / Janavasabha) — Dhammadasa 'mirror of dhamma' (4 aveccappasada + ariyakanta sila, no jhana) + mass lay fruit-declarations at Nadika
Dhammapada 372 — 'natthi jhanam apannassa natthi panna ajhayato' (the strongest pro-mutual-dependence verse, for the counter-case)
DN 2 (Samaññaphala) / MN 27 — gradual-training pericope: four jhanas → directed insight → asavakkhaya (always terminates in arahantship, never indexed to lower fruits)
DN 33 / DN 34 (Sangiti / Dasuttara) — concentration lists incl. paritta/mahaggata/appamana samadhi; animitta/appanihita/suññata; the three samadhi (savitakka-savicara etc.)
SN 22.5 / SN 56.1 — 'samahito yathabhutam pajanati' (the concentration→insight causal formula using generic 'samahito' not 'jhana')

Visuddhimagga (Ch. III–IV on parikamma/upacara/appana nimitta; Ch. XVIII+ on the dry-insight worker) and Abhidhammatthasangaha — the commentarial floor-filling: upacara-samadhi, sukkhavipassaka, lokuttara/magga-jhana
AN 3.86 (Pathama-sikkha / first on the trainee — samadhismim mattaso kari, the partial-doer gradient; stream-enterer & once-returner partial in samadhi, non-returner fulfils)
AN 3.87 (Dutiya-sikkha — second on the trainee, the parallel gradient)
AN 9.12 Saupadisesa (the nine persons with-residue; the mattaso/paripuri samadhi grading across sotapanna→anagami)
Puggalapannatti (the person-typology; mattaso-kari / paripuri-kari formal definitions)
SN 12.70 Susima (pannavimutta arahants who do NOT touch the eight liberations with the body — the dry-insight crux; dhammatthiti-nana precedes nibbana-nana)
MN 70 Kitagiri (the sevenfold ariya typology ubhatobhagavimutta→saddhanusari; dhammanusari/saddhanusari enter the path with undeveloped faculties, no eight-liberations)
Ud 1.10 Bahiya (ditthe ditthamattam; on-the-spot awakening with no jhana narrative)
SN 12.23 Upanisa (the transcendental dependent-origination: ...sukha → samadhi → yathabhuta-nanadassana; samadhi as natural proximate cause of insight, not jhana-gated)
AN 11.2 / AN 10.2 Cetana-karaniya (the spontaneous-causation chain pamojja→piti→passaddhi→sukha→samadhi; 'no need to will' the steps)
AN 4.41 Samadhibhavana (four developments of concentration by function: pleasant-abiding / knowledge-vision / sati-sampajanna / taint-destruction)
AN 4.170 Yuganaddha (Ananda; four ways to arahantship: samatha-first, vipassana-first, yoked, and the dhammuddhacca case — insight-led paths canonically named)
AN 4.161-169 (the catasso patipada — four practice-modes by ease×speed; dukkhapatipada still reaches the goal)
SN 41.7 Godatta & MN 43 Mahavedalla (animitta / appanihita / sunnata cetovimutti-cetosamadhi defined and distinguished — the theme-defined concentrations)
SN 40.x Moggallana-samyutta (the formless and signless attainments; animitta cetosamadhi)
AN 3.100/3.103 Nimitta (the goldsmith simile — balance the samadhi-nimitta, paggaha-nimitta, upekkha-nimitta; canonical functional nimitta vs commentarial visual ladder)
The gradual-training pericope (DN 2 Samannaphala & parallels — 'nivaranehi cittam parisodheti' as the discrete pre-first-jhana hindrance-purification step)

The dhammacakkhu / anupubbikatha stock narrative (kallacitta muducitta vinivaranacitta udaggacitta pasannacitta → virajam vitamalam dhammacakkhum; the listening-threshold composure — appears across DN/MN/Vinaya conversion accounts)
Dhammasangani (Abhidhamma — ekaggata as sabbacittasadharana universal cetasika; the canonical-Abhidhammic seed of momentary concentration)
Patisambhidamagga, Samadhi-katha & Yuganaddha-vagga (the late-canonical samadhi systematization; first plausible canonical home for proto-upacara/appana analysis)
Nettippakarana & Petakopadesa (para-canonical hermeneutic manuals — candidate bridge texts for commentarial samadhi-grading)

Visuddhimagga, Samadhi-niddesa (Buddhaghosa's explicit parikamma/upacara/appana ladder + the parikamma/uggaha/patibhaga-nimitta scheme — the locus classicus of access concentration; the 0-canon term's actual home)
Vimuttimagga-tradition material where present in commentary (the alternative samadhi-schematization)
AN 6.46 Mahacunda (the dhamma-yogi vs jhayi tension — those-devoted-to-dhamma vs those-devoted-to-jhana should respect each other; canonical acknowledgment of two practitioner styles)

MN 117 Mahacattarisaka — ariya sammasamadhi sa-upaniso sa-parikkharo = cittass'ekaggata equipped by the seven supports; also the lokiya/lokuttara sammasamadhi split and micchasamadhi contrast (cst-mn / SuttaCentral mn117)
MN 44 Culavedalla (Dhammadinna) — niddesa: 'yo cittassa ekaggata ayam samadhi'; samadhi-nimitta = 4 satipatthana, samadhi-parikkhara = 4 sammappadhana (mn44)
DN 22 Mahasatipatthana — sammasamadhi DEFINED by the four-jhana stock formula (the maximalist anchor) (dn22)
MN 141 Saccavibhanga — same four-jhana definition of sammasamadhi (mn141)
SN 45.8 Magga-vibhanga — same four-jhana definition of sammasamadhi (sn45.8)

AN 3.86 / AN 3.87 (Sekha/Sikkha suttas) — 'silesu paripurakari, samadhismim mattaso kari' gradient: stream-enterer & once-returner = doers-to-a-measure in concentration; non-returner = fulfiller (an3.86, an3.87)
AN 9.12 Saupadisesa — the nine persons with residue, the same mattaso-kari gradient applied to the lower noble persons (an9.12)
Puggalapannatti — the person-typology parallels (mattaso kari; the seven ariyapuggala; kayasakkhi/ditthippatta/saddhavimutta/pannavimutta) (abhidhamma puggalapannatti)
SN 12.70 Susima — pannavimutta arahants WITHOUT the eight liberations 'touched with the body'; dhammatthitinana before nibbananana; the Sarattha-ppakasini commentary's sukkhavipassaka gloss (sn12.70)
MN 70 Kitagiri — the seven persons; kayasakkhi defined by jhana-touch vs ditthippatta/saddhavimutta defined by wisdom-seeing WITHOUT jhana-touch; saddhanusari/dhammanusari as stream-path (mn70)
AN 4.41 Samadhibhavana — four developments of concentration by function; the asavakkhaya one = contemplating rise-and-fall of the aggregates (an4.41)
SN 12.23 Upanisa — transcendental DO: sukha -> samadhi -> yathabhutananadassana -> nibbida -> viraga -> vimutti (one samadhi-node before insight) (sn12.23)

MN 70 Kitagiri Sutta — seven persons (ubhatobhagavimutta, pannavimutta, kayasakkhi, ditthippatta, saddhavimutta, dhammanusari, saddhanusari); pannavimutta liberated without the peaceful formless liberations
SN 12.70 Susima Sutta — pannavimutta arahants who deny the formless attainments; 'pubbe dhammatthitiñanam paccha nibbane ñanam' (the canon's nearest dry-insight hint)

AN 9.36 Jhana Sutta — 'jhanam nissaya asavanam khayo'; contemplating jhanic experience as anicca/dukkha/anatta; routes the not-fully-freed to non-return / Pure Abodes (jhana-platform → non-return, not lower fruits)
SN 55.x (Sotapattisamyutta) + the four sotapattiyanga (sappurisasamseva, saddhammassavana, yonisomanasikara, dhammanudhammapatipatti) — stream-entry causation WITHOUT jhana
MN 117 Mahacattarisaka — sammasamadhi as cittekaggata supported by the seven path factors (sa-upanisa saparikkhara); the relational, non-fixed-altitude definition of right concentration
SN 12.23 Upanisa Sutta + AN 11.1-5 (transcendental dependent origination) — samadhi (unspecified) as the link before yathabhutañanadassana; affect-cascade pamojja→piti→passaddhi→sukha→samadhi
DN 22 Mahasatipatthana / SN 45.8 Vibhanga — the standard sammasamadhi = four jhanas definition (the descriptive baseline)

AN 4.94 / AN 4.170 (samatha-vipassana yoking, Yuganaddha) — the four ways to arahantship including vipassana-first and the 'dhammuddhacca' case; bears on whether tranquillity must precede or merely pair with insight

MN 36 Mahasaccaka — Bodhisatta's rejection of Alara/Uddaka's attainments as 'not enough' (jhana-praised-yet-insufficient); also the recollection of childhood first-jhana as the way (reframing jhana's role)
Visuddhimagga (Vism III-XI samadhi-niddesa) — the commentarial parikamma→upacara→appana ladder and padakajjhana; the 0-canon floor-fill
Patisambhidamagga (samadhi / vimokkha sections) — supramundane path-moment concentration; samathayanika vs suddhavipassanayanika seeds

SN 12.23 Upanisā Sutta — the natural-causation chain dukkha→saddhā→pāmojja→pīti→passaddhi→sukha→samādhi→yathābhūta-ñāṇadassana→nibbidā→virāga→vimutti; the canonical proof-text that samādhi is caused (sukhino cittaṃ samādhiyati) and sits as upanisā of knowledge-and-vision

AN 10.1 Kimatthiya (and AN 10.2 Cetanākaraṇīya) — 'wholesome virtues have non-remorse as purpose/benefit', then avippaṭisāra→pāmojja→pīti→passaddhi→sukha→samādhi→yathābhūta→nibbidā-virāga→vimuttiñāṇadassana; the virtue-root of caused-samādhi, explicitly 'one need not will it (na cetanāya karaṇīyaṃ)'

AN 11.1 / AN 11.2 — the same kim-atthiya ladder in elevens; the 'na cetanāya karaṇīyaṃ' (no act of will needed) sutta is the single most direct statement that these fruits, including samādhi, arise causally not by intention — the textual heart of Lens 17

AN 3.86 (Sikkhā / Paṭhama-sikkhattaya) and AN 3.87 — silesu paripūrakārī / samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī / paññāya mattaso kārī gradient across the noble persons; the stream-enterer & once-returner as doers-to-a-measure in concentration
AN 9.12 Saupādisesa — the nine persons with residue, distributing the paripūra/mattaso gradient across the trainee fruits
Puggalapaññatti (Abhidhamma) — the person-typology restating the mattaso-kārī / paripūrakārī grading; a non-canon-vs-canon layer check on the gradient
AN 6.10 Mahānāma — the six recollections each ending 'sukhino cittaṃ samādhiyati', applied to a noble (lower-fruit) disciple; caused-samādhi attached to a named sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī
MN 117 Mahācattārīsaka — ariya sammā-samādhi defined as cittassa ekaggatā 'equipped with' the seven path factors (sa-upaniso saparikkhāro); concentration defined relationally/causally

DN 2 Sāmaññaphala (and MN 27 Cūḷahatthipadopama, MN 39 Mahā-Assapura) — the gradual-training pericope where pāmojja→pīti→passaddhi→sukha→samādhi arises from virtue + sense-restraint + hindrance-abandoning, BEFORE the four-jhāna formula
AN 4.170 (Ānanda, Yuganaddha) — the four modes: samatha-first, vipassanā-first, yoked, and dhamma-excitement; canonical license for insight-leading routes
AN 6.46 Mahācunda (Dhammayoga) — the canon refereeing jhāyī vs dhammayoga, refusing to privilege jhāna as the sole path

SN 48 Indriya-saṃyutta (samādhindriya definition) — 'vossaggārammaṇaṃ karitvā labhati samādhiṃ' (making release the object, one GAINS concentration) vs the four-jhānas definition; the receptive-verb framing of the concentration-faculty

The four sotāpattiyaṅga (SN 55 Sotāpatti-saṃyutta — aveccappasāda in Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha + ariyakanta-sīla) and the four factors-for-stream-entry (sappurisa-saṃseva, saddhamma-savana, yoniso-manasikāra, dhammānudhamma-paṭipatti) — the constitutive lists, to show no jhāna is named as constitutive of the lowest fruit

Visuddhimagga + Aṭṭhakathā on samādhi (the parikamma/upacāra/appanā tripartition) and the Aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā gloss on AN 3.86 mattaso-kārī — the commentarial layer that supplies upacāra-samādhi as the 'measure'; the locus where the canon-vs-commentary signature on the answer is read
AN 3.86 (Paṭhama-sikkhā / Sekha) — silesu paripurakari, samadhismim mattaso kari: the gradient locus
AN 3.87 (Dutiya-sikkhā) — the parallel grading with the sub-types (ekabiji, kolankola, sattakkhattuparama)
AN 3.88 (Tatiya-sikkhā) — the three-trainings completion mapping
AN 9.12 Saupadisesa — nine persons with residue graded by which trainings are fulfilled; the explicit 'doer to a measure' assignment of the lower fruits
Puggalapaññatti (the persons-manual) — the formal definitions of sotapanna/sakadagami/anagami and the seven ariya-puggala incl. saddhanusari/dhammanusari
SN 56.11 Dhammacakkappavattana — Kondanna's dhamma-eye ('yam kinci samudayadhammam...') at the first sermon, the paradigm sudden lower-fruit attainment

MN 70 Kitagiri — the seven persons, the explicit statement that only kayasakkhi/ubhatobhagavimutta touch the formless with the body, and the 'gradual not sudden' faith-ladder to realizing the highest truth with the body
MN 56 Upali / Vin Mahavagga (Yasa, Yasa's father) — anupubbikatha → kallacitta → dhammacakkhu, the graduated-talk-to-stream-entry recipe in a domestic setting
Udana 1.10 Bahiya — 'in the seen just the seen', instantaneous arahantship standing in the street, no jhana frame (downward bound on the floor)
MN 64 Malunkyaputta (Maha) — contemplating the aggregates FROM within jhana to attain non-return: the locus that DOES install jhana, but for the non-returner step
MN 4 Bhayabherava / the awakening stem 'so evam samahite citte parisuddhe...abhininnamesi' — fourth-jhana-based asavakkhaya (jhana for arahantship/full awakening)
SN 12.23 Upanisa — the pamojja → piti → passaddhi → sukha → samadhi → yathabhutañanadassana transcendental sequence (natural affective concentration)
SN 55.x Sotapattisamyutta (the four sotapattiyanga + aveccappasada limbs; Mahanama's lay-stream-entry assurances; Dighavu deathbed) — the definitional and lay/deathbed cluster
AN 6.10 Mahanama (the six recollections as the lay-follower's practice while living the household life)
DN 18 Janavasabha + DN 16 Mahaparinibbana (Natika tally) — the census of named lay dead graded into the four fruits
Dhammapada 372 — natthi jhanam apannassa, natthi pañña ajhayato (the mutual-conditioning verse, pro-jhana side)
MN 27 Culahatthipadopama / MN 51 / MN 107 Ganakamoggallana — the gradual-training sequences placing jhana relative to the breakthrough
Theragatha/Therigatha + Apadana frames for Patacara, Kisagotami, Sopaka, Sankicca, the seven-year-old arahants (speed/age/grief-trigger cases)
Khujjuttara (Itivuttaka frame) — the slave-woman stream-enterer (social-constraint bound)
Udana / commentary on Mandukadevaputta (the frog reborn a stream-entering deva — non-human low-floor reductio)
Vinaya Parajika 4 (uttarimanussadhamma) — the enumerated menu of falsely-claimable attainments separating jhana/samadhi/samapatti from magga/phala
Visuddhimagga (esp. the concentration chapters and the Pañña section's gotrabhu/anuloma run; the upacara/appana/khanika apparatus and the sukkhavipassaka category) — the commentarial gap-filler
Patisambhidamagga (canonical-but-late, for any vipassana-samadhi vocabulary bridging canon to commentary)
inference_subjects: The non-returner's fetter-set (sensual desire + ill-will) and its overlap with what jhana suppresses — fixes the concentration-fulfilment ceiling at the 3rd fruit
Alara Kalama / Uddaka Ramaputta (formless masters without liberation) — decouples concentration-depth from the fruit
Susima's pannavimutta arahants who lack the formless attainments — the empirical wedge for sub-jhanic liberation
The dhammacakkhu-on-the-spot conversion narratives (Yasa, Upali, the 1000 fire-ascetics, Bahiya) — concentration-light practice portrait of the lower fruits
Bahiya Sutta (the 'in the seen only the seen' instant arahantship) — extreme case of awakening with no described concentration practice at all
The five-faculty balancing and sharp/dull-faculty persons — builds inter-individual variation into the required samadhi, dissolving a single floor
Sona's lute (effort/concentration tuning) — implies a correct, non-maximal concentration level
The hindrance-suppression durability question (vikkhambhana vs momentary clearing) — reframes the floor as a hindrance-state
Abhidhamma ekaggata as a universal cetasika — makes 'some samadhi' definitionally omnipresent, including in the path-moment
The seven purifications' citta-visuddhi stage — the commentary's own floor, set at access not absorption
Lokuttara (supramundane) path-moment concentration — momentary by nature, relocating the 'necessary' samadhi off the jhana ladder
The gladdening causal sequence (pamojja→piti→passaddhi→sukha→samadhi) — samadhi as natural outflow of virtue/joy, not separately engineered
Anuruddha's eleven defilements-of-concentration (upakkilesa) — adepts wobble, so the lower fruits can't demand high stability

Translator/exegetical debate metadata (Cousins on access concentration's lateness; Gombrich/Wynne on jhana-origins; Shankman/Brahmali on the jhana definition) — orthogonal scholarly layer that flags WHICH loci each camp leans on
The seven/nine types of noble persons (satta/nava ariyapuggala) — the partition that assigns jhāna to some ariya-types and not others
The five spiritual faculties (pañcindriya) and their grading of beings by maturity — partial-samādhi-faculty as the stream-enterer's floor
The paññāvimutta ('liberated-by-wisdom') arahant lacking the formless attainments — proof that even full liberation has a non-formless-jhāna track (a fortiori the lower fruits)
The kāyasakkhī / ubhatobhāgavimutta 'touching the eight liberations with the body' — the contrastive jhāna-typed persons
Lay (gihī / kāmabhogī) stream-enterers and once-returners — bounding the concentration-floor from above by who may hold the fruit while living sensually
Deathbed / sick-room consolation suttas — natural experiment holding jhāna at ~zero while affirming the fruit
The natural-concentration causal chain (sīla→pāmojja→samādhi) — concentration as downstream fruit of virtue, not upstream prerequisite
The six recollections (cha anussati) as the canonical lay/stream-enterer meditation — an explicitly access-level (non-absorption) samādhi practice
Commentarial sukkhavipassaka / khaṇikasamādhi / upacārasamādhi — the tradition's own admission, by coining the term, that the canonical floor is sub-jhānic
Right-concentration's two definitions (four-jhānas in DN22/SN45.8 vs functional cittekaggatā-with-supports in MN117) — the equivocation at the heart of the pro-jhāna syllogism
The three trainings (sikkhattaya) graded across the fruits — stream-entry as sīla-complete, citta-incomplete
Gotrabhū / the change-of-lineage and the moment-of-path (maggakkhaṇa) — Abhidhamma/commentarial momentariness, where 'concentration at the path-moment' is defined and quantified post-canonically
The dhammacakkhu-arising at the end of discourses — stream-entry as a hearing-and-penetrating event rather than an absorption event
Brahmavihāra/mettā practice leading to anāgāmī — jhāna-adjacent practice tied (canonically) to the THIRD fruit, not the first

Abhidhamma path-consciousness (sotāpattimagga-citta) always accompanied by jhāna-factors (vitakka-vicāra-pīti-sukha-ekaggatā) — the Abhidhamma claim that EVERY path-moment is technically jhānic, the systematized pro-jhāna position to weigh against the Sutta silence
The exact width of the with-vs-without-attainments split: which grades possess 'santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā' and which the canon explicitly certifies as lacking them

The mattaso-kārī gradient (sīla full / samādhi & paññā 'to a measure' for the lower fruits; samādhi-fulfilment deferred to the non-returner) as the canon's only quantitative statement of the concentration floor
Faculty-dominance (paññā/saddhā/samādhi-indriya) as the canon's actual variance theory for the ariyas — and the conspicuous absence of a samādhi-dominance grade gating the lower fruits
The sotāpanna sub-grading (ekabījī/kolaṅkola/sattakkhattuparama) and once-returner attenuation — graded by residual rebirths/fetters, never by jhāna-depth

The five non-returner sub-types (antarā-/upahacca-/asaṅkhāra-/sasaṅkhāra-parinibbāyī, uddhaṃsota-akaniṭṭhagāmī) — graded by momentum-to-final-nibbāna, with jhāna correlated only to the pure-abode rebirth-destination, not to the lower-fruit floor
Nīvaraṇa-suppression / kallacitta receptivity as the recurrent practice-floor in conversion narratives — the canonical phenomenon the commentary later renames upacāra-samādhi
First-jhāna-as-platform for cutting the lower fetters (MN 64 / AN 9.36) and the exclusion of the two peak attainments from the breakthrough

The canonical-zero of upacāra/khaṇika-samādhi and sukkhavipassaka, and the chronological stratum (canon vs Abhidhamma Pp vs Aṭṭhakathā) at which the sub-jhānic floor and the dry-insight category are first reified
The samatha-vipassanā yoke-orders (esp. vipassanā-pubbaṅgama) as the canonical seed of the dry/bare-insight reading, and the deficiency-typologies that treat samatha as a separable, addable component

The definitional content of ubhatobhāgavimutta (all eight liberations / cessation vs a single peaceful liberation) fixing the TOP of the map against which every lower grade's stated attainment is measured

Whether sammā-samādhi-as-path-factor is read as four-jhāna absorption everywhere it is DEFINED vs cittekaggatā-girded-by-the-path (MN 117), and how 'present in measure' reconciles the path-requirement with the mattaso grading
The nine anupubbavihāra (4 jhāna + 4 formless + saññāvedayitanirodha) as the 0-9 ruler onto which each ariya-puggala's certified attainments can be plotted to literally draw the concentration map

Stratification of upacāra-samādhi / khaṇika-samādhi / appanā-samādhi by corpus layer (predicted: 0 in mūla nikāya+Vinaya, positive only in aṭṭhakathā+ṭīkā+Visuddhimagga) — the canonical-absence-as-finding
Stratification of sukkhavipassaka / suddhavipassanāyānika / samathayānika / vipassanāyānika by layer — does the canon ever NAME the dry practitioner?
Stratification of lokuttara-jhāna / maggacitta-as-jhāna by layer — when did the path-moment become jhānic?
The 'mattaso kārī' gloss in Manorathapūraṇī — does the commentary quantify the unquantified canonical measure as sub-jhānic or as 'jhāna-but-not-in-full'?
Differential definitions of the seven noble persons (which carry the eight-deliverances clause, which don't) — the canon's own samādhi-vs-fruit map
The semantic range of jhāyati (technical absorption vs broad 'meditate' vs pejorative 'brood' vs homograph 'burn') — to decontaminate any raw jhāna count

Asymmetry test: existence of a 'concentration-without-wisdom is fruitless' pericope vs ABSENCE of a symmetric 'wisdom-without-concentration is fruitless' pericope — indirect weighting of the two factors
The vinīvaraṇacitta / pañca-nīvaraṇe pahāya threshold — whether hindrance-freedom is a recognized state distinct from and below full jhāna (the phenomenological content of upacāra without the name)
Whether sammāsamādhi is EVER defined non-jhānically (as bare ekaggatā) anywhere in the canon, esp. Abhidhamma-bhājanīya vs Suttanta-bhājanīya split in the Vibhaṅga

The fruit-by-fruit concentration map: which fruits (sotāpatti, sakadāgāmi, anāgāmi, arahatta) the canon ties to which samādhi grade — testing the prompt's claim that full samādhi attaches specifically to non-return
The causal positioning of samādhi (downstream of pāmojja/passaddhi, upstream of yathābhūtañāṇa) in the upanisā/bojjhaṅga cascades — samādhi as emergent fruit vs engineered prerequisite
Cases of high samādhi without liberation (Āḷāra, Uddaka, Devadatta) establishing insufficiency, to set up the necessity-asymmetry argument
The modal-necessity vocabulary (ṭhānaṃ vijjati / na ṭhānaṃ vijjati / anāgantuṃ etaṃ ṭhānaṃ) wherever it attaches to a jhāna clause — the canon's rare explicit necessity statements
Akuppā cetovimutti vs paññāvimutti pairing — whether 'unshakeable liberation of mind' (the samādhi side) is required of ALL arahants or only the both-ways type
The five faculties (indriya) and their relative balance — samadhindriya weak vs pannindriya/saddhindriya strong as the structural basis for the lower fruits
The ten fetters and the once-returner's mere ATTENUATION (tanutta) of greed/hatred with sensual desire still present — mapping concentration-depth onto which fetter is removed at which fruit
The five hindrances and whether 'suppressed' (access) vs 'absent' (absorption) is the operative threshold for insight (panne dubbalikarane)
The natural-causation / upanisa cascade (saddha→pamojja→piti→passaddhi→sukha→samadhi→yathabhutañanadassana) — concentration as emergent middle term, not precondition
The four iddhipada, esp. vimamsa-samadhi — wisdom-generated concentration inverting the concentration-first order
Deathbed teachings (Gilana suttas) and the dying attaining non-return — the no-runway-for-jhana natural experiment
Child / very-young and animal/deva (frog-deva, Manduka) awakenings — the extreme low end of the capacity distribution
The 'snap of the fingers' (accharasanghata) suttas praising momentary wholesome states — the minimal-duration end
The dhammayogi vs jhayi sociological dispute (AN 6.46) — institutional existence of a wisdom-led practitioner type
The lokuttara/magga-jhana doctrine — how the commentary preserves samma-samadhi=jhana by relocating jhana to the path-moment for dry-insight workers
The three liberation-doors (animitta/appanihita/suññata samadhi) — concentration defined by aspect (impermanence/non-self/suffering) rather than absorptive depth
Paritta-samadhi ('limited concentration', DN 33) — direct canonical proof the canon knows non-absorptive concentration
The samatha/vipassana yoking typology (AN 4.170) and the dhammuddhacca route — insight running ahead of concentration
Mass posthumous lay fruit-declarations (Nadika, Janavasabha) — the demographic implausibility of universal-jhana stream-entry
The self-certification 'mirror of dhamma' checklist content — what the canon tells a disciple to look for (faith+virtue, not jhana)
The gradual-training pericope's invariable termination in arahantship (never sotapatti) — evidence it maps the maximal path, not the lower-fruit floor
Vinaya ordination/attainment narratives where listeners attain on-the-spot — distribution of dhammacakkhu events relative to any meditation context
The well-water simile (SN 12.68) — the canon's own image of seeing-without-touching = insight ahead of body-realization/absorption
Sotapatti as defined by fetter-removal (3 cognitive fetters: self-view, doubt, rule-clinging) — an insight/cognition metric with no concentration term
The commentary's coinage activity itself (upacara, khanika, sukkhavipassaka all 0-canon) — the gap-filling as evidence the canon underspecified the floor
The mattaso-kari / paripuri-kari gradient (the canon's only explicit concentration-measure, defined relationally not absolutely)
cittekaggata / ekaggata as a universal Abhidhamma cetasika (momentary concentration's metaphysical seed — present in every mind-moment)
The 'samahite citte yathabhutam pajanati' formula (concentration-as-proximate-cause-of-insight, depth-unspecified)
animitta / appanihita / sunnata cetosamadhi (the three theme-defined concentrations — samadhi classified by object not depth)
The four sotapattiyanga (stream-entry's stated factors: hearing+wise-attention, with jhana conspicuously ABSENT)
