Pannavimutta / sukkhavipassaka and the Susima problem (liberation bounded below the eight-liberations — the dry-insight ceiling)
MN 70's sevenfold ariya typology and the saddhanusari/dhammanusari (path-entrants with undeveloped attainments — the canon's minimum-concentration statement)
The five indriya/bala (samadhi as a continuous, tikkha/mudu-graded faculty — the canon's real 'how-much' apparatus)
AN 4.170 Yuganaddha & AN 4.41 samadhibhavana (insight-led paths and function-typed concentration — concentration can follow or accompany insight)
The catasso patipada (four practice-modes by ease×speed; dukkhapatipada reaches the goal with deficient samatha)
Bahiya / ugghatitannu / khippabhinna (fast, concentration-light realizations — the low-runway extreme)
Canonical nimitta-usage (functional three-signs balance) vs commentarial uggaha/patibhaga-nimitta ladder (where the absorption-acquisition machinery is post-canonical)
The dhamma-yogi vs jhayi distinction AN 6.46 (canonical recognition of insight-leaning vs absorption-leaning practitioners)
Activity/postural concentration & sampajanna-continuity (the sustained-composure phenomenology that 'momentary concentration' later names)
The Patisambhidamagga/Netti/Petaka stratum (the systematizing bridge where proto-commentarial samadhi-analysis would first surface if anywhere in the canon)
The Dhammasangani ekaggata-as-universal vs Sutta-pitaka silence split (the layer-by-layer accretion of the momentary-concentration concept)

The seven ariyapuggala typology (kayasakkhi vs ditthippatta vs saddhavimutta vs pannavimutta vs ubhatobhagavimutta) — whether the canon's noble-person grid is partitioned by jhana-possession, which tells us directly whether non-jhana noble attainment is canonically admitted

The four sotapattiyanga and stream-entry attainment narratives (dhammacakkhum udapadi mid-discourse) — whether concentration appears among the stated causes of stream-entry; lay listeners attaining while seated is narrative evidence of the floor

Micchasamadhi and non-Buddhist jhana attainers (Alara/Uddaka, heretics reborn in form realms) — proves deep concentration is neither sufficient nor unique to the path, so rightness is conferred by view/company not depth
The avikkhepa (non-distraction) and ekaggata (one-pointedness) Abhidhamma glosses + the universal-cetasika status of ekaggata — establishes the depth-floor-zero baseline of the bare factor

The samatha/vipassana yoking taxonomy (yuganaddha; the adhipanna-vipassana-without-cetosamatha person) — shows insight can run ahead of tranquillity, mapping onto the mattaso-kari gradient from the practitioner side

The transcendental dependent-origination chain (upanisa) and the bojjhanga maturation (piti-passaddhi-sukha-samadhi -> yathabhutananadassana) — defines the operative samadhi functionally as the collectedness that grounds seeing-as-it-is
The four iddhipada (chanda/viriya/citta/vimamsa samadhi) — concentration individuated by motive driver including an investigation-driven (insight-coupled) variety, orthogonal to depth
The threefold liberation-samadhi (sunnata/animitta/appanihita) and cetosamadhi / appamana-cetosamadhi — concentration individuated by liberative object/orientation rather than altitude

The commentarial concentration apparatus (parikamma/upacara/appana, patibhaga-nimitta, khanika-samadhi) — its stratigraphic confinement to commentary/tika documents where the canon's gap was filled and by what non-canonical construct

The lokiya/lokuttara sammasamadhi split and maggakkhana-ekaggata-as-lokuttara-jhana — the Abhidhamma reconciliation by which the jhana-formula is technically satisfied at the path-moment without prior mundane jhana mastery (the dry-insight worker's lokuttara first jhana)

The lute-string / water-clarity similes and the 'parisuddha-pariyodata-kammaniya-thita-anejjappatta' quality-refrain — the canon's qualitative (continuum/balance) vocabulary for the required state vs the discrete jhana-count vocabulary

The three sikkha (adhisila/adhicitta/adhipanna) framing of the gradient — adhicitta-sikkha is where 'samadhismim mattaso kari' sits; whether adhicitta is defined as jhana or as ekaggata in the training-formula contexts

Dhammapada 372 and the jhayati/jhana philology — whether 'jhana' in the pro-concentration verses means the four numbered absorptions or meditative cultivation in general (decides how much the maximalist verses prove)
The paññavimutta (wisdom-released) arahant and the Susima case — proof the highest fruit needs no formless attainments, lower-bounding the concentration requirement for everything below it
The seven-noble-persons typology (MN 70) — concentration-attainment plotted against fruit; the dhammanusari/saddhanusari (stream-entry-bound) defined WITHOUT a samadhi stipulation
The gradual-instruction 'ready/hindrance-free/malleable mind' (kallacitta...vinivaranacitta) — the canon's phenomenological floor that the commentary later names upacara
The four patipadas and tikkha/mudu faculties — same fruit reached at different concentration depths, the internal argument for sufficiency-band over fixed-depth
samannagata-with-the-eightfold-path vs paripuri-of-samadhi — the possess-vs-fulfill grammar; potential counter-evidence (stream-enterer 'endowed' with right concentration) and its reconciliation

Gift-analysis / bhojane mattaññuta / virtue-grading (Dakkhinavibhanga, moderation-in-eating) — orthogonal domains where the canon DOES quantify, calibrating the significance of samadhi's un-quantification; 'matta' as right-measure not counted-quantity
The MN 127 mahaggata-by-spatial-extent and MN 43 paritta/appamana ladder — the canon demonstrably CAN graduate concentration by measure, so its silence on the stream-entry measure is a choice
Abhidhamma universal-ekaggata + Vism cittavisuddhi (upacara/appana) — the scholastic layer that converts the canon's functional floor into named tiers and licenses access-concentration insight
Khanika-samadhi and the vipassana-moment (commentarial/Mahasi) — the lowest stratum of the concentration-floor stack; dating the dry-insight license
The heartwood similes (MN 29/30) — concentration as instrumental/subordinate, reframing 'how much' into 'enough for release to follow' (outcome-defined sufficiency)
yavadeva-ñanamattaya / nikamalabhi-akicchalabhi — sufficiency-idiom vs mastery-idiom; the lower fruit as 'enough to see', the non-returner/arahant as 'mastered/abiding' jhana

Comparative morphology asymmetry — whether samadhi/samahita takes -tara comparatives at all (dial) or only punctual enter-verbs (threshold), a grammatical tell about the canon's conception of concentration-as-quantity
The stratigraphic distribution of upacāra/appanā/parikamma/khaṇika-samādhi (canon=0, Abhidhamma-piṭaka=?, aṭṭhakathā=bloom) as a dating test for the access-concentration construct
The exact scope of the paññāvimutta definition — whether it denies only the arūpa/eight liberations or jhāna generally (decides if dry-insight means no-jhāna or merely no-formless)
The Susīma vs SĀ 347 asymmetry as a model case of Theravāda sharpening a jhāna-denial absent in the common stock
Sotāpattiyaṅga / stream-entry causation lists as jhāna-free recipes bounding the lower-fruit floor downward
The Vibhaṅga Abhidhamma-bhājanīya reduction of sammāsamādhi to cittassekaggatā as a pre-commentarial seed of path-moment (momentary) concentration
The five-faculties balancing model (saddhā/paññā-led temperaments, saddhānusārī/dhammānusārī) as the canon's variable-quantum alternative to a fixed concentration threshold
Hindrance-removal + the pāmojja→samādhi cascade as the unnamed functional substrate later reified as upacāra
The Kathāvatthu ekābhisamaya (one-moment penetration of the four truths) doctrine as compatible with momentary/access concentration
Sarvāstivāda anāgamya-samādhi and the kṣaṇika darśana-mārga as an independent Northern solution to the same under-determined measure (convergence argument)
The samāhito yathābhūtaṃ pajānāti formula as the canonical floor-from-above: zero concentration is excluded even where jhāna is not mandated
AN 6.46 dhamma-yogī/jhāyī mutual-respect passage as in-canon acknowledgement of two legitimate practitioner types
The placement of the FIRST samādhi-paripūrakārī at the non-returner level as the canon's own (only) quantification of the gradient, sitting above both lower fruits

The lexical stratification of upacāra-samādhi and khaṇika-samādhi: whether each term first appears in aṭṭhakathā vs. only in ṭīkā — dates when the sub-jhānic floor entered the tradition and tests Cousins/Gombrich's 'late' thesis directly
The functional theory of hindrance-suppression (vikkhambhana-pahāna vs. tadaṅga-pahāna) as a label-independent proxy for 'how much quieting insight requires'
The Abhidhamma universal-mental-factor doctrine (ekaggatā present in every citta) as a terminological route to a near-zero floor
The supramundane-jhāna / lokuttara-jhāna doctrine that recodes every path-moment as jhānic, dissolving the 'prerequisite vs. concomitant' ambiguity in the canon's 'measure'

The descriptive-vocabulary asymmetry: sotāpanna described by dassana/diṭṭhi-sampatti ('seeing') vs. higher attainers by samādhi/cetovimutti — an argument-from-silence that the lower fruits were conceived as insight-events with a deliberately light concentration-floor
The person-typology layer (sukkhavipassaka, paññāvimutta, saddhāvimutta, diṭṭhippatta, kāyasakkhi, ubhatobhāgavimutta) as the personnel-side encoding of who needs how much concentration
Fraction/portion similes (koṭṭhāsa, kalā, padesa) as the commentary's figurative quantification habit applied to vague canonical degrees
Pādaka-jhāna as the operational (not merely classificatory) test of whether the dry path is a real method or an empty category

The threefold-training architecture (adhicitta-sikkhā = jhāna?) as a structural constraint that can contradict the soteriological concession of dry attainment, exposing the internal seam the modern debate exploits
The samādhi-indriya/bala maturity-grading of the noble persons as the systematic home of the canon's relational (less-than-the-higher) answer that explains why no absolute quantum was ever fixed
Five hindrances vs graded access
Formless demoted MN26
MN70 samadhi vs wisdom/faith lane
Five faculties balance
Fetter-defined fruits
Jhana without fruit
SN12.23 samadhi necessary-but-unquantified
Lokuttara-jhana split

The pre-breakthrough 'pliant, hindrance-free, uplifted, clear mind' formula (kallacitta) as the canon's un-named access-concentration — the single most load-bearing inference, letting us say the FLOOR is canonically attested even though the term upacāra is 0
The five faculties / saddhānusārī & dhammānusārī typology — the absence of a concentration-defined stream-entry type implies samādhi is not the gating faculty for the first breakthrough
Mass and simultaneous dhammacakkhu scenes — a scale reductio against per-person deep-jhāna at stream-entry
Attainment under acute pain / on the deathbed — bounds the required concentration from ABOVE (incompatible with appanā-jhāna)
Phala-samāpatti as a downstream FRUIT one re-enters — shows deep absorption is a consequence, not a precondition, of attainment, explaining why 'fulfiller of samādhi' is reserved for the non-returner
The Susīma paññāvimutta arahants — liberation explicitly without the higher attainments, the upper-end analogue of a low-concentration lower-fruit path

The lokuttara-jhāna doctrine's equivocation between jhāna-by-factor-presence (ekaggatā in every wholesome citta) and jhāna-by-absorptive-development — dissolves the apparent commentarial requirement at the path-moment
Term-stratum distribution of upacāra/appanā/khaṇika/parikamma-nimitta (0 canon) vs the states they name (present in canon under other words) — the clean canon-vs-commentary demonstration
Gotrabhū's split sense (canonical 'lineage-person' in AN vs commentarial cittavīthi-moment) — localizes the pre-magga concentration machinery to the post-canonical stratum
akāliko/sandiṭṭhiko self-description of the fruit as immediate — soft theological pressure against an absorption-ramp before the fruit

The Buddha's own four-jhāna→tevijjā awakening, disaggregated as ARAHANT/Buddha-grade rather than stream-entry-grade — supports a SPLIT floor (deep jhāna high, threshold composure low) that exactly fits the AN 3.86 gradient

The seven noble persons (ariya-puggala) sorted by presence/absence of the formless liberations 'touched with the body' — kāyasakkhī & ubhatobhāgavimutta (have them) vs paññāvimutta & diṭṭhippatta (lack them): direct evidence of awakened persons without the full jhāna/formless ladder.

The fetter-model placement: which fetters fall at which fruit — restlessness (uddhacca) and form/formless-lust (rūpa/arūparāga) at arahantship only, none of the three lowest fetters being samādhi-related: tells you where in the ladder high samādhi becomes structurally implicated.

The threefold training (sīla/samādhi/paññā) and adhicitta-sikkhā glossed as the four jhānas: the strongest structural counter-pressure toward a universal jhāna step; must be weighed honestly since it can either mandate jhāna or, via 'to-a-measure', dissolve into the gradient.

The four paṭipadā (modes of progress by faculty-sharpness) and dandhābhiññā (slow direct-knowledge) attainers: whether weak-faculty persons still reach the goal, just slowly — speed-not-eligibility reading.

The commentarial concentration triad parikamma/upacāra/appanā and the sukkhavipassaka — the apparatus that both creates the access-floor and the dry-insight exemption; dating its first appearance localises the whole drift.
Faculty-balancing & adhipati (dominance) doctrine — the niche for paññā-led, samādhi-junior attainers.

Canonical terminological drift of 'appanā' — sutta/Abhidhamma sense (fixing of applied thought, an aspect of vitakka) vs commentarial sense (absorption-concentration): a term whose meaning-shift is itself a dating instrument for the jhāna-gate.

Right-concentration (sammā-samādhi) defined as the four jhānas in the path-factor formula vs samādhindriya NOT so defined — the asymmetry between the path-factor and the faculty is an orthogonal clue about whether 'concentration' means jhāna everywhere or only in the eightfold-path slot.

The dhammānusārī / saddhānusārī floor (those on the way to stream-entry with faculties merely under development) — the lowest rung, defined by incomplete faculties, as the canon's own statement of the minimum.

Anupubba-sikkhā / gradual-training pericopes and any liberation template reaching release through perception-of-impermanence/not-self without an explicit jhāna step — structural skips that bound how mandatory the jhāna rung is.

The fetter-model of the four fruits (which samyojana is cut at each) cross-referenced with samatha's canonical job (abandoning raga/kama-raga) — if the samatha-target fetter is cut only at non-return, the lower fruits are structurally calm-light
The 'doer to a measure / fulfiller' (mattaso kari / paripurakari) grading as an implicit quantification of the floor without a number

Stream-entry-on-hearing narratives and the kalla-mudu-vinivarana-udagga-pasanna pre-dhamma-eye mind-state as the canon's de facto sub-jhanic floor (hindrances momentarily suppressed = a canonical proto-upacara)
The pannavimutta / ubhatobhaga-vimutta decoupling of full liberation from the formless attainments (top fruit reachable without deepest calm => monotonic lower-fruit floor)
The seven-noble-persons faculty-architecture (SN 48.24) sorting the entry tier by faith/wisdom dominance rather than samadhi attainment
Commentarial vocabulary (upacara-samadhi, khanika-samadhi, sukkha-vipassaka, vipassana-yanika) as gap-fill markers — their attachment points locate the exact canonical silences
The balance/over-balance doctrine (effort vs serenity, the five-faculty pairing, the lute-string) implying a tunable RANGE of samatha with a floor, not a fixed jhanic gate
The definitional tension between samma-samadhi=four-jhanas (SN 45.8) and the trainee being only a measure-doer in samadhi — whether the jhana-definition states path-completion vs entry-requirement

The upanisa chain (samadhi -> yathabhuta-nanadassana) vs AN 4.170 insight-first ordering — reconciliation forces 'samadhi' in the chain to mean composure-insight-rides-on rather than pre-attained jhana
The samasisi / simultaneous-completion figure and yuganaddha mechanics (same-object problem) as a test of whether momentary concentration suffices for the yoked way
Lay attainers (countless sotapanna/sakadagami laypeople) who are nowhere described as jhana-practitioners — sociological negative space about the realistic floor for the lower fruits
Jhana attributed canonically to the NON-returner's residence (anagami reborn in the rupa/arupa Pure Abodes) — the structural pairing of deep absorption with the third fruit, not the first two
The Anguttara/Samyutta 'samahita' vs 'jhayi' lexical choice in insight-enabling formulas as a fine probe of whether composure-short-of-absorption was deliberately intended
the four limbs/possessions of stream-entry (sotāpattiyaṅga) — sufficiency ledger
the fetter-by-fruit ledger (samyojana) — subtractive definition of fruits
the seven/nine noble persons taxonomy (ariyapuggala) — who is defined by attainments vs not
the mattaso-kārī ↔ paripūra-kārī three-trainings gradient — the only fruit-keyed samādhi quantity
paññāvimutta / sukkhavipassaka (dry-insight arahant) — top fruit with reduced samādhi
Susīma's arahants — explicit awakening without formless attainments
the four routes of AN 4.170 (samatha/vipassanā ordering) — non-mandatory samatha-first
competing definitions of sammā-samādhi (jhāna-list vs path-supported one-pointedness vs Abhidhammic ekaggatā)
nīvaraṇa-pahāna 'workable mind' threshold — the canon's actual soft floor (≠ named jhāna)
the upanisa proximate-cause chain — samādhi necessary-in-kind but unquantified
commentarial upacāra / appanā / parikamma / khaṇika-samādhi — post-canonical floor apparatus
Abhidhamma lokuttara-jhāna of the path-moment — jhāna as concomitant not precondition
five faculties' uneven maturation (indriya-vemattatā) — concentration as one dial of several
lay/householder + deva stream-enterers still enjoying sensuality — population evidence
concentration-tuning suttas (lute-string, three nimittas) — continuum vs gate framing
the on-the-spot dhamma-eye conversion pericope — narrated mechanism of stream-entry
Dhp 372 + jhāyatha exhortations — strongest pro-jhāna voice, but exhortative not criterial
cross-stratum vocabulary distribution of concentration-tier terms — empirical canon/commentary boundary
the four sotapattiyanga (causes of stream-entry) — jhana's absence from the list
the persons-typology (nine/seven persons; saupadisesa) — concentration graded by fruit
transcendental dependent origination / upanisa cascade — 'samadhi' unspecified as the link to yathabhutañana
the Dhamma-eye episodes after graduated talks — sudden lay/sick stream-entry with no jhana narrated
arahants entering jhana/cessation for present comfort — pure abiding-function at the top
the AN 4.41 four-purposes partition — jhana mapped to abiding, taint-destruction to aggregate contemplation
the jhana↔Pure-Abodes / non-return cosmology — full jhana wired to the non-return tier
MN 117's relational sammasamadhi — one-pointedness defined by path-context not altitude
the commentarial upacara/appana/padakajjhana/sukkhavipassaka cloud — measuring the floor-fill
the brahmavihara as a parallel 'vihara' (dwelling) that can also be soteriologically wired (metta→non-return) — testing whether 'vihara'-labelled states are systematically abiding-over-cause
deathbed and illness suttas (gilana) — attainment under conditions hostile to jhana cultivation
the seven enlightenment factors ordering (samadhi-bojjhanga late, after piti/passaddhi) — concentration as a matured fruit of the sequence rather than its entry gate
grammatical verb-governance of 'jhana' (viharati vs abhininnameti/nissaya/padaka) — the abiding-vs-cause grammar census
Bodhisatta's pre-awakening jhana attainments judged insufficient (MN 36) — attainment decoupled from liberation
vimuttisukha / phalasamapatti — fruition-attainment as a dwelling enjoyed post-path, not a cause of it

The grammatical voice of samādhi-verbs across strata (middle/passive resultative -samādhiyati 'becomes concentrated' vs agentive samādhiṃ bhāveti / jhānaṃ samāpajjati) — the morphology itself encodes caused vs cultivated

The fetter/hindrance accounting of the stream-enterer (doubt vicikicchā abandoned, worry kukkucca suppressed by perfected virtue) — what obstructions the fruit removes determines concentration-readiness without a jhāna count

The demographic profile of named lower-fruit attainers in the suttas (Anāthapiṇḍika, Visākhā, lay sotāpannas) — whether the canon ever ascribes the four jhānas to them, a population-level inference about the floor
Generosity / dāna / cāga as a concentration source — an orthogonal subject where gladness→samādhi appears for non-meditators
The recollection (anussati) practices as a non-jhānic concentration delivery system attached to noble disciples
avippaṭisāra and the 'na cetanāya karaṇīyaṃ' (no will needed) framing — the canon's explicit denial that these fruits, samādhi included, are willed
The placement of samādhi in causal lattices (mid-chain in SN 12.23, below knowledge-and-vision) vs as a terminal attainment — directionality of conditioning
The commentarial tripartition parikamma/upacāra/appanā and its near-absence in the canon — a layer-signature that locates the quantified sub-jhānic floor in the Aṭṭhakathā, not the suttas

ariyakanta-sīla (virtue dear to the noble ones) as the named stream-entry component and the canonically-named upstream cause of caused-samādhi — closes the loop between the mattaso-kārī gradient and the upanisā mechanism
Right concentration defined by path-factor companionship (MN 117) rather than jhāna depth — concentration individuated by its causes

The samādhindriya 'gained by making release the object' definition — concentration grown by soteriological orientation, which the stream-streamer (one entered upon the stream toward release) structurally possesses

The yuganaddha / vipassanā-first sequencings and the jhāyī-vs-dhammayoga even-handedness — canonical refusal to make jhāna the sole gate, bounding how much concentration the path can be said to require
Lay disciples (upasaka/upasika) and their attested fruits without jhana training
Women, slaves, and court women as attainers (social access to seclusion as a proxy variable)
Children and young novices as attainers (training-duration ceiling)
Deathbed and gravely-ill attainers (pain as a jhana-blocker)
Non-human attainers — devas, the frog, yakkhas (mode-of-life precludes human jhana regime)
Speed of attainment / mass attainment 'at the end of the verse' (temporal compression)
Posture and activity at the attainment moment (walking/eating/working vs cross-legged absorption)
The seven-ariya-puggala typology (formless-touch as the differentia of the TOP types only)
The 'doer to a measure' gradient itself (mattaso vs paripuri mapped to fruits)
The four factors for stream-entry / two conditions for right view (causal enumeration omitting jhana)
Hindrance-suppression vocabulary as the actual pre-insight requirement (weaker than jhana)
The affective concentration cascade (pamojja→samadhi) and the six recollections (lay-accessible steadiness)
Vinaya false-claim taxonomy (jhana vs magga/phala as separately falsifiable = conceptually decoupled)
Etadagga jhana/iddhi specialists vs lay lower-fruit cohort (non-overlap correlation)
Grief/samvega-triggered conversions (momentary collectedness, not samatha, as trigger)
Seclusion-infrastructure vocabulary (arañña/suññagara/patisallana) clustering with higher attainment
Commentarial coinages absent from canon (upacara, khanika, sukkhavipassaka) as gap-markers
Population/census tallies of fruit-distribution (demographic gradient as concentration proxy)
Cross-tradition parallels (Agama/Chinese) presence-or-absence of a jhana frame before the dhamma-eye
Jhana-as-basis texts (MN 64, the fourth-jhana awakening stem) located at the UPPER fruits only

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The core gradient formula itself: 'doer to a measure / fulfiller' across the three trainings (mattaso kari vs paripurakari)
avenues.rationale: This is the exact verse cluster (AN 3.86/3.87, AN 9.12, Pp) where the canon grades partial vs full attainment and explicitly assigns the partial-concentration trainee the lower fruits. The hinge of the whole question.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canonical text that pairs a NON-full measure of samadhi with stream-entry/once-return — direct evidence the lower fruits do NOT require fulfilled (jhanic) concentration.
avenues.inference_path: If 'mattaso kari' in samadhi is canonically sufficient for sotapanna/sakadagami while 'paripurakari' is reserved for the non-returner, the canon ITSELF grades concentration as graded, not all-or-nothing — and never says the 'measure' is a jhana. A near-absence of any number attached to 'matta' is itself the finding: the canon gives a gradient with no quantum, which is precisely the gap upacara fills. Co-occurrence of mattaso-kari verses with arahatta/anagami fruit-lists lets you reconstruct the full fruit-by-training-degree matrix.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The commentarial fill: access vs absorption concentration (upacara / appana) and neighbourhood/moment concentration
avenues.rationale: Tests the claim that upacara-samadhi is 0-canon and a purely commentarial construct. Locating every occurrence (and which layer it lives in) calibrates exactly how much of the 'floor' is post-canonical.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The stratum (canon=0 expected, Abhidhamma?, atthakatha, tika) at which the sub-jhanic concentration vocabulary first appears — pinning the commentarial origin of the 'access' floor.
avenues.inference_path: If 'upacara' returns zero rows in mula/Abhidhamma and only fires in atthakatha+tika, that confirms the floor-below-jhana is a commentarial interpolation — the canon's 'measure' was reinterpreted as 'access' by Buddhaghosa et al. Conversely a single canonical hit would overturn the consensus. The DENSITY of khanikasamadhi (momentary concentration) in commentary specifically signals the vehicle by which dry-insight was retro-licensed: it is the concentration the bare-insight worker is said to have.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Limited / immeasurable concentration — parittam samadhi vs appamanam samadhi
avenues.rationale: The canon contrasts limited and immeasurable concentration (e.g. AN 4.123-ish, Abhidhamma jhana-vibhanga). 'Limited concentration' is a candidate canonical idiom for a low-but-real samadhi floor distinct from full jhana.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon has its OWN graded-magnitude vocabulary for concentration (limited/immeasurable/exalted) independent of the jhana count.
avenues.inference_path: If 'parittasamadhi' is canonical and tied to lower rebirths/attainments while 'appamana'/'mahaggata' track jhana, the canon already encodes a sub-maximal concentration tier in its own words — which the dry-insight side can claim as scriptural warrant, and which would mean upacara is just a renaming, not an invention. Even an orthogonal hit (limited concentration → limited rebirth) lets you infer the canon thinks concentration comes in degrees that matter soteriologically.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The four developments / kinds of samadhi-bhavana and 'concentration that leads to X'
avenues.rationale: AN 4.41 grades four samadhi-developments by their fruit, including one merely for pleasant abiding and one for the destruction of the taints. The fruit-graded typology is the canon's own answer to 'how much, for what'.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A canonical mapping of concentration-types to outcomes, including which concentration is said to lead to the destruction of the taints (the awakening-relevant one).
avenues.inference_path: If the only samadhi explicitly 'samvattati' to asava-khaya is the jhana-tier one, that is evidence FOR a jhana requirement; if the taint-destroying development is described in dhamma-vipassana terms (contemplating arising/passing) without absorption, that is evidence for the dry path. The 'leads-to' verb (samvattati) is the inference vehicle: trace every concentration-noun that takes asavakkhaya/nanadassana as its goal and you reconstruct the canon's implicit floor.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The stream-enterer's actual equipment: faculties, factors-of-stream-entry, and what is NEVER required
avenues.rationale: If the canon lists what makes a stream-enterer (four limbs of stream-entry, unwavering confidence, virtue) and jhana/samadhi is conspicuously NOT among them, the absence is load-bearing.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canonical job-description of the lower-fruit attainer — and whether concentration attainment appears in it at all.
avenues.inference_path: A NEAR-ABSENCE here is the strongest dry-insight evidence: the sotapattiyanga lists (faith, virtue, etc.) omit jhana, and the dhammanusari/saddhanusari types are explicitly distinguished from the kayasakkhi (body-witness, the jhana-attainer). If the jhana-less follower-types (dhammanusari, saddhanusari, ditthippatta, saddhavimutta) can reach stream-entry while only kayasakkhi/ubhatobhagavimutta are defined by formless-attainment, the canon has already split the path into a concentration-heavy and a concentration-light lane. Co-occurrence of saddhanusari with sotapatti and its CONTRAST with kayasakkhi is the decisive matrix.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The seven types of noble persons (kayasakkhi etc.) — concentration as the axis that separates them
avenues.rationale: MN 70 (Kitagiri) and Pp define the seven persons by whether they 'touch the eight liberations with the body' (formless jhana) vs reach release by wisdom alone. This is the canon's own jhana-vs-dry distinction.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: An explicit canonical taxonomy in which pannavimutta (wisdom-liberated) reaches the goal WITHOUT the formless attainments that define ubhatobhagavimutta.
avenues.inference_path: The pannavimutta is the canonical lynch-pin of the dry-insight debate (does pannavimutta still have the four rupa-jhanas, or none?). If the canon defines pannavimutta purely by absence of the formless liberations and is SILENT on whether they have rupa-jhana, that silence is the exact contested gap. Search the gloss of 'na phusitva viharati' (does not touch with the body) to see how far down the concentration ladder the canon lets an arahant stand — and by inference, how much further down a sotapanna may stand.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Right concentration defined AS the four jhanas — and whether that definition is exceptionless
avenues.rationale: The stock definition of samma-samadhi (the 8th path-factor) IS the four jhanas (DN 22, SN 45.8). If samma-samadhi is necessary for the path and is always defined as jhana, that is the strongest pro-jhana argument; any non-jhanic definition is the counter.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether samma-samadhi ever receives a sub-jhanic gloss anywhere in the corpus, or is monolithically the four jhanas.
avenues.inference_path: The pro-jhana case rests on: (1) samma-samadhi = path-factor = jhana, (2) stream-entry requires the path. The dry side must break link (1) or (2). Search for the 'noble right concentration with its supports' (sa-upanisa, AN-style) where samadhi is shown as the FRUIT of the prior seven factors rather than a precondition — if right view through right mindfulness produce right concentration, then concentration is downstream of insight, inverting the requirement. A commentarial gloss reading 'jhana' as 'including upacara/khanika' would show the tradition itself softening the jhana definition to fit dry-insight practitioners.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Suttas that explicitly thematize 'enough/this much' concentration or warn against over/under
avenues.rationale: The 'as far as / just this much' formulas (yavadeva, ettavata) and the goldilocks tuning suttas (effort too taut/too slack) are where the canon talks about sufficiency and calibration of mental cultivation.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Canonical language of sufficiency ('this much suffices') applied to concentration — the closest the canon comes to quantifying the measure.
avenues.inference_path: If 'ettavata' or 'yavadeva' attaches to a concentration-state as 'enough for' a fruit, you get a canonical sufficiency threshold. Even an orthogonal hit — yavadeva applied to mindfulness ('there is mindfulness just to the extent of knowing', Satipatthana refrain) — lets you infer the canon's model of 'bare sufficiency' for awakening factors, which transfers by analogy to concentration. The tuning suttas (Sona's lute) imply concentration has a correct level that is NOT 'maximum', undercutting a maximalist jhana reading.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Cittekaggata / unification as the minimal definitional core of samadhi (Abhidhamma)
avenues.rationale: Abhidhamma (Dhammasangani, Vibhanga) defines samadhi minimally as ekaggata, a universal mental factor present in EVERY mind-moment. This is the technical basis for 'momentary concentration' as real samadhi.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon/Abhidhamma's definitional floor for samadhi is the universal ekaggata factor rather than jhana — making 'some samadhi' trivially always present.
avenues.inference_path: If samadhi-as-ekaggata is a universal cetasika (Abhidhamma), then the 'measure' of samadhi a stream-enterer needs could be vanishingly low by definition — every wholesome insight-moment already contains samadhi. This is the Abhidhammic root of khanikasamadhi and the strongest systematic warrant for dry insight. A co-occurrence of ekaggata with the path-moment (maggacitta) descriptions would show that even the stream-entry mind-moment is defined to contain concentration, dissolving the 'no concentration' worry into 'minimal concentration always present'. Orthogonal inference: if ekaggata is graded by object (paritta/mahaggata/appamana arammana), the magnitude vocabulary reappears at the factor level.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The samatha/vipassana yoking and the four ways to arahantship (Yuganaddha)
avenues.rationale: AN 4.170 (Yuganaddha) lists four routes including samatha-first, vipassana-first, and yoked — the canonical seed for a vipassana-led path. The commentary's 'suddhavipassanayanika' (pure-insight vehicle) is the dry-insight terminus.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canonical sanction (or not) for developing insight BEFORE/without leading with serenity — and the commentarial coinage of the bare-insight vehicle.
avenues.inference_path: If 'vipassanapubbangama' (insight-preceded) is canonical, the canon admits a path where concentration is NOT cultivated first — strong dry-insight evidence, though the canon still says samadhi eventually arises ('then the path is born, he develops it'). The KEY inference is the gap between the canonical 'vipassana-first' (concentration comes later but still comes) and the commentarial 'suddhavipassanayanika' (pure-insight, no jhana ever): search 'suddhavipassana' to see it is commentary-only, confirming the fully-dry path is a commentarial extrapolation FROM a canonical insight-first option. 'Dhammuddhacca' (agitation about dhammas, AN 4.170-adjacent) marks the failure-mode of too-little concentration — its treatment reveals how much samadhi the tradition thought minimally necessary.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Concentration as proximate cause of yathabhutananadassana — the 'concentrated mind sees things as they are' formula
avenues.rationale: The transcendental dependent-origination chain (SN 12.23, upanisa) makes samadhi the proximate cause of knowing-and-seeing-as-it-is. If concentration is causally necessary for liberating insight, a floor is structurally implied.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The causal necessity (and the degree-language) by which concentration conditions liberating wisdom — does 'samahita' mean jhana-concentrated or merely composed?
avenues.inference_path: The whole debate can be relocated onto the single word 'samahita': if the upanisa-sutta's 'samahito yathabhutam janati passati' requires jhanic samahita, concentration is structurally upstream of stream-entry and a floor exists; if 'samahita' can mean a momentarily collected mind, the floor collapses to near-zero. Search how commentary glosses 'samahita' in this formula — a gloss admitting upacara/khanika here is the textual moment the tradition lowered the floor. Orthogonal: the gladdening-sequence (pamojja→piti→passaddhi→sukha→samadhi) shows samadhi as a NATURAL outflow of joy from virtue/insight, implying it need not be separately engineered to jhana depth.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Lower-fruit attainment narratives WITHOUT jhana — on-the-spot awakenings during a talk
avenues.rationale: Countless suttas show listeners attaining the Dhamma-eye (stream-entry) on the spot during a graduated discourse — apparently with no jhana practice. The phenomenology of these events is prime dry-insight evidence.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether stream-entry is depicted as requiring prior concentration attainment or arising directly from hearing/contemplating the Dhamma 'in that very seat'.
avenues.inference_path: The 'dhammacakkhu arose in that very seat' formula is the empirical core: if hundreds of stream-entries occur mid-discourse with NO mention of jhana, the canon's PRACTICE-PORTRAIT of the lower fruits is concentration-light, regardless of the theory. A near-total absence of jhana-language in stream-entry narratives is decisive negative evidence. Counter-inference: the commentary may retro-supply that such listeners had 'access concentration' at the moment of insight — search whether atthakatha inserts upacara/khanika into these narratives, which would show the tradition felt the need to back-fill a floor the suttas left empty. The anupubbikatha (graduated talk: giving→virtue→heaven→renunciation→four truths) is the canonical 'preparation' that REPLACES jhana for these attainers.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The jhana-as-necessary maximalist passages — to weigh the other side fairly
avenues.rationale: Dhp 372 ('no wisdom without jhana'), the 'meditate, do not be negligent' exhortations, and the nine progressive abidings are the pro-jhana proof-texts. Mapping their force and their addressees calibrates the requirement.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The strongest canonical statements that jhana is indispensable — and crucially WHO they are addressed to (full liberation/anagami vs stream-entry).
avenues.inference_path: The decisive move is addressee-scoping: if 'no wisdom without jhana' and the meditate-exhortations target the HIGHER fruits (final liberation, the non-returner = the concentration-fulfiller) while the lower fruits get the concentration-light treatment, both sides are reconciled and the answer to the question is GRADED (little for sotapanna, much for anagami/arahant). Search whether jhana-mandatory verses co-occur with arahatta/anagami vs sotapatti. Even Dhp 372's near-absence of any stream-entry pairing is informative. Saupadisesa (AN 9.12) explicitly says even the mattaso-kari can attain — read alongside the jhana-maximalist verses, it bounds where each applies.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Five faculties / powers balanced — concentration as ONE of five, never solo-decisive
avenues.rationale: Concentration is one of five faculties; their balancing and relative sharpness (tikkha/mudu indriya) is how the canon and commentary explain why different persons need different amounts of samadhi.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canonical framework in which a sharp-wisdom/sharp-faith person can awaken with less concentration than a concentration-led person — the structural basis for variable floors.
avenues.inference_path: If the faculties must be 'balanced' but persons differ in which faculty leads (the indriya-paropariyatta knowledge, the panca-puggala by sharp/dull faculties), the canon BUILT IN inter-individual variation in the required concentration — there is no single floor, only a balanced-enough condition. This dissolves the question: 'how much concentration' has no scalar answer, only 'enough to balance your faith/energy/mindfulness/wisdom'. The commentary's tikkhindriya/mudindriya gloss is where dry-insight (sharp-wisdom, low-samadhi) gets its psychological home. Orthogonal inference: the samadhindriya is itself DEFINED as the four jhanas in some suttas yet as ekaggata in Abhidhamma — the same canon-vs-Abhidhamma split as samma-samadhi, doubling the evidence.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Hindrance-abandonment as the functional definition of 'enough concentration'
avenues.rationale: Jhana is canonically entered by abandoning the five hindrances. The question 'how much concentration' can be reframed as 'must the hindrances be fully suppressed (jhana) or only temporarily quieted (access)?'
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon ties the lower fruits to FULL hindrance-suppression (jhana-equivalent) or to a lighter purification of mind sufficient for insight.
avenues.inference_path: Reframing the floor as a hindrance-state: full continuous suppression = jhana; momentary suppression = the dry-insight worker's condition. If the canon says insight requires a 'mind freed from the hindrances' (vinivaranacitta) but does NOT say it must be jhana-continuous, the floor is 'hindrances quiet enough to see clearly', which access/khanika supplies. Commentarial 'vikkhambhanappahana' (suppression-abandoning) vs the momentary clearing during insight is the technical fork — search how the tradition distinguishes the durability of hindrance-suppression needed. Orthogonal: the upakkilesa (Anuruddha's defilements-of-concentration) sutta shows even deep meditators wobble, implying the lower fruits cannot demand a stability that adepts struggle to hold.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The gradual training (anupubbasikkha) sequence — where concentration sits relative to insight and fruit
avenues.rationale: The gradual-training pericope (DN 2 etc.) places virtue → sense-restraint → mindfulness → jhana → insight → liberation. The seven purifications (Vism) make citta-visuddhi (concentration-purity) a stage before insight-purities.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canonical/commentarial ORDERING that makes concentration a prerequisite stage — and exactly what counts as completing citta-visuddhi.
avenues.inference_path: The seven purifications are the commentary's strongest 'floor' argument: citta-visuddhi precedes ditthi-visuddhi (insight). But Vism EXPLICITLY says citta-visuddhi = upacara OR appana — search the gloss to confirm the commentary itself permits ACCESS concentration to satisfy the concentration-purity stage. That single admission is the textual hinge: the systematic tradition that most demands a concentration floor sets that floor at access, not jhana. Therefore the dry-insight worker satisfies citta-visuddhi with khanika-samadhi (the Visuddhimagga's own provision). The inference chain: floor exists (citta-visuddhi) BUT is set sub-jhanic by the very text asserting it.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Negative/contrastive cases: those who have jhana but no insight, and those who reach the goal reportedly without the formless attainments
avenues.rationale: Non-Buddhist contemplatives (Alara, Uddaka) had the formless attainments yet no liberation — showing concentration is NEITHER sufficient NOR, by the contrast, the operative cause of awakening. Mundane vs supramundane concentration is the commentarial sorting.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Canonical proof that deep concentration alone does not liberate — decoupling samadhi-depth from the fruit and shifting the operative weight onto insight.
avenues.inference_path: If the deepest meditators (formless-attainers) explicitly do NOT awaken, then concentration-depth is not the variable that produces the fruit; insight is. By inference, the lower fruits — being the SHALLOW end of insight — need correspondingly little of a faculty (concentration) that even at maximum does not by itself liberate. This is the abductive backbone of the dry-insight case: awakening tracks insight, not samadhi-depth, so the samadhi-floor for the lowest insight is plausibly minimal. The lokiya/lokuttara-samadhi distinction (commentary) is where the tradition concedes that ordinary jhana is not the path-concentration — relocating the necessary samadhi to the path-moment itself (lokuttara), which is momentary by definition.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: Etymological/definitional entries in the dictionaries and Abhidhamma definitional lists for samadhi and its synonyms
avenues.rationale: The Abhidhamma definitional strings (Dhammasangani's samadhi pericope: thiti, santhiti, avatthiti, avisahara, avikkhepo, samatha, samadhindriya...) reveal the canonical conceptual minimum of samadhi as steadiness, not absorption.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The formal definition-set of samadhi that fixes its minimal sense as steadiness/non-distraction — present in every wholesome mind-moment.
avenues.inference_path: If the authoritative definitional pericope characterizes samadhi as steadiness/non-scattering (thiti, avikkhepo) rather than absorption, then a stream-enterer trivially has 'samadhi' the moment their mind is steadily collected on the truths — no jhana implied. The definition itself sets a near-floorless floor. This is the lexicographic complement to the ekaggata avenue: the canon's own gloss of the WORD samadhi licenses the minimal reading. A dictionary (PED/DPD) entry distinguishing samadhi (broad: composure) from jhana (narrow: absorption) confirms the two are not synonyms, so 'requires samadhi' does not entail 'requires jhana'.

avenues.lens: How much concentration the path requires for the lower fruits — mapping the canon's graded "doer-to-a-measure" (samadhismim mattaso kari) formula against the commentarial sub-jhanic "access concentration" (upacara-samadhi), to locate where, if anywhere, the canon fixes a concentration floor for stream-entry/once-return and whether it licenses a dry-insight path.
avenues.avenue: The non-returner = concentration-fulfiller pairing, and what specifically the higher fruit adds
avenues.rationale: AN 3.86/9.12 explicitly make the non-returner the fulfiller of concentration (and partial in wisdom). What the anagami eliminates (sensual desire, ill-will) is exactly what jhana suppresses — linking full concentration to the third fruit, not the first two.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canonical pairing of FULL concentration with the THIRD fruit (non-returner), implying the first two fruits sit below the concentration-fulfilment line.
avenues.inference_path: This is the positive mirror of the whole question: if 'samadhismim paripurakari' = anagami, and anagami eliminates sensual desire/ill-will (the hindrances jhana suppresses), then the canon DELIBERATELY locates full concentration at the third fruit. By subtraction, the first two fruits require LESS than full concentration — i.e. some sub-fulfilment 'measure', exactly the mattaso-kari. The non-returner avenue thus FIXES THE CEILING that the stream-entry avenue's floor sits below, and the gap between them (sotapanna's measure ... anagami's fulfilment) is the canon's actual, un-quantified answer. The elimination of kama-raga (which jhana suppresses) at the 3rd fruit is the mechanistic link tying concentration-fulfilment to that specific fruit and NOT the lower ones.

avenues.lens: LENS 2 — What concentration (if any) does sotapatti actually REQUIRE? The question is whether jhāna is ever NAMED as a prerequisite for stream-entry, or whether stream-entry is constituted entirely by non-concentration factors (the four sotāpattiyaṅga: aveccappasāda in Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha + ariyakanta-sīla, the breaking of the three saṃyojana, the two entry-modes dhammānusārī/saddhānusārī). The strategy is to enumerate where concentration WOULD be named if it were required, and read its presence, absence, or substitution. A systematic near-absence of samādhi/jhāna in the canonical definitions of stream-entry — while sīla, saddhā/pasāda, and paññā ARE named — is itself the strongest positive finding: it would show the canon constitutes the first fruit on faith+virtue+penetrative-view, not on attainment of jhāna, supporting the 'dry/bare-insight is at least canonically admissible for the lower fruits' side (Polak, Cousins on access, contra a strict jhāna-mandatory reading for sotāpatti). Equally, any locus that DOES couple samādhi/jhāna to sotāpanna would be decisive the other way. The 'doer-to-a-measure in concentration' grading (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12) places the sotāpanna+sakadāgāmī BELOW the concentration-fulfiller (anāgāmī) on the very axis in question — so the canon's own gradient predicts a sub-full-jhāna concentration suffices for the lower two fruits; the task is to find what the floor actually IS, and whether it is ever named at all for the first fruit.
avenues.avenue: The four sotāpattiyaṅga (factors-of-a-stream-enterer) — the canonical CONSTITUTIVE definition. If the formula that DEFINES what a stream-enterer possesses contains no samādhi/jhāna term, that is the core positive evidence.
avenues.rationale: This is the canon's own checklist of what a sotāpanna HAS. The four are: unwavering confidence in Buddha, Dhamma, Saṅgha, and the virtues dear to the noble ones. Concentration is conspicuously absent as a listed factor.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A hit confirms the constitutive list; the question is whether ANY occurrence of the four-factor formula appends or substitutes a samādhi term. Plain four-factor hits with no samādhi = strong near-absence finding.
avenues.inference_path: CRITICAL: the closing epithet of the sīla-factor is 'samādhi-saṃvattanika' (virtues that are 'conducive to concentration'). This is the single thread that connects sotāpatti-sīla to concentration — but note it is conducive-TO, i.e. concentration is downstream of the noble virtue, not a prerequisite already attained. This phrasing itself is an argument: at stream-entry, virtue that LEADS to concentration is present, full concentration is not yet required. A near-absence of any stronger samādhi coupling, combined with this one teleological mention, is the decisive structural reading.

avenues.lens: LENS 2 — What concentration (if any) does sotapatti actually REQUIRE? The question is whether jhāna is ever NAMED as a prerequisite for stream-entry, or whether stream-entry is constituted entirely by non-concentration factors (the four sotāpattiyaṅga: aveccappasāda in Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha + ariyakanta-sīla, the breaking of the three saṃyojana, the two entry-modes dhammānusārī/saddhānusārī). The strategy is to enumerate where concentration WOULD be named if it were required, and read its presence, absence, or substitution. A systematic near-absence of samādhi/jhāna in the canonical definitions of stream-entry — while sīla, saddhā/pasāda, and paññā ARE named — is itself the strongest positive finding: it would show the canon constitutes the first fruit on faith+virtue+penetrative-view, not on attainment of jhāna, supporting the 'dry/bare-insight is at least canonically admissible for the lower fruits' side (Polak, Cousins on access, contra a strict jhāna-mandatory reading for sotāpatti). Equally, any locus that DOES couple samādhi/jhāna to sotāpanna would be decisive the other way. The 'doer-to-a-measure in concentration' grading (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12) places the sotāpanna+sakadāgāmī BELOW the concentration-fulfiller (anāgāmī) on the very axis in question — so the canon's own gradient predicts a sub-full-jhāna concentration suffices for the lower two fruits; the task is to find what the floor actually IS, and whether it is ever named at all for the first fruit.
avenues.avenue: The three fetters broken at stream-entry (sakkāyadiṭṭhi, vicikicchā, sīlabbataparāmāsa) — what is REMOVED, not what is attained. None of the three is a concentration-deficit; all are view/doubt/clinging-to-rites.
avenues.rationale: Stream-entry is defined by the destruction of three fetters. If the path to it required jhāna, one would expect jhāna or a concentration-fetter in the constitutive account; instead the obstacles removed are cognitive/affective (identity-view, doubt, attachment to rites), removable by insight/penetration of the Dhamma.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Confirms the fetter-list. Decisive that the named obstacles to the first fruit are NOT concentration-deficits — the work of stream-entry is diṭṭhi-correction, not jhāna-attainment.
avenues.inference_path: If awakening's lower rung is gated by removing wrong-VIEW and DOUBT (both paññā/saddhā territory) rather than by attaining absorption, then the canonical mechanism of stream-entry is penetrative seeing of the Dhamma, not depth of samādhi. The total absence of any 'unattained-jhāna' fetter at this stage is an argument from the structure of what-must-go that concentration is not the gate.

avenues.lens: LENS 2 — What concentration (if any) does sotapatti actually REQUIRE? The question is whether jhāna is ever NAMED as a prerequisite for stream-entry, or whether stream-entry is constituted entirely by non-concentration factors (the four sotāpattiyaṅga: aveccappasāda in Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha + ariyakanta-sīla, the breaking of the three saṃyojana, the two entry-modes dhammānusārī/saddhānusārī). The strategy is to enumerate where concentration WOULD be named if it were required, and read its presence, absence, or substitution. A systematic near-absence of samādhi/jhāna in the canonical definitions of stream-entry — while sīla, saddhā/pasāda, and paññā ARE named — is itself the strongest positive finding: it would show the canon constitutes the first fruit on faith+virtue+penetrative-view, not on attainment of jhāna, supporting the 'dry/bare-insight is at least canonically admissible for the lower fruits' side (Polak, Cousins on access, contra a strict jhāna-mandatory reading for sotāpatti). Equally, any locus that DOES couple samādhi/jhāna to sotāpanna would be decisive the other way. The 'doer-to-a-measure in concentration' grading (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12) places the sotāpanna+sakadāgāmī BELOW the concentration-fulfiller (anāgāmī) on the very axis in question — so the canon's own gradient predicts a sub-full-jhāna concentration suffices for the lower two fruits; the task is to find what the floor actually IS, and whether it is ever named at all for the first fruit.
avenues.avenue: The two entry-modes: dhammānusārī (Dhamma-follower) and saddhānusārī (faith-follower) — the persons who ENTER the path to stream-entry. The faith-follower in particular cannot plausibly be gated on jhāna.
avenues.rationale: MN 70 (Kīṭāgiri) and SN 25 grade seven persons. The kāyasakkhī ('body-witness') and ubhatobhāgavimutta are explicitly the ones who 'touch the formless/eight liberations with the body' — i.e. jhāna IS named for THEM. By contrast the saddhānusārī and dhammānusārī (the entrants to the stream-path) are defined by faith/wisdom, with NO jhāna predicate.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A hit on kāyasakkhī coupled to 'aṭṭha vimokkhe kāyena phusitvā' shows the canon DOES name jhāna — but only for the higher/concentration-typed persons, NOT for the faith/wisdom entrants. This is the canon partitioning the seven so that jhāna is required for SOME ariya-types and not others.
avenues.inference_path: DECISIVE CONTRAST: if the canon names 'touching the eight liberations with the body' (= jhāna/formless attainment) as the differentia of kāyasakkhī/ubhatobhāgavimutta, and pointedly does NOT name it for saddhānusārī/dhammānusārī/diṭṭhippatta/saddhāvimutta/paññāvimutta, then the canon itself has DECIDED that the stream-path can be entered without jhāna. The paññāvimutta ('liberated-by-wisdom') who does NOT have the formless attainments is the textbook 'dry' arahant — its mere existence in the seven-persons list is canonical proof that even full liberation is admissible without the formless jhānas. Read backward: if even arahantship has a non-jhāna track, a fortiori stream-entry does.

avenues.lens: LENS 2 — What concentration (if any) does sotapatti actually REQUIRE? The question is whether jhāna is ever NAMED as a prerequisite for stream-entry, or whether stream-entry is constituted entirely by non-concentration factors (the four sotāpattiyaṅga: aveccappasāda in Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha + ariyakanta-sīla, the breaking of the three saṃyojana, the two entry-modes dhammānusārī/saddhānusārī). The strategy is to enumerate where concentration WOULD be named if it were required, and read its presence, absence, or substitution. A systematic near-absence of samādhi/jhāna in the canonical definitions of stream-entry — while sīla, saddhā/pasāda, and paññā ARE named — is itself the strongest positive finding: it would show the canon constitutes the first fruit on faith+virtue+penetrative-view, not on attainment of jhāna, supporting the 'dry/bare-insight is at least canonically admissible for the lower fruits' side (Polak, Cousins on access, contra a strict jhāna-mandatory reading for sotāpatti). Equally, any locus that DOES couple samādhi/jhāna to sotāpanna would be decisive the other way. The 'doer-to-a-measure in concentration' grading (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12) places the sotāpanna+sakadāgāmī BELOW the concentration-fulfiller (anāgāmī) on the very axis in question — so the canon's own gradient predicts a sub-full-jhāna concentration suffices for the lower two fruits; the task is to find what the floor actually IS, and whether it is ever named at all for the first fruit.
avenues.avenue: SN 55 Sotāpatti-saṃyutta — the dedicated samyutta on stream-entry. The single highest-yield locus-cluster; whatever IS and ISN'T required for the first fruit is densest here.
avenues.rationale: SN 55 collects the stream-entry teachings: the four factors repeated, the 'mirror of the Dhamma' (dhammādāsa), the consolations to the dying (Dīghāvu, Mahānāma), and the four 'streams of merit'. The systematic content of this samyutta is the best census of what the canon attaches to sotāpatti.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: If the entire Sotāpatti-saṃyutta defines and re-defines the stream-enterer via the four pasāda-factors and never once makes jhāna a condition, that is the most concentrated near-absence evidence available.
avenues.inference_path: The dhammādāsa (mirror of the Dhamma, SN 55.8) lets a disciple DECLARE THEIR OWN stream-entry by checking the four factors — confidence in the triple gem + noble virtue. A self-administered checklist that contains no samādhi-test is the canon saying the first fruit is VERIFIABLE without reference to attainment-states. The deathbed instructions (Mahānāma SN 55.21-22: a confused, frightened, non-meditating lay disciple is still assured of non-decline) is the strongest single argument that stream-entry is not a samādhi-grade: a dying man cannot enter jhāna, yet is assured the goal.

avenues.avenue: Penetration / breakthrough vocabulary — abhisamaya, dhammacakkhu, the moment of seeing 'whatever is subject to arising is subject to cessation'. This is the EVENT of stream-entry; what cognitive faculty does it name?
avenues.rationale: The actual stream-entry event in the canon (e.g. the listeners at the end of suttas) is the arising of the 'dust-free, stainless Dhamma-eye'. The verb is SEEING/penetrating, not absorbing. The faculty named is cakkhu/paññā, not samādhi.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: If the breakthrough is consistently a paññā/dassana event ('the Dhamma-eye arose') with no jhāna predicate at the moment of entry, the constitutive act of stream-entry is insight-typed.
avenues.inference_path: Many dhammacakkhu-arisings in the canon occur AT THE END OF A DISCOURSE — the listener was sitting and listening, not in absorption, when the breakthrough happened. If stream-entry repeatedly arises in the act of hearing-and-penetrating rather than in a meditative attainment, the canon is depicting a path to the first fruit that does not pass through jhāna at the decisive moment. (Caveat to test: did such listeners have prior jhāna 'off-screen'? The texts are silent — that silence itself is the datum.)

avenues.avenue: The samādhi-indriya / samādhi-bala and the faculty-balancing texts — is concentration a NECESSARY indriya for the stream-path, and at what strength?
avenues.rationale: SN 48 (Indriya-saṃyutta) grades beings by the maturity of the five faculties — and explicitly says that those in whom the faculties are WEAK/incomplete are still on the path (saddhānusārī/dhammānusārī are the LOWEST faculty-grade). Samādhi is one of five, not privileged.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: SN 48.12-18 grade the ariya by faculty-completeness; the lowest grades (faith-follower, Dhamma-follower) have the faculties present but immature. If the stream-PATH entrant is defined by INCOMPLETE faculties, then full samādhi-faculty (jhāna) is by definition NOT required to enter.
avenues.inference_path: POWERFUL: SN 48.12 says of the five faculties 'one who has completed and fulfilled them is an arahant; weaker, a non-returner; weaker, a once-returner; weaker, a stream-enterer; weaker still, a Dhamma-follower / faith-follower.' This is the SAME gradient as samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī — concentration (as one indriya) is fulfilled only at the top. A stream-enterer by this text has the samādhi-faculty present but NOT fulfilled. That is the canonical floor: present-but-partial concentration, never quantified, explicitly sub-maximal. This locus may be the single best answer to 'what is the measure for the lower fruits' — the answer is 'the faculty is there but weak,' and the canon refuses to put a jhāna-number on it.

avenues.avenue: Lay stream-enterers and their attainment-profile — named lay sotāpanna who are NOT described as jhāna-attainers. Anāthapiṇḍika, Visākhā, the 'many lay followers' formulae.
avenues.rationale: The canon names large numbers of lay stream-enterers (and even once-returners) who are householders 'enjoying sensual pleasures' (kāmabhogī). A jhāna-attainer is technically isolated from sensuality at the moment of absorption; the persistence of a sensual lay life alongside confirmed stream-entry is structurally hard to reconcile with a jhāna-mandatory reading.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Census of named lay sotāpanna with no jhāna ascription, especially any described as kāmabhogī, is strong indirect evidence that the lower fruits do not require sustained jhāna mastery.
avenues.inference_path: If sakadāgāmī (the second fruit) is held by 'kāmabhogī' householders (the canon lists householder once-returners), and a once-returner by AN 3.86 is STILL only a 'doer-to-a-measure' in concentration, then the canon is comfortable placing two fruits' worth of attainment in people whose daily life includes sensuality — incompatible with a requirement of mastered, frequently-entered jhāna for those fruits. The lay census is an orthogonal route to the concentration-floor: it bounds the floor from above by who-is-allowed-to-hold-the-fruit.

avenues.avenue: The Anāgāmī as the concentration-fulfiller — the UPPER boundary. Pin where 'samādhismiṃ paripūrakārī' attaches, to fix by contrast where the lower fruits sit.
avenues.rationale: AN 3.86/87 and AN 9.12 give the explicit three-tier grading: the stream-enterer/once-returner are 'doer-to-a-measure' (mattaso kārī) in concentration AND wisdom but FULFILLERS in virtue; the non-returner is the FULFILLER in concentration. This is the canonical key text — it gives the gradient that the whole question turns on.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Direct confirmation of the three-tier samādhi grading. Stream-enterer = full sīla, partial samādhi, partial paññā. The canon explicitly does NOT make full concentration a condition of the first two fruits — it RESERVES full concentration for the third.
avenues.inference_path: This is the load-bearing inference of the entire lens. AN 3.86 is the canon QUANTIFYING the gradient qualitatively: it says in so many words that the lower two fruits are held with only PARTIAL concentration. Therefore any reading that requires mastered jhāna for stream-entry contradicts the canon's own grading, which reserves concentration-fulfilment for the anāgāmī. The 'measure' is left unquantified — and the avenue's payoff is to show the canon DELIBERATELY leaves it unquantified while being explicit that it is sub-fulfilment. The commentarial upacāra-samādhi is the later attempt to name this deliberately-unnamed measure; its zero canonical attestation (per the brief) is itself a finding to foreground.

avenues.avenue: Whether jhāna is EVER syntactically coupled to sotāpanna/sotāpatti in one breath — the direct adversarial test for the jhāna-mandatory side. Search for co-occurrence.
avenues.rationale: The honest adversarial move: actively look for ANY canonical passage that conditions stream-entry on jhāna. If none exists despite exhaustive search, the negative is established by the failure of the strongest pro-jhāna search to find a coupling.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Any genuine jhāna→sotāpatti conditional would weaken the dry-path reading. Near-zero coupling strengthens it. The texture of WHERE jhāna and sotāpatti co-occur (e.g. only in 'a stream-enterer who ALSO attains jhāna becomes a non-returner' type passages) is itself informative.
avenues.inference_path: If the only places jhāna and the lower fruits co-occur are passages where ADDING jhāna to a stream-enterer's profile UPGRADES them toward non-return (e.g. AN 9.12's gradient, or the 'one who fulfils concentration' becoming anāgāmī), then jhāna functions canonically as what MOVES you UP from the stream, not as what gets you INTO it. Co-occurrence read directionally flips from a threat to a confirmation: jhāna is the engine of the THIRD fruit, decoupled from the first.

avenues.avenue: The gradual training (anupubbasikkhā) and whether stream-entry is placed BEFORE or AFTER the jhāna block in any sequenced path. Position in the sequence is evidence.
avenues.rationale: In the Sāmaññaphala-style gradual path, the jhānas come as 'higher mind' (adhicitta) BEFORE the insight-knowledges. But the three-trainings grading (AN 3.86) places the stream-enterer as a FULFILLER of adhisīla only, a partial-doer of adhicitta. The tension between a sequenced path (jhāna-then-insight) and a graded attainment (stream-entry on partial concentration) is itself a major datum in the live debate.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon ever asserts that one must COMPLETE the adhicitta (jhāna) training before the first fruit, or whether the fruits are graded ACROSS an incomplete training. AN 3.86 supports the latter.
avenues.inference_path: The three-trainings grading is the canon's own resolution of the sequence-vs-grade tension: you can be a stream-enterer (full sīla, partial citta, partial paññā) WITHOUT having completed the citta-training. So even if the FULL path's ideal order is sīla→samādhi→paññā, the FRUITS do not wait for samādhi to be completed. This directly defeats the 'you must finish the jhāna stage before any fruit' reading and is the bridge between Lens 2 (sotāpatti) and the master gradient question.

avenues.avenue: Right Concentration in the Noble Eightfold Path defined AS the four jhānas — the strongest pro-jhāna structural argument, to be confronted directly. If sammāsamādhi = the four jhānas, and the path is needed for any fruit, does that mandate jhāna for stream-entry?
avenues.rationale: The standard definition of sammāsamādhi (the 8th path-factor) IS the four jhānas (DN 22, SN 45.8). The pro-jhāna camp argues: stream-entry requires the path, the path includes sammāsamādhi, sammāsamādhi = jhāna, therefore jhāna is required. This must be enumerated and its premises tested.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon ALWAYS defines sammāsamādhi as the four jhānas, or admits alternative definitions (e.g. MN 117's 'unification of mind equipped with the seven factors' = ariya sammāsamādhi, which is more functional than absorption-specific).
avenues.inference_path: Two defeaters to enumerate: (1) MN 117 defines noble right-concentration functionally as 'cittassa ekaggatā with the seven supports' — unification, not necessarily absorption-jhāna; this opens a sub-jhānic reading of sammāsamādhi compatible with the AN 3.86 'partial concentration' of the stream-enterer. (2) The stream-PATH person (saddhānusārī) is by definition still DEVELOPING the eightfold path; possessing the path-factor 'right concentration' as a developing faculty is not the same as having mastered jhāna. So even granting sammāsamādhi = jhāna at fulfilment, the ENTRANT has it only partially — consistent with AN 3.86. The pro-jhāna syllogism equivocates between 'the path includes X' and 'X must be completed before the first fruit.'

The question is whether jhāna is ever NAMED as a prerequisite for stream-entry, or whether stream-entry is constituted entirely by non-concentration factors (the four sotāpattiyaṅga: aveccappasāda in Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha + ariyakanta-sīla, the breaking of the three saṃyojana, the two entry-modes dhammānusārī/saddhānusārī). avenues.avenue: Sukha/pīti and the 'pleasant abidings' coupled to stream-entry — whether the joy of the four pasāda-factors substitutes for jhānic pīti.
avenues.rationale: The 'natural-concentration' sequence (e.g. AN 11.2 / the 'no need for an act of will' chain: virtue→non-remorse→gladness→joy→tranquillity→pleasure→concentration) shows concentration ARISING from virtue+gladness, not from deliberate jhāna-cultivation. This is the canonical mechanism by which the stream-enterer's full sīla yields concentration 'automatically'.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: If the canon repeatedly derives samādhi as the FRUIT of sīla+pāmojja rather than as a precondition, then the stream-enterer (full sīla) is positioned to GENERATE concentration organically — the floor is 'whatever concentration virtue-born gladness produces,' again unquantified and not framed as deliberate jhāna.
avenues.inference_path: This is the affective bridge: the sīla-factor of sotāpatti is 'samādhi-saṃvattanika' (concentration-conducive), and AN 11.2 spells out the mechanism — the noble virtue of the stream-enterer NATURALLY conduces to concentration through non-remorse and gladness. So the canon's model is sīla→samādhi (downstream), not samādhi→sotāpatti (upstream). The concentration of a stream-enterer is the by-product of their secured virtue, not a separately-mastered jhāna gate. This reframes the 'measure' as emergent, not prerequisite — orthogonal support for the dry-path-admissible reading.
