A near-absence is as probative as a hit: if the commentaries must coin upacāra-samādhi and sukkhavipassaka, the canon did not supply them — that silence is the finding.
avenues.avenue: Lists of the path-equipment where samādhi/jhāna sits among co-equal factors (37 bodhipakkhiyā, five indriyas/balas, jhāna as ONE of five faculties to be BALANCED) — does any single faculty get singled out as indispensable?
avenues.rationale: Samādhi appears as one of five faculties (with faith, energy, mindfulness, wisdom) and one of five powers, and grounds the four iddhipādas (chanda/viriya/citta/vīmaṃsā-samādhi). Whether the canon ever privileges samādhi as the non-negotiable faculty, or treats it as one balanceable element, bears on its necessity.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: How samādhi is weighted relative to wisdom in the faculty/power schemes — co-equal, subordinate, or indispensable.
avenues.inference_path: WEIGHTING INFERENCE. If the canon repeatedly defines the samādhi-faculty AS the four jhānas (it does in the Indriya-vibhaṅga), that re-instantiates the definitional pro-jhāna argument at the faculty level. BUT if the faculties are to be BALANCED (indriya-samatta) and wisdom is elsewhere called chief (paññuttara, paññā as the roof-peak in MN/AN similes), the scheme treats samādhi as necessary-but-not-supreme. The orthogonal inference: the four iddhipādas each pair a drive with samādhi (chanda-samādhi etc.), suggesting samādhi is the common substrate of all attainment — a structural pro-necessity datum independent of the jhāna debate. Counter-check the SAMĀDHI-FACULTY definition in the Sutta vs Abhidhamma: if Suttanta-bhājanīya defines samādhindriya as the four jhānas but Abhidhamma-bhājanīya defines it as bare cittass'ekaggatā, the TWO canonical layers disagree on whether the concentration-faculty IS jhāna — and the Abhidhamma's ekaggatā definition is the canonical seed the commentary grows upacāra from. That intra-canonical definitional split would be a major finding.

avenues.lens: LENS 4 — Is jhāna required for awakening at all? The question is whether the canonical (CST Pāli: nikāya + Vinaya + Abhidhamma + aṭṭhakathā + ṭīkā) record makes the four rūpa-jhānas a NECESSARY condition for any of the four fruits (especially the lower two, stream-entry and once-return), or whether it leaves room for a "dry-insight"/bare-insight (sukkhavipassaka) path. The lens reasons from BOTH directions: (a) pro-jhāna loci — sammāsamādhi DEFINED as the four jhānas, the "depending on the first jhāna the destruction of taints" frame, the jhāyatha exhortations, the jhāna→nibbāna path framings; and (b) anti-necessity / dry-path loci — the paññāvimutta arahant who lacks the formless (and arguably the jhānas), the "sixty bhikkhus" liberated-by-wisdom passages, the susīma-style admissions, the gradation that demands only "a measure" of samādhi for the lower fruits, and the commentarial sukkhavipassaka/upacāra-samādhi machinery that fills a canonical gap. Strategy: don't just count jhāna-mentions; mine the QUALIFIER structure (who is said to attain what WITHOUT what), the negative space (fruits attained where jhāna is conspicuously unmentioned), and the commentarial neologisms that betray a felt canonical gap. A near-absence is as probative as a hit: if the commentaries must coin upacāra-samādhi and sukkhavipassaka, the canon did not supply them — that silence is the finding.
avenues.avenue: MN 70 Kīṭāgiri's seven-persons typology and the 'gradual training, not sudden gnosis' frame — where paññāvimutta and the lower trainees (dhammānusārī, saddhānusārī) are placed relative to jhāna
avenues.rationale: MN 70 ranks seven noble persons; kāyasakkhī ('body-witness') is defined by touching the eight deliverances with the body, while diṭṭhippatta and saddhāvimutta and the two 'followers' (anusārī, the stream-entry-path persons) are defined WITHOUT that clause. The presence/absence of the 'touches the deliverances' clause across the seven is the canon's own gradation of concentration-attainment against fruit-level.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Which of the seven persons are defined as touching the formless deliverances and which are NOT — i.e. the canon's explicit mapping of samādhi-attainment onto noble-person type.
avenues.inference_path: DIFFERENTIAL-DEFINITION INFERENCE. The seven-persons list is a natural experiment: kāyasakkhī and ubhatobhāgavimutta are DEFINED by touching the eight deliverances; diṭṭhippatta, saddhāvimutta, dhammānusārī, saddhānusārī are NOT. Since dhammānusārī/saddhānusārī are the persons on the PATH to stream-entry (the lowest fruit), and their canonical definitions OMIT any deliverance/jhāna clause, the canon defines the lowest noble persons WITHOUT specifying jhāna-attainment. That structured omission is strong evidence the LOWER fruits carry no defined formless (and arguably no defined jhāna) requirement, while the deliverance-touchers are a parallel SAMĀDHI-graded track. Cross-check the COMMENTARY (Ps on MN 70): does it supply jhāna to the diṭṭhippatta/anusārī persons, or affirm they may be sukkhavipassaka? If Ps says diṭṭhippatta CAN be a dry-insight person, the commentary explicitly reads the dry path into the canon's silence here — the cleanest place to catch the gap-filling in the act. Also note MN 70's 'not sudden gnosis but gradual' (anupubba) frame argues the path needs cultivation, tempering any over-quick dry reading.

avenues.avenue: Jhāna-praising vs jhāna-relativizing texts and the anti-formalism strand (no jhāna for the fool, MN 108; jhāna ≠ the 'jhāna' the Buddha praises; the horse-jhāna / khaḷuṅka simile; 'do not be negligent of jhāna, do not contend' MN 19?)
avenues.rationale: MN 108 (Gopaka-Moggallāna) distinguishes the jhāna the Buddha praises from the jhāna he does not (the hindrance-ridden brooding of the unliberated) — using the same root jhāyati pejoratively for obsessive brooding. This complicates 'jhāna is required': the canon itself flags that not all 'jhāna' is the right kind, and that the WORD covers good and bad states.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: That jhāyati/jhāna is semantically split in the canon between the cultivated absorptions and pejorative brooding — affecting how to count and interpret raw jhāna hits.
avenues.inference_path: DISAMBIGUATION-OF-THE-EVIDENCE INFERENCE. This avenue is methodological insurance: any raw count of 'jhāna/jhāyati' hits is contaminated by (a) pejorative brooding (MN 108, the horse who broods 'oats, oats'), (b) the homograph jhāyati 'burns/withers', and (c) compound forms (jhānaṅga, jhānantarika). The inference for the main question: the canon's own awareness that jhāyati can be hindrance-driven brooding shows 'jhāna' is not a monolithic technical term, which slightly loosens the definitional pro-jhāna argument — the Buddha praises a SPECIFIC jhāna (hindrance-free, the four absorptions), not jhāna-as-such. This refines rather than refutes the jhāna camp, and crucially tells us to filter counts. Orthogonal payoff: the GOOD/BAD jhāna split implies the operative criterion is again hindrance-freedom (the praised jhāna is the one without the five), looping back to the threshold-decoupling avenue — the canon may care about hindrance-freedom as the real requirement, with full absorption as its paradigm but not its only instance.

The question is whether the canonical (CST Pāli: nikāya + Vinaya + Abhidhamma + aṭṭhakathā + ṭīkā) record makes the four rūpa-jhānas a NECESSARY condition for any of the four fruits (especially the lower two, stream-entry and once-return), or whether it leaves room for a "dry-insight"/bare-insight (sukkhavipassaka) path. A near-absence is as probative as a hit: if the commentaries must coin upacāra-samādhi and sukkhavipassaka, the canon did not supply them — that silence is the finding.
avenues.avenue: Commentarial/Abhidhammic machinery of the path-moment (magga-citta) as a single mind-moment necessarily accompanied by jhāna-factors — the Abhidhamma argument that EVERY path-moment IS jhānic (so even the dry-insight worker has 'jhāna' at the moment of path)
avenues.rationale: The Abhidhamma/commentary holds that the supramundane path-consciousness (lokuttara-jhāna) always carries jhāna-factors, so even the sukkhavipassaka attains 'first-jhāna-level' path-consciousness at the moment of breakthrough. This is the tradition's reconciliation: dry BEFORE the path-moment, jhānic AT it. The presence of lokuttarajjhāna / maggacitta vocabulary is the marker.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Where the lokuttara-jhāna / path-moment doctrine lives (Abhidhamma + commentary, not nikāya) and how it reconciles the dry path with the jhāna-definition of sammāsamādhi.
avenues.inference_path: RECONCILIATION-LAYER INFERENCE. The lokuttara-jhāna doctrine is the tradition's SOLUTION to the very tension this lens probes: it preserves 'sammāsamādhi = jhāna' (the path-moment is jhānic) WHILE permitting a dry approach (no mundane jhāna needed beforehand). Its STRATIFICATION is the finding: if lokuttarajjhāna/maggacitta/maggakkhana vocabulary is absent from the four nikāyas and present in Abhidhamma+commentary, then the reconciliation is post-canonical — the canon never said the path-moment is a jhāna; the Abhidhamma engineered that to square 'mattaso kārī'/dry-insight reality with the jhāna-definition of right-concentration. This is the keystone inference uniting the whole lens: the canon GRADES samādhi and leaves the floor unquantified; the Abhidhamma+commentary build TWO devices to fill the gap (upacāra-samādhi below the path, lokuttara-jhāna at the path) — and the existence of these two devices is itself the proof that the canonical record left the concentration-requirement underdetermined. Confirming both devices' absence from mūla closes the argument.

The question is whether the canonical (CST Pāli: nikāya + Vinaya + Abhidhamma + aṭṭhakathā + ṭīkā) record makes the four rūpa-jhānas a NECESSARY condition for any of the four fruits (especially the lower two, stream-entry and once-return), or whether it leaves room for a "dry-insight"/bare-insight (sukkhavipassaka) path. The lens reasons from BOTH directions: (a) pro-jhāna loci — sammāsamādhi DEFINED as the four jhānas, the "depending on the first jhāna the destruction of taints" frame, the jhāyatha exhortations, the jhāna→nibbāna path framings; and (b) anti-necessity / dry-path loci — the paññāvimutta arahant who lacks the formless (and arguably the jhānas), the "sixty bhikkhus" liberated-by-wisdom passages, the susīma-style admissions, the gradation that demands only "a measure" of samādhi for the lower fruits, and the commentarial sukkhavipassaka/upacāra-samādhi machinery that fills a canonical gap. A near-absence is as probative as a hit: if the commentaries must coin upacāra-samādhi and sukkhavipassaka, the canon did not supply them — that silence is the finding.
avenues.avenue: Negative/limit texts: practitioners who have jhāna but are NOT liberated (Devadatta, the formless masters Āḷāra/Uddaka, the 'fools' with attainments) — jhāna as NEITHER sufficient NOR, by implication, the differentiator
avenues.rationale: Āḷāra and Uddaka mastered the formless yet the Bodhisatta judged it 'does not lead to disenchantment, dispassion, nibbāna'. Devadatta had jhāna/iddhi yet fell. This shows the canon holds concentration INSUFFICIENT for liberation — wisdom is the differentiator. Bears on the lens by clarifying that the debate is necessity, not sufficiency.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Canonical cases of high attainment without liberation, proving samādhi alone is not the liberating factor.
avenues.inference_path: INSUFFICIENCY→NECESSITY INFERENCE. These texts settle that jhāna/formless is NOT SUFFICIENT (wisdom is always the cutting edge). The probative move for THIS lens is the contrapositive question: if the canon is so emphatic that concentration without wisdom liberates no one, is it equally emphatic that wisdom without (deep) concentration liberates no one? The ASYMMETRY is the finding. Search for any parallel statement that bare wisdom/insight WITHOUT samādhi 'does not lead to nibbāna' — the prediction is that NO such symmetric warning exists (there is no 'vipassanā without samādhi is fruitless' pericope matching the 'samādhi without paññā is fruitless' one). That asymmetry — canon polices concentration-without-wisdom but never wisdom-without-concentration — is indirect evidence the canon weights wisdom as the indispensable factor and treats concentration as instrumental/gradable, consistent with the dry path being tolerable. Orthogonal: Āḷāra/Uddaka also show the canon's authors KNEW deep concentration intimately and still subordinated it — a deliberate doctrinal choice, not ignorance of jhāna.

avenues.lens: The question "how much concentration does the path require, especially for the lower fruits?" is fundamentally a question about a GRADIENT the canon states but never quantifies. The canon gives an ordinal scale (mattaso kari "doer to a measure" → paripuri-kari "fulfiller") keyed to the four fruits, but the quantitative floor below which insight cannot mature is left open. The only way to pin this down by corpus search is to triangulate from MANY indirect angles, because no passage says "X amount of concentration suffices for stream-entry." The strongest levers are: (1) the explicit grading texts (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12, Pp) where the measure-doer ALREADY attains the lower fruits — proving sub-fulfilment concentration suffices for them; (2) the bare-insight cluster (Lens 5) where arahants deny the jhanas/formless/powers entirely — proving even the HIGHEST fruit can be reached without those attainments, which a fortiori collapses the floor for lower fruits; (3) the descriptions of stream-entry's actual requisites (faith, the four sotapattiyanga, dhammanusari/saddhanusari) which conspicuously do NOT list jhana; (4) the precise semantic range of samadhi/ekaggata/cittass'ekaggata vs jhana vs upacara — whether the canon ever uses a graded-but-sub-jhanic concentration vocabulary, or whether "access concentration" is a pure commentarial coinage (0 canon hits would itself be a major finding); (5) the negative space — passages that COULD have demanded jhana for the lower fruits but don't, and the distribution of jhana-attainment across named stream-enterers/once-returners (esp. lay disciples who are explicitly NOT jhana-attainers yet are sotapanna). Inference runs heavily through ABSENCE and CO-OCCURRENCE: if the standard stream-entry pericope never collocates with jhana terms, that silence is evidence; if the measure-doer is defined by the SAME concentration verb-phrase as the fulfiller but graded by adverb (mattaso vs paripuri), the difference is degree not kind; if upacara/khanika-samadhi return zero in the canon but saturate the commentary/tika, the access-concentration solution is datably post-canonical. Orthogonal subjects (deathbed stream-entry, child/animal awakenings, the "in the time it takes to snap the fingers" suttas, the suddenness of some awakenings, the layfollower lists, the jhana-as-pleasant-abiding-here-and-now framing vs jhana-as-path framing) all leak information about whether sustained absorption is on the critical path.
avenues.avenue: The explicit grading texts: the measure-doer (mattaso kari) vs fulfiller (paripuri-kari) in the threefold-training scale keyed to the fruits
avenues.rationale: This is the spine of the whole question. AN 3.86/3.87 (Sekha/Sikkha suttas) and AN 9.12 (Saupadisesa) grade trainees: the one who FULFILLS virtue but is only a 'doer to a measure' (mattaso kari) in concentration and wisdom still attains stream-entry and once-return; only the non-returner fulfils concentration; only the arahant fulfils wisdom. This directly proves sub-fulfilment concentration is sufficient for the two lower fruits.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A hit on 'samadhismim mattaso kari' co-occurring with sotapanna/sakadagami terms in the same passage is the single strongest canonical statement that partial/incomplete concentration suffices for the lower fruits.
avenues.inference_path: The contrast pair mattaso-kari vs paripuri-kari encodes that the difference between a stream-enterer's and a non-returner's concentration is one of DEGREE on a single scale, not presence/absence. If 'paripuri' (fulfilment) is reserved for the non-returner, then the lower-fruit concentration is BY DEFINITION incomplete absorption. Even a near-absence of any numeric measure here confirms the canon gives only an ordinal grade, forcing the quantification question onto inference. Co-occurrence of mattaso with the seven persons (satta puggala) ties the grade to the fruit-ladder.

avenues.avenue: Lens 5 core: the panna-vimutta (wisdom-liberated) arahant who lacks the formless attainments and powers — Susima
avenues.rationale: SN 12.70 (Susima) is the keystone: arahants explicitly DENY the iddhis, the formless/peaceful liberations (santa vimokkha atikkamma rupe aruppa), and being touched-with-the-body kaya-witnesses — yet are liberated. The Buddha's reply 'first the knowledge of the stability of the dhamma, after that the knowledge of nibbana' (pubbe dhammatthiti-nanam, atha nibbane nanam) makes insight the operative cause. If the highest fruit needs no formless attainment, the lower fruits a fortiori need far less.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Hits on 'na ca kho ... atthi imesam dhammanam samapatti' and 'pannavimutta' confirm a canonically-attested class of liberated persons who lack the higher absorptions, demolishing any claim that the full jhana/formless ladder is necessary even for arahantship.
avenues.inference_path: This is an a-fortiori lever: if arahantship (the ceiling) is reachable by panna-vimutta persons who deny the formless attainments, then stream-entry and once-return (the floor) cannot require MORE concentration than arahantship. The denial-formula's exact scope matters: Susima's arahants deny iddhi + formless + kaya-sakkhi but the text is silent on whether they have the FOUR (rupa) jhanas — that silence is itself the live scholarly battleground (Brahmali/Sujato read jhana as implied; Gombrich/Cousins/Polak read it as genuinely absent). Mapping exactly what is denied vs left unsaid pins which reading the Pali licenses.

avenues.avenue: The panna-vimutta vs ceto-vimutta split and the named liberation-types (ubhatobhagavimutta, kaya-sakkhi, ditthippatta, saddhavimutta, dhammanusari, saddhanusari)
avenues.rationale: MN 70 (Kitagiri) and AN 9.44 / SN 48 enumerate the seven (or nine) noble persons. The ubhatobhagavimutta and kaya-sakkhi are DEFINED by contact with the formless 'peaceful liberations'; the panna-vimutta, ditthippatta, saddhavimutta are defined by NOT having them ('na ca kho ... atthi imesam dhammanam samapatti'). Crucially the dhammanusari and saddhanusari (the lowest, who are on the path to stream-entry) are defined by faith/wisdom-following with NO concentration-attainment clause at all.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The exact membership of each of the seven persons, and which ones carry the 'na ... samapatti' (lacks-the-attainments) tag, maps the minimum-concentration floor for each fruit. Saddhanusari/dhammanusari carrying zero jhana requirement is the floor of the floor.
avenues.inference_path: The seven-persons taxonomy is a built-in concentration gradient: it sorts liberated/path persons by HOW MUCH absorption they touched. Three of seven explicitly lack the formless attainments. The two path-attainers (anusari) lack even a stated concentration clause. This is the canon's own answer to 'how little is enough' — it answers by exhibiting persons at each fruit who have minimal absorption. A near-absence of jhana language in the anusari definitions is positive evidence that path-entry's concentration floor is very low.

avenues.avenue: AN 4.87-90 the four kinds of ascetic / samana (samanamacala, samanapundarika, samanapaduma, samanasukhumala) and the gradation of attainment
avenues.rationale: AN 4.87-90 grades four ascetics: the lower ones (the 'unshaken' samana) are non-returners who have NOT touched the eight liberations with the body, while the higher (white-lotus/lotus) HAVE. This is a direct canonical statement that a high fruit (non-return) can be reached WITHOUT the eight liberations / formless contact.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: 'attha vimokkhe kayena phusitva ... na viharati' attached to a non-returner proves the formless attainments are not necessary even for the third fruit, sharpening the floor downward.
avenues.inference_path: If non-return (third fruit) is attested WITHOUT the eight liberations, then the necessity of deep concentration is decoupled from fruit-height across the whole ladder. The gradation among the four ascetics is precisely a gradation of attainment-depth NOT correlated 1:1 with fruit, which is evidence the canon treats concentration-depth as a partly-independent axis from the fruit attained.

avenues.lens: The question "how much concentration does the path require, especially for the lower fruits?" is fundamentally a question about a GRADIENT the canon states but never quantifies. The canon gives an ordinal scale (mattaso kari "doer to a measure" → paripuri-kari "fulfiller") keyed to the four fruits, but the quantitative floor below which insight cannot mature is left open. The only way to pin this down by corpus search is to triangulate from MANY indirect angles, because no passage says "X amount of concentration suffices for stream-entry." The strongest levers are: (1) the explicit grading texts (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12, Pp) where the measure-doer ALREADY attains the lower fruits — proving sub-fulfilment concentration suffices for them; (2) the bare-insight cluster (Lens 5) where arahants deny the jhanas/formless/powers entirely — proving even the HIGHEST fruit can be reached without those attainments, which a fortiori collapses the floor for lower fruits; (3) the descriptions of stream-entry's actual requisites (faith, the four sotapattiyanga, dhammanusari/saddhanusari) which conspicuously do NOT list jhana; (4) the precise semantic range of samadhi/ekaggata/cittass'ekaggata vs jhana vs upacara — whether the canon ever uses a graded-but-sub-jhanic concentration vocabulary, or whether "access concentration" is a pure commentarial coinage (0 canon hits would itself be a major finding); (5) the negative space — passages that COULD have demanded jhana for the lower fruits but don't, and the distribution of jhana-attainment across named stream-enterers/once-returners (esp. lay disciples who are explicitly NOT jhana-attainers yet are sotapanna). Inference runs heavily through ABSENCE and CO-OCCURRENCE: if the standard stream-entry pericope never collocates with jhana terms, that silence is evidence; if the measure-doer is defined by the SAME concentration verb-phrase as the fulfiller but graded by adverb (mattaso vs paripuri), the difference is degree not kind; if upacara/khanika-samadhi return zero in the canon but saturate the commentary/tika, the access-concentration solution is datably post-canonical. Orthogonal subjects (deathbed stream-entry, child/animal awakenings, the "in the time it takes to snap the fingers" suttas, the suddenness of some awakenings, the layfollower lists, the jhana-as-pleasant-abiding-here-and-now framing vs jhana-as-path framing) all leak information about whether sustained absorption is on the critical path.
avenues.avenue: The actual stated requisites of stream-entry: the four sotapattiyanga and the four factors of stream-entry — does jhana ever appear?
avenues.rationale: The canon repeatedly lists what PRODUCES stream-entry (association with good persons, hearing the true Dhamma, wise attention, practice in accordance) and what a stream-enterer POSSESSES (verified confidence in Buddha/Dhamma/Sangha + virtue dear to the noble ones). Jhana is conspicuously absent from BOTH lists.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: If the four sotapattiyanga and the four factors-of-a-stream-enterer never collocate with jhana/samadhi-attainment vocabulary, the omission across dozens of repetitions is strong evidence jhana is not on stream-entry's critical path.
avenues.inference_path: This is the central ARGUMENT-FROM-ABSENCE. The stream-entry pericope is one of the most-repeated formulas in the canon. If a deep-concentration requirement existed, this is exactly where it would be stated. Its systematic absence — replaced by faith, hearing, wise attention, and virtue — is positive evidence the floor is sub-jhanic. The fourth sotapattiyanga (dhammanudhamma-patipatti) is the only practice-slot and it is generic 'practice in accordance', not absorption. A co-occurrence audit (does samadhi EVER appear inside the sotapattiyanga list?) settles it.

avenues.avenue: Is 'access concentration' (upacara-samadhi) and momentary concentration (khanika-samadhi) genuinely zero in the canon? Datable commentarial coinage
avenues.rationale: The commentary fills the canon's quantification-gap with upacara-samadhi (access concentration), a sub-jhanic threshold. The threefold parikamma/upacara/appana scheme and the nimitta progression are Visuddhimagga/Aṭṭhakathā vocabulary. Establishing that these terms are ZERO in the four nikayas + Vinaya + Abhidhamma proper but saturate the Aṭṭhakathā/Ṭīkā dates the 'access concentration' solution as post-canonical.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Zero canonical hits + dense commentarial hits for upacara/khanika/appana/parikamma is a clean stratigraphic result: the dry-insight floor's technical vocabulary is a commentarial invention, not a canonical category. Even ONE canonical hit would overturn the standard claim.
avenues.inference_path: This is inference from PRESENCE/ABSENCE across strata. If the canon never names a sub-jhanic concentration grade, then the canon's own answer to 'how much concentration' is either (a) jhana, or (b) an unnamed lower threshold it never technicalized. The commentary's need to COIN upacara is itself evidence the canon left the floor undefined. Conversely, if appanasamadhi (absorption concentration) also appears only commentarially, then even the jhana=samadhi equation is partly a later systematization. Mapping which of {parikamma, upacara, appana, khanika} appear where dates each layer of the concentration taxonomy.

avenues.avenue: The jhana formula itself: when is jhana presented as the path-factor (samma-samadhi) vs a pleasant-abiding-here-and-now vs the requisite for the lower fruits?
avenues.rationale: Samma-samadhi in the eightfold path is DEFINED as the four jhanas (e.g. SN 45.8, DN 22). This is the strongest pro-jhana lever: if the path REQUIRES right concentration, and right concentration IS the four jhanas, then jhana is on the path. The counter-reading: the path is completed at arahantship, so the jhana-definition of samma-samadhi may describe the FULFILLED path (non-returner/arahant), not the stream-entry threshold. Whether jhana is framed as 'pleasant abiding' (ditthadhammasukhavihara) vs 'the path' matters for how load-bearing it is.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The contexts where the four-jhana formula appears — path-definition vs gradual-training vs pleasant-abiding — reveal whether the canon treats jhana as universally required or as one (high) register of practice. Co-occurrence of the jhana formula with sotapanna/lower-fruit terms (rare) vs with arahant/non-returner terms (common) is decisive.
avenues.inference_path: The pro-jhana case rests almost entirely on the samma-samadhi=four-jhanas equation. But the equation is consistent with jhana being required only for the FULFILLED path (matching mattaso-kari: the stream-enterer has not yet fulfilled samma-samadhi). So the same texts the jhana-camp cites are compatible with the dry-insight floor IF the path-factors mature progressively. Auditing whether the four-jhana formula EVER co-occurs with stream-entry attainment, vs always with full liberation, tests this. A near-absence of jhana+sotapanna co-occurrence supports progressive fulfilment.

avenues.avenue: Sudden / deathbed / on-the-spot awakenings and lay stream-enterers who are not jhana-attainers
avenues.rationale: The 'eye of dhamma arose' (dhammacakkhum udapadi) repeatedly happens AT THE END OF A TALK (desana-pariyosane), on the spot, to listeners with no described jhana practice — including laypeople. The 'snap of the fingers' suttas (AN 1) praise even momentary good states. The Buddha declares many deceased lay followers stream-enterers/once-returners/non-returners (e.g. the Janavasabha / Mahaparinibbana 'mirror of dhamma' lists) without any record of their jhana attainment.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Mass on-the-spot dhammacakkhu attainments and posthumous lay-disciple fruit-declarations, with no jhana in the frame, show the canon routinely grants the lower fruits to non-meditators-in-absorption.
avenues.inference_path: If stream-entry arises mid-discourse to a layperson, there was no opportunity for jhana-development in that moment. The natural reading is that wise attention to a teaching at the moment of hearing suffices — a sub-jhanic floor. The MASS declarations (hundreds of stream-enterers after a talk) make the jhana-for-every-stream-enterer claim statistically implausible unless 'jhana' is redefined down to a momentary unification. This is inference from the NARRATIVE distribution of attainment: who gets the fruit, and what were they doing.

avenues.avenue: Concentration as one of the five faculties/powers and the 'balanced faculties' — can the samadhi-faculty be weak relative to panna?
avenues.rationale: The five faculties (faith, energy, mindfulness, concentration, wisdom) are graded — SN 48 says by the DEGREE/difference of the faculties (indriyanam vemattata) one becomes arahant, non-returner, ... down to dhammanusari/saddhanusari. This is a canonical statement that the SAME faculties at LOWER intensity yield the lower fruits — including a low concentration-faculty.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: 'indriyanam vemattata/nanattam' producing the ladder of noble persons is a direct canonical claim that fruit-height is a function of faculty-INTENSITY, so the lower fruits correspond to lower (weaker) concentration.
avenues.inference_path: SN 48.12-18 explicitly derives the seven noble persons from the degree of completion of the five faculties — 'samattatta paripuratta' (completeness/fulfilment). This MIRRORS the mattaso/paripuri grading. It tells us the canon CONCEIVES of partial concentration producing the lower fruits. The samadhindriya being weak-but-present at stream-entry is exactly 'a measure of concentration.' Also: which faculty leads? If panna/saddha can lead and samadhi lag, that licenses the dry-insight reading. The dhammanusari (wisdom-led) and saddhanusari (faith-led) are named for the LEADING faculty, never the concentration-led — a telling asymmetry.

avenues.avenue: The Abhidhamma / Puggalapannatti definitions — does the scholastic layer quantify the measure or keep it ordinal?
avenues.rationale: Puggalapannatti repeats the mattaso/paripuri grading and DEFINES each noble person by which fetters are abandoned (stream-enterer: 3 fetters; once-returner: 3 + weakening of greed/hatred/delusion; non-returner: 5 lower fetters). The fruit is defined by FETTER-removal, NOT by concentration-attainment. The Abhidhamma keeps the measure ordinal/fetter-based, never numeric.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: If the Abhidhamma defines the fruits purely by fetters abandoned (tini sannojanani / tanutta / pancorambhagiya) and never by a samadhi threshold, then even the systematizing layer declines to put a concentration floor on the lower fruits.
avenues.inference_path: The fetter-model is the canon's actual DIAGNOSTIC for the fruits. It is an insight/defilement metric, not a concentration metric. That the lower fruits are diagnosed by removal of self-view, doubt, and rule-clinging (all cognitive/insight defilements) — with greed/hatred only WEAKENED at once-return and not removed till non-return — tells us the lower-fruit work is primarily insight, with concentration's full role deferred to the non-returner (who removes sensual desire + ill-will, the fetters most tied to absorption). This MAPS the concentration-floor onto the fetter-ladder: deep samadhi correlates with the fifth-fetter (kamacchanda) removal at non-return, exactly where 'paripuri samadhi' is assigned. A near-absence of samadhi in the fetter-definitions confirms the lower floor is low.

avenues.avenue: Hindrance-suppression as the functional definition of 'enough concentration' — the nivaranas and their abandonment
avenues.rationale: The jhana formula opens by 'secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states' — i.e. the five hindrances are suppressed. If 'enough concentration for insight' means 'hindrances temporarily quelled', that threshold can be met at access level (hindrances suppressed) WITHOUT full absorption. The nivarana-abandonment vocabulary is the functional pivot between dry-insight and jhana camps.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon ever describes insight proceeding under merely-suppressed (not absorption-level) hindrances. The phrase 'cetaso upakkilese panne dubbalikarane' (defilements that weaken wisdom) links hindrance-removal to wisdom's functioning.
avenues.inference_path: The whole debate can be recast as: does insight require hindrances ABSENT (jhana) or merely SUPPRESSED/WEAKENED (access)? The canon's repeated linkage of the five hindrances to weakened wisdom (panne dubbalikarane) implies the minimum is 'hindrances quelled enough for wisdom to function' — a functional, not absorption-level, criterion. This is a sub-jhanic floor specified functionally rather than numerically. If the canon describes seeing-clearly the moment hindrances drop (without naming jhana), that is the implicit floor.

Orthogonal subjects (deathbed stream-entry, child/animal awakenings, the "in the time it takes to snap the fingers" suttas, the suddenness of some awakenings, the layfollower lists, the jhana-as-pleasant-abiding-here-and-now framing vs jhana-as-path framing) all leak information about whether sustained absorption is on the critical path.
avenues.avenue: Anapanasati / satipatthana as the insight vehicle — is samadhi instrumental-and-modest or absorptive?
avenues.rationale: In satipatthana/anapanasati the concentration is described with 'ekaggata/samahita/ekodibhava' and 'samadahati cittam' but the SOTERIOLOGICAL move is 'yathabhutam pajanati' (knows as it really is). Whether the unification described is jhanic or merely collected is the crux. The 'samahito yathabhutam pajanati' formula (concentrated, one knows as it really is) is the canon's own statement of the concentration→insight link.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: 'samahito yathabhutam pajanati' shows concentration is INSTRUMENTAL to seeing-as-it-is, but the GRADE of samahito is unspecified — leaving room for a modest floor. Co-occurrence of yathabhutam-pajanati with ekaggata (vs with full jhana) tests whether collectedness or absorption is the stated prerequisite.
avenues.inference_path: The pivotal causal formula 'samahito yathabhutam pajanati' (SN 22.5, etc.) makes concentration the PROXIMATE condition for liberating insight — but uses the generic 'samahito' (collected/concentrated), not 'jhanam samapanno' (absorbed in jhana). If the canon consistently uses the WEAKER concentration term in the insight-causal slot and reserves the jhana-formula for 'pleasant abiding', that lexical choice is evidence the insight-floor is collectedness, not absorption. This is inference from LEXICAL SELECTION in the causal pericope.

avenues.avenue: The samatha/vipassana yoking and the four ways to arahantship (AN 4.170 Yuganaddha) — is samatha-first mandatory?
avenues.rationale: AN 4.170 (Yuganaddha / Ananda) gives FOUR routes to arahantship: samatha-first, vipassana-first, the two yoked, and the dhammuddhacca-viggahita route. The vipassana-pubbangama (insight-leading) route canonically licenses developing insight BEFORE/without prior samatha-cultivation — the textual seed of the dry-insight path.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: 'vipassanapubbangamam samatham bhaveti' canonically attests an insight-first route, with samatha arising AFTER insight. This is direct evidence that deep prior concentration is not mandatory for the path's culmination.
avenues.inference_path: If even arahantship can be reached insight-first (samatha following), then for the LOWER fruits the prior-concentration requirement is weaker still. The four-routes text refuses to privilege samatha as the universal gateway. The dhammuddhacca-route (insight so strong it causes mental agitation about the dhamma) is especially telling: it describes insight RUNNING AHEAD of concentration. Inference: the canon's own typology of paths includes routes where concentration is consequence not precondition — collapsing the necessity claim for the lower fruits.

avenues.avenue: The 'pleasant abiding' vs 'requisite' framing of the jhanas, and jhana praised but not made universally mandatory
avenues.rationale: Several texts call the jhanas 'pleasant abidings in the here-and-now' (ditthadhammasukhavihara) and 'abhicetasika' (belonging to the higher mind) — a register of ATTAINMENT, not a universal gate. Some texts note disciples who are 'not gainers of jhana at will' (na nikamalabhi catunnam jhananam) yet are accomplished. AN 6.61-type and the 'akicchalabhi/akasiralabhi' (gains-without-difficulty) language grades jhana-access as a separate accomplishment.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: If accomplished noble disciples are explicitly noted as NOT gainers-of-jhana-at-will, that proves jhana-mastery is dissociable from noble attainment. The 'pleasant abiding' framing demotes jhana from path-requisite to optional higher comfort.
avenues.inference_path: The 'na nikamalabhi catunnam jhananam' clause (a noble person who is not a gainer of the four jhanas at will) — if attested of stream-enterers/once-returners — is a smoking-gun dissociation of fruit from jhana-mastery. Even the existence of a TERM for 'gainer of jhana at will' as a distinct accomplishment implies many noble persons are NOT such gainers. Inference from the lexicon of jhana-ATTAINMENT (distinct from jhana-as-path): the canon tracks jhana-mastery as an optional excellence, evidence it is not the floor for the lower fruits.

avenues.avenue: Once-return and the weakening (not removal) of greed/hatred — what concentration does THAT specifically require?
avenues.rationale: The once-returner has the three fetters gone PLUS attenuated (tanu) greed/hatred/delusion, but has NOT removed sensual desire/ill-will (the 4th/5th fetters, removed only at non-return). Since deep concentration is most associated with overcoming sensual desire (kamacchanda is the hindrance jhana most directly opposes), the fact that the once-returner has NOT removed kamacchanda implies the once-returner's concentration is sub-jhanic-stable.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: That once-return is defined by mere ATTENUATION (tanutta) of greed/hatred, with sensual desire still present, shows the second fruit does not require the sustained kamacchanda-suppression that jhana entails.
avenues.inference_path: This is the precise diagnostic the question wants for the lower fruits. Jhana's signature is the dropping of sensual desire (vivicceva kamehi). The once-returner still HAS sensual desire (only weakened) — therefore the once-returner has not stabilized the very state jhana embodies. Inference: the once-returner's concentration floor is below jhana, because the fetter jhana removes is still present in him. By the same logic the stream-enterer (greed/hatred fully intact, only the three cognitive fetters gone) needs even less concentration. The fetter-to-hindrance mapping QUANTIFIES the floor ordinally: full kamacchanda-mastery (=jhana) is a NON-RETURNER feature, matching 'paripuri samadhi = anagami.'

avenues.avenue: Negative/orthogonal: texts that DEMAND jhana, to weigh against — the strongest pro-jhana loci
avenues.rationale: Dhammapada 372 ('natthi jhanam apannassa, natthi panna ajhayato' — there is no jhana for one without wisdom, no wisdom for one without jhana) is the strongest single pro-mutual-dependence verse. The 'jhayatha bhikkhave ma pamadattha' exhortations make jhana an imperative. These must be enumerated to fairly weigh the necessity case.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Dhp 372 and the jhana-imperatives establish a strong canonical strand making jhana central. Their distribution (verse/exhortation vs path-definition) and whether they specify a FRUIT calibrate how much they bear on the LOWER fruits specifically.
avenues.inference_path: Honest enumeration must surface the counter-evidence. But note the inference: Dhp 372 asserts mutual dependence of jhana and panna at the TERMINAL state ('one with both jhana and panna is near nibbana', Dhp 372b), describing the ideal/fulfilled practitioner — compatible with the non-returner/arahant having both, and silent on the stream-enterer. The jhana-imperatives are addressed to bhikkhus pursuing the GOAL, not calibrated to stream-entry. So even the strongest pro-jhana texts may speak to the fulfilled path, leaving the lower-fruit floor untouched. Weighing requires checking whether ANY pro-jhana text indexes jhana to sotapatti specifically.

avenues.avenue: Children, animals, and minimal-capacity awakenings — the extreme low end of the capacity distribution
avenues.rationale: The canon records stream-entry in very young persons, the dying, and even (in commentary/Vimanavatthu) a frog-deva who attained stream-entry while listening. Sick-bed teachings (Gilana suttas) bring deathbed listeners to non-return. These extreme cases bound the capacity question: if a dying person or a seven-year-old attains the fruit, sustained jhana cultivation was not the vehicle.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Attestations of fruit-attainment in incapacitated or minimal-training subjects (dying, very young, momentary listeners) show the lower fruits do not require the prolonged samatha cultivation jhana demands.
avenues.inference_path: This is inference from the EXTREMES of the attainment distribution. A deathbed or child stream-entry is a natural experiment: the subject had no runway for jhana mastery, yet got the fruit. If such cases are non-trivial in number, the jhana-necessity claim for the lower fruits fails by counterexample. Even commentarial/Vimanavatthu cases matter as the tradition's own sense of the floor — they would not narrate a frog-deva stream-enterer if the tradition believed jhana were strictly prerequisite. Orthogonal subjects (petas, yakkhas attaining) further stretch the envelope.

avenues.avenue: The faith-follower / dhamma-follower (anusari) as the genuine floor of the path and their guaranteed destiny
avenues.rationale: SN 25 (Okkantasamyutta) defines the dhammanusari and saddhanusari as those who have ENTERED the fixed course of rightness (sammattaniyama) — guaranteed stream-entry — on the basis of mere faith (saddhamatta) or accepting the teachings after pondering (dhammamatta). NO concentration attainment is named. SN 55.24-25 (Saranani) shows even one of 'mere faith and mere affection' (saddhamatta pemamatta) is heaven-bound / path-assured.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The anusari definitions establish the path's ENTRY floor as faith/wisdom acceptance with zero stated concentration requirement, and SN 55 (Saranani the Sakyan, a drinker) shows the bar is shockingly low on the concentration side.
avenues.inference_path: This is the absolute floor of the path. If entering the fixed-course-of-rightness (the irreversible turn toward stream-entry) requires only 'a measure of faith' or 'accepting the teachings after reflection' — with concentration unmentioned — then the concentration floor for path-ENTRY is essentially nil, and stream-entry sits just above it. The Saranani case (SN 55.24, a Sakyan who drank, declared stream-enterer at death) is a canonical hard case the commentary scrambles to explain — its very awkwardness for the tradition is evidence the canon's floor is lower than later orthodoxy wanted. Inference from the tradition's DISCOMFORT (commentarial gymnastics) is itself a signal.

avenues.avenue: Vimuttayatana / liberation-occasions and hearing-as-vehicle — concentration arising AT the moment of insight, not before
avenues.rationale: AN 5.26 (Vimuttayatana) describes five occasions of liberation (hearing/teaching/reciting/pondering/grasping a concentration-sign), where joy→rapture→tranquillity→happiness→concentration arise IN SEQUENCE culminating in liberation. Crucially concentration comes at the END of the cascade triggered by hearing — 'sukhino cittam samadhiyati' (of the happy one the mind becomes concentrated). Concentration is the FRUIT of the insight-process, not its precondition.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The vimuttayatana cascade (hearing → gladness → rapture → tranquillity → happiness → concentration → liberation) shows concentration arising as a LATE term in a process kicked off by cognition/hearing, not a prerequisite cultivated in advance.
avenues.inference_path: This is the natural-causation sequence (also in SN 12.23 upanisa, AN 11.2). It places samadhi DOWNSTREAM of gladness-from-virtue/hearing and UPSTREAM of yathabhutañanadassana. Read forward, it does NOT require pre-existing jhana — the concentration is generated by the gladdening effect of the teaching. Inference: the canon's own causal model of how liberation unfolds makes concentration an emergent middle term, consistent with a low entry-floor: you don't bring jhana TO the process, the process produces sufficient concentration. The phrase 'sukhino cittam samadhiyati' is the engine — happiness conditions concentration, not the reverse.

avenues.lens: The question "how much concentration does the path require, especially for the lower fruits?" is fundamentally a question about a GRADIENT the canon states but never quantifies. The canon gives an ordinal scale (mattaso kari "doer to a measure" → paripuri-kari "fulfiller") keyed to the four fruits, but the quantitative floor below which insight cannot mature is left open. The only way to pin this down by corpus search is to triangulate from MANY indirect angles, because no passage says "X amount of concentration suffices for stream-entry." The strongest levers are: (1) the explicit grading texts (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12, Pp) where the measure-doer ALREADY attains the lower fruits — proving sub-fulfilment concentration suffices for them; (2) the bare-insight cluster (Lens 5) where arahants deny the jhanas/formless/powers entirely — proving even the HIGHEST fruit can be reached without those attainments, which a fortiori collapses the floor for lower fruits; (3) the descriptions of stream-entry's actual requisites (faith, the four sotapattiyanga, dhammanusari/saddhanusari) which conspicuously do NOT list jhana; (4) the precise semantic range of samadhi/ekaggata/cittass'ekaggata vs jhana vs upacara — whether the canon ever uses a graded-but-sub-jhanic concentration vocabulary, or whether "access concentration" is a pure commentarial coinage (0 canon hits would itself be a major finding); (5) the negative space — passages that COULD have demanded jhana for the lower fruits but don't, and the distribution of jhana-attainment across named stream-enterers/once-returners (esp. lay disciples who are explicitly NOT jhana-attainers yet are sotapanna). Inference runs heavily through ABSENCE and CO-OCCURRENCE: if the standard stream-entry pericope never collocates with jhana terms, that silence is evidence; if the measure-doer is defined by the SAME concentration verb-phrase as the fulfiller but graded by adverb (mattaso vs paripuri), the difference is degree not kind; if upacara/khanika-samadhi return zero in the canon but saturate the commentary/tika, the access-concentration solution is datably post-canonical. Orthogonal subjects (deathbed stream-entry, child/animal awakenings, the "in the time it takes to snap the fingers" suttas, the suddenness of some awakenings, the layfollower lists, the jhana-as-pleasant-abiding-here-and-now framing vs jhana-as-path framing) all leak information about whether sustained absorption is on the critical path.
avenues.avenue: The upanisa/transcendental-dependent-origination chain — where samadhi sits relative to knowing-and-seeing
avenues.rationale: SN 12.23 (Upanisa) gives the supramundane chain: ...sukha → samadhi → yathabhutañanadassana → nibbida → viraga → vimutti → khayañana. Samadhi is the PROXIMATE cause of knowledge-and-vision-of-things-as-they-are. This is the canonical statement that SOME concentration is necessary for liberating insight — but the chain says 'samadhi', not 'jhana', and derives it from sukha (from passaddhi from piti from pamojja from saddha).
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: 'samadhi-upaniso yathabhutañanadassana' canonically makes concentration a necessary proximate condition for liberating knowledge — establishing concentration is NOT dispensable, while leaving its GRADE (jhana vs collectedness) unspecified.
avenues.inference_path: This cuts BOTH ways and must be honestly weighed. It refutes a 'zero concentration' caricature: the canon requires SOME samadhi for yathabhutañanadassana. But it specifies neither jhana nor a numeric floor — it derives samadhi naturally from happiness/tranquillity. The pro-jhana camp reads 'samadhi' here as jhana; the dry-insight camp reads it as the access/momentary collectedness that any happy, tranquil mind has. Inference: the floor is 'enough samadhi for the mind to see clearly,' positioned in a natural cascade from gladness — which is attainable below absorption. The chain's derivation of samadhi from sukha (not from kasina-training) supports the modest reading.

avenues.avenue: Orthogonal: the meditator-types debate (jhayi vs dhammayogi), and tension over whether absorption-people or doctrine-people are superior
avenues.rationale: AN 6.46 (Mahacunda) records an actual community TENSION between the jhayi (absorption-meditators) and the dhammayogi (doctrine-specialists), each disparaging the other, with the Buddha (via Mahacunda) validating BOTH. The dhammayogi 'see the deep meaningful matter having penetrated it with wisdom' while the jhayi 'touch the deathless element with the body.' This canonically recognizes a wisdom-led practitioner type distinct from absorption-meditators.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canonical recognition of dhammayogi (wisdom-workers) as a legitimate, liberation-bound type alongside jhayi proves the tradition acknowledged practitioners whose strength was insight, not absorption.
avenues.inference_path: This orthogonal community-sociology text is strong indirect evidence: if the canon must ADJUDICATE a dispute between absorption-people and doctrine-people, both types existed and both reached the goal. The dhammayogi are the institutional ancestors of the dry-insight practitioner. The Buddha's refusal to rank them, and the assignment of 'penetrating the deep with wisdom' to the non-absorption type, licenses a wisdom-dominant path. Inference from the existence of a NAMED rival type: the canon's social world contained accomplished non-jhana-centric practitioners, so the jhana-necessity claim was contested even internally.

avenues.avenue: Kosambi/Musila-Narada (SN 12.68) — knowing dependent-origination 'as it is' without being an arahant or a kaya-witness
avenues.rationale: SN 12.68 (Kosambi) distinguishes Musila (who KNOWS dependent origination and that nibbana is the cessation of becoming, by personal knowledge 'apart from faith') from Narada (who likewise SEES nibbana 'well with right wisdom as it is' but says 'I am not an arahant, I have not touched it with the body' — the well-water simile). Narada has correct liberating insight WITHOUT the body-witness contact (kaya-sakkhi = formless attainment).
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Narada's 'I see nibbana rightly with wisdom but have not touched it with the body, I am not an arahant' is a canonical portrait of advanced insight (at least non-return) PRESENT WITHOUT the formless body-contact — directly separating insight from deep absorption.
avenues.inference_path: The well-water simile (you can SEE the water without being able to touch it bodily) is the canon's own metaphor for insight-ahead-of-absorption. Narada has yathabhuta wisdom of nibbana but not arahant-level body-realization. This is the dry-insight gradient made explicit: knowledge-by-wisdom precedes/exceeds concentration-contact. Inference: if the canon depicts a near-arahant who SEES correctly but lacks body-touch (the formless), then a fortiori the lower fruits do not require body-touch. Paired with Susima (SN 12.70) this forms a two-text canonical core for wisdom-led attainment.

avenues.avenue: Gradual-training sequencing — is jhana INSIDE the gradual path before insight, and at what fruit does insight-knowledge enter?
avenues.rationale: The gradual-training pericope (DN 2, MN 27, etc.) places the four jhanas BEFORE the insight-knowledges ('with mind thus concentrated... he directs it to knowledge of the destruction of the taints'). This is the strongest STRUCTURAL pro-jhana evidence: the standard map puts jhana before liberating insight. But this pericope describes the COMPLETE path to arahantship in one sitting — it is silent on the lower fruits and on whether the sequence is the only route.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The gradual-training sequence (jhana → directed insight → taint-destruction) shows the canonical IDEAL route runs through jhana. Its scope (always terminating in arahantship/taint-destruction, never indexed to stream-entry) shows it maps the FULL path, not the lower-fruit floor.
avenues.inference_path: This is the pro-jhana camp's structural backbone and must be honestly weighed. But the inference cuts finer: the gradual-training always culminates in asavakkhaya (arahantship), never in sotapatti. It describes the COMPLETE path of a renunciant going all the way in one ideal arc. It does NOT claim every stream-enterer traverses jhana first; it describes the fulfilled trajectory. Read with mattaso-kari: the stream-enterer is PARTWAY along this very arc, not yet at the jhana-fulfilment that the non-returner reaches. Inference: the gradual path is the MAXIMAL route; the lower fruits are waypoints on it that can be reached before the jhana-stage is mastered (matching 'samadhismim mattaso kari'). The pericope's invariable arahant-termination is the key tell that it does not set the lower floor.

avenues.avenue: Lexical census of all concentration-grading vocabulary to map the canon's full samadhi taxonomy vs the commentary's
avenues.rationale: A full census of every concentration term and every QUALIFIER the canon attaches (paritta/mahaggata/appamana 'limited/exalted/measureless' in DN 33; animitta/appanihita/suññata 'signless/desireless/empty' concentrations; sammā vs micchā) reveals whether the canon ALREADY has a graded concentration vocabulary distinct from jhana — which would show the canon could specify a floor but chose terms other than jhana for the insight context.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canon's own qualifiers on samadhi (paritta/mahaggata 'limited/exalted', the three insight-concentrations animitta/appanihita/suññata) show a richer concentration taxonomy than jhana-only — and the insight-concentrations (signless/desireless/empty) are concentration MODES aimed at liberation rather than absorptive depth.
avenues.inference_path: If the canon has 'limited concentration' (paritta samadhi) and three liberation-oriented concentrations (animitta/appanihita/suññata-samadhi) that are defined by their OBJECT/aspect (impermanence, non-self, suffering) rather than by absorptive depth, then the canon already conceives concentration that serves insight without being measured on the jhana-depth scale. This is positive evidence the insight-relevant concentration is a MODE not a DEPTH — partially answering 'how much' with 'enough to sustain the signless/desireless/empty contemplation,' which is functionally sub-jhanic. The PRESENCE of paritta-samadhi ('limited concentration') in the canon directly refutes any claim that the canon only knows absorptive concentration.

avenues.avenue: Stream-enterer's actual described practice and the 'mirror of dhamma' / large-scale fruit declarations
avenues.rationale: DN 16 / DN 18 give the 'mirror of dhamma' by which a noble disciple declares his OWN stream-entry — defined entirely by the four aveccappasada (verified confidence) + noble virtue, with no concentration clause — and record MASS posthumous fruit-declarations (e.g. at Nadika, scores of lay followers declared stream-enterers/once-returners/non-returners). The criterion the Buddha gives the disciple to self-certify involves NO jhana.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The Dhammadasa self-certification formula (confidence in the Triple Gem + noble virtue) and mass lay declarations show stream-entry's operational definition omits concentration entirely.
avenues.inference_path: The 'mirror of dhamma' is the canon handing the disciple a CHECKLIST to verify his own stream-entry — and the checklist is faith + virtue, not jhana. If the tradition believed jhana were required, the self-certification mirror would include it. Its absence from the diagnostic is strong evidence jhana is not constitutive of the fruit. The mass declarations (hundreds of lay stream-enterers, many householders not living a jhana-cultivation lifestyle) make the universal-jhana claim demographically implausible. Inference from the DIAGNOSTIC's content and the demographic SCALE of attainment.

avenues.avenue: Commentarial stratum: where exactly upacara/sukkhavipassaka/dry-insight is defined, to date the floor-filling move
avenues.rationale: The commentary (Visuddhimagga, Aṭṭhakathā, Abhidhammatthasangaha) coins sukkhavipassaka ('dry-insight worker'), suddhavipassanayanika ('pure-insight vehicle'), and the doctrine of LOKUTTARA-JHANA / magga-jhana — the idea that at the path-MOMENT even the dry-insight worker has a momentary path-jhana (so technically everyone has 'first-jhana' factors at the magga-citta). This commentarial move RECONCILES dry-insight with the jhana-requirement by relocating the jhana to the path-instant.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Where 'sukkhavipassaka' and 'maggajjhana/lokuttarajjhana' are defined reveals the commentary's solution to the very question: it grants a dry-insight path to the fruits while asserting a momentary path-jhana at the magga-moment, satisfying the samma-samadhi=jhana equation technically.
avenues.inference_path: This is the crucial STRATIGRAPHIC inference. The commentary's invention of (a) upacara-samadhi as the insight-floor and (b) lokuttara/magga-jhana at the path-moment is precisely the tradition SOLVING the canon's underspecification. The existence of these coinages is evidence the canon LEFT A GAP that needed filling. By dating sukkhavipassaka and maggajjhana to the commentary (zero canon), we show: the dry-insight path is a commentarial CONCESSION that the lower fruits (and even arahantship) can be reached on minimal mundane concentration, with the jhana-requirement preserved only as a technical path-moment formality. The Abhidhamma's pancangika/maggajjhana doctrine is the formal machinery. This both VALIDATES dry-insight as the tradition's own settled position AND shows the canon never demanded the absorptions for the lower fruits.

The canon gives an ordinal scale (mattaso kari "doer to a measure" → paripuri-kari "fulfiller") keyed to the four fruits, but the quantitative floor below which insight cannot mature is left open. The only way to pin this down by corpus search is to triangulate from MANY indirect angles, because no passage says "X amount of concentration suffices for stream-entry." The strongest levers are: (1) the explicit grading texts (AN 3.86/87, AN 9.12, Pp) where the measure-doer ALREADY attains the lower fruits — proving sub-fulfilment concentration suffices for them; (2) the bare-insight cluster (Lens 5) where arahants deny the jhanas/formless/powers entirely — proving even the HIGHEST fruit can be reached without those attainments, which a fortiori collapses the floor for lower fruits; (3) the descriptions of stream-entry's actual requisites (faith, the four sotapattiyanga, dhammanusari/saddhanusari) which conspicuously do NOT list jhana; (4) the precise semantic range of samadhi/ekaggata/cittass'ekaggata vs jhana vs upacara — whether the canon ever uses a graded-but-sub-jhanic concentration vocabulary, or whether "access concentration" is a pure commentarial coinage (0 canon hits would itself be a major finding); (5) the negative space — passages that COULD have demanded jhana for the lower fruits but don't, and the distribution of jhana-attainment across named stream-enterers/once-returners (esp. lay disciples who are explicitly NOT jhana-attainers yet are sotapanna). Inference runs heavily through ABSENCE and CO-OCCURRENCE: if the standard stream-entry pericope never collocates with jhana terms, that silence is evidence; if the measure-doer is defined by the SAME concentration verb-phrase as the fulfiller but graded by adverb (mattaso vs paripuri), the difference is degree not kind; if upacara/khanika-samadhi return zero in the canon but saturate the commentary/tika, the access-concentration solution is datably post-canonical. Orthogonal subjects (deathbed stream-entry, child/animal awakenings, the "in the time it takes to snap the fingers" suttas, the suddenness of some awakenings, the layfollower lists, the jhana-as-pleasant-abiding-here-and-now framing vs jhana-as-path framing) all leak information about whether sustained absorption is on the critical path.
avenues.avenue: Iddhipada / the development pace and 'samadhi as faculty led by which of chanda/viriya/citta/vimamsa' — wisdom-dominant samadhi
avenues.rationale: The four iddhipada describe concentration ACQUIRED BY a dominant factor — including vimamsa-samadhi (concentration-through-investigation) — i.e. a concentration that is generated and led by investigative WISDOM (vimamsa/dhammavicaya). This is canonical warrant for a wisdom-dominant concentration, the structural basis of the insight-led path.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: 'vimamsa-samadhi-padhanasankhara' (concentration-due-to-investigation as a basis of spiritual power) shows the canon recognizes concentration ARISING FROM investigation — wisdom producing concentration, not concentration preceding wisdom.
avenues.inference_path: The vimamsa-iddhipada is a sleeper lever: it canonically licenses concentration that is a FUNCTION of investigation. This inverts the 'concentration-first' picture and matches the vipassana-pubbangama route. Inference: the canon's own taxonomy of how concentration arises includes an investigation-led mode, so a practitioner whose strength is dhammavicaya generates the requisite samadhi THROUGH insight-work. This means the insight-practitioner is not 'lacking concentration' but generating a wisdom-flavored concentration — dissolving the dichotomy and lowering the prior-absorption requirement for the path.

avenues.lens: Lens 6 — Access / momentary / preparatory concentration and its canonical near-equivalents. The task: locate where the commentarial vocabulary (upacara, khanika, parikamma, appana) enters the corpus, prove its zero-or-near-zero footprint in the mula canon, and — crucially — map the CANONICAL phenomena that the commentary later reifies as "access concentration." The canon grades concentration by GRADIENT (mattaso kari = partial-doer, the stream-enterer/once-returner; paripuri-kari = fulfiller, the non-returner) but never NAMES a sub-jhanic plateau. So the inferential target is twofold: (1) the absence-signature — if upacara/khanika are commentary-only, the sub-jhanic floor is an exegetical construct, not a canonical category, which directly bears on the dry-insight debate; and (2) the positive canonical alternatives — object-defined concentrations (animitta/appanihita/sunnata cetosamadhi, the 'concentration without a theme'), cittekaggata as a universal cetasika (one-pointedness present in EVERY mind-moment per Abhidhamma, which is the Abhidhammic seed of 'momentary concentration'), and samadhi that is graded by what-it-rests-on rather than how-deep-it-goes. A hit on the commentarial terms in a mula row, a co-occurrence of cittekaggata with non-jhanic contexts, or the distribution of animitta-samadhi across strata each lets me infer how much of 'access concentration' the canon authorizes versus how much the Visuddhimagga back-projected.
avenues.avenue: Locate the commentarial concentration-tier vocabulary (the three/four-fold samadhi scheme) and date its entry by stratum
avenues.rationale: These four terms (parikamma = preparatory, upacara = access, appana = absorption; khanika = momentary) are the commentary's load-bearing scheme. The canon supposedly has none of them as samadhi-grades. Finding the EXACT stratum where each first appears (Patisambhidamagga? Vimuttimagga-adjacent? Nettippakarana? Petakopadesa? pure Atthakatha/Tika?) tells me whether 'access concentration' has any canonical foothold or is wholly post-canonical.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A hit in a mula row (especially a Sutta-pitaka row, not Abhidhamma) would be a near-fatal counterexample to the 'access concentration is commentary-only' claim. A hit confined to Atthakatha/Tika rows confirms the construct is exegetical. A hit in Patisambhidamagga/Nettippakarana (canonical-but-late) would locate the bridge. The position=1 volume-headers and citation fields let me bucket by stratum.
avenues.inference_path: If upacara/parikamma/appana appear ZERO times in Sutta+Vinaya mula and only in Abhidhamma-commentary + Atthakatha, then the sub-jhanic 'floor' the path requires is a category the canon never asserts — strengthening the dry-insight reading that the canon only ever names the four jhanas and bare cittekaggata. If even one early-canonical row carries upacara in a samadhi sense, the construct has a seed and the debate shifts. Near-absence is itself the finding: count = 0 in mula is the headline.

avenues.avenue: Map cittekaggata / cittassekaggata (one-pointedness) OUTSIDE the four-jhana formula — the canonical phenomenon access-concentration glosses
avenues.rationale: Cittekaggata is the precise canonical word the commentary equates with 'the concentration present in access.' In the Abhidhamma it is a universal cetasika (present in every citta, even sense-sphere ones) — that is literally 'momentary concentration' in seed form. Finding cittekaggata predicated of non-jhanic states (walking, listening to Dhamma, sila contexts, the moment of stream-entry) shows the canon DOES recognize unification below jhana, which is what khanika-samadhi later formalizes.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Co-occurrence of ekaggata/samahita with contexts that are explicitly NOT the jhana-pericope (e.g. samahite citte yathabhutam pajanati — the 'concentrated mind sees as it is' formula, which appears attached to insight, not necessarily to fourth-jhana) shows graded unification the canon names directly. The 'samahite citte... yathabhutam' formula is the single most important locus: it asserts that SOME concentration enables insight without specifying jhana.
avenues.inference_path: If the canon attaches 'samahite citte' (concentrated mind) to seeing-as-it-is in many rows WITHOUT the four-jhana stock-phrase preceding it, that is positive evidence the canon recognizes an insight-enabling concentration that is depth-unspecified — which is exactly the conceptual space both 'access concentration' (commentary) and 'the jhana requirement is real but the word samadhi is broader' (Brahmali) fight over. The Abhidhamma's universal-cetasika status of ekaggata lets me infer the canon already had 'every mind has some unification,' so 'momentary concentration' is a redescription, not an invention.

avenues.avenue: The three object-defined liberations/concentrations: animitta, appanihita, sunnata cetosamadhi — concentration graded by THEME not DEPTH
avenues.rationale: These are the canon's own NON-jhanic (or jhana-orthogonal) concentration categories — defined by their object/absence-of-object (signless, undirected, empty) rather than by a depth-ladder. They show the canon classifies samadhi by theme. This is the strongest canonical alternative to the commentary's depth-ladder: 'concentration without a theme' (animitta cetosamadhi) is named, has loci, and is not one of the four jhanas.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Distribution across strata. If animitta/appanihita/sunnata cetosamadhi cluster in specific suttas (SN 41.7 Godatta, MN 43 Mahavedalla, SN 40 Moggallana, the Patisambhidamagga) and the threefold scheme is canonical, it proves the canon's own taxonomy of samadhi is object-first. A near-absence in early strata with bloom in Abhidhamma/Patis would show even THIS taxonomy is partly systematized late.
avenues.inference_path: Whatever concentration the canon recognizes that is NOT a jhana is the answer to the lens. If the signless/undirected/empty concentrations are robustly early-canonical, the canon plainly has non-jhanic samadhi categories — so the question 'does the path require jhana' is malformed: the canon requires SOME samadhi and names several kinds. If they are late, the depth-ladder vs theme-ladder both turn out to be systematizations of a looser early usage.

avenues.avenue: The 'samahite citte yathabhutam pajanati' transitional formula and its variants (the proximate-cause-of-insight clause)
avenues.rationale: The canonical causal chain (gladness→rapture→tranquillity→bliss→samadhi→seeing-as-it-is, e.g. the upanisa/transcendental-dependent-origination series SN 12.23, AN 11.2) places samadhi as proximate cause of yathabhuta-nana WITHOUT necessarily routing through the four jhanas. The word the chain uses is just 'samadhi' / 'cittam samadhiyati,' not 'jhana.' This is the textual heart of the Brahmali-vs-Polak fight.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: How often the canon names samadhi (not jhana) as the insight-enabler, and whether the jhana-pericope is interpolated where 'samadhi' alone is stated. Co-occurrence counts of 'cittam samadhiyati' with the upanisa-series vs. with the four-jhana stock phrase quantify how depth-committed the canon's insight-concentration actually is.
avenues.inference_path: If the proximate-cause-of-insight is repeatedly 'samadhi' as a bare term embedded in a natural-causation series (sukha → samadhi → yathabhuta), and the four-jhana formula is a SEPARATE template that the path-gradual-training splices in elsewhere, then the canon underdetermines depth: it requires samadhi-as-condition, leaving the door for both jhanic and sub-jhanic readings. The commentary closes that door downward (upacara suffices); Brahmali closes it at jhana. Counting which template carries 'samadhi → insight' tells me which reading the raw text leans toward.

avenues.avenue: The mattaso-kari / paripuri-kari gradient itself and the sila/samadhi/panna fraction language (the canon's only explicit 'measure')
avenues.rationale: AN 3.86/3.87, AN 9.12 (Saupadisesa), Puggalapannatti carry the ONLY explicit quantification language the canon offers: the stream-enterer/once-returner is a 'partial doer' (mattaso/padesa-kari) in concentration; the non-returner fulfills it. This is the gradient the whole question hangs on. Mapping every occurrence of mattaso-kari and padesa-kari pins exactly what the canon says about the lower fruits' concentration requirement.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether 'partial in samadhi' is ever cashed out in any concrete way (a number of jhanas, a named lesser state) or left purely relative. If across all occurrences mattaso-kari is NEVER paired with a named sub-state, the canon's silence is total and the commentary's upacara is pure gap-filling. If it IS once paired with, say, 'the first jhana' for the lower fruits, that quantifies the floor.
avenues.inference_path: This is the question's exact textual hinge. If mattaso-kari is consistently relational (less than the non-returner) and never absolute (X jhanas), then the canon gives a gradient with no floor — meaning the lower fruits' concentration requirement is UNDERSPECIFIED in the canon, and any floor (jhana for Brahmali, access for the commentary, bare unification for dry-insight) is interpretive. The fact that the SAME suttas say the partial-doer in samadhi STILL attains stream-entry/once-return is itself the strongest canonical evidence that full jhana is NOT required for the lower two fruits.

avenues.avenue: Bare-insight / dry-visioned arahant vocabulary (sukkhavipassaka, paññavimutti, the Susima problem)
avenues.rationale: SN 12.70 (Susima) is the canonical crux for dry-insight: arahants who declare they are pannavimutta (released-by-wisdom), explicitly NOT possessing the formless attainments / eight liberations 'touched with the body,' yet liberated. If the canon names a liberation route that bypasses the deep attainments, that bounds how much concentration the highest fruit needs — and a fortiori the lower fruits.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether pannavimutta is defined by ABSENCE of the higher attainments (the Susima reading) or merely by emphasis. The exact wording 'na kho pana atthavimokkhe kayena phusitva viharanti' (they do not dwell having touched the eight liberations with the body) is the load-bearing clause. Its presence quantifies a liberation-without-formless-jhana category.
avenues.inference_path: If the canon explicitly admits arahants lacking the eight liberations / formless attainments (pannavimutta per Susima), then the MAXIMUM concentration the path requires is bounded below the formless realm — and since the lower fruits require less than arahantship, the floor for stream-entry is lower still. This makes the commentary's upacara-for-insight a plausible downward extrapolation OF a canonical pattern (liberation with less-than-maximal samadhi), even if the specific term is uncanonical. A near-absence of any 'jhana-less arahant' would instead support Brahmali.

avenues.avenue: Stream-entry's stated prerequisites — the sotapatti factors and whether samadhi/jhana appears among them
avenues.rationale: The four sotapattiyanga (association with good people, hearing the true Dhamma, wise attention, practice in accordance) are the canon's explicit recipe for stream-entry — and notably samadhi/jhana is NOT listed among them. The arising of the 'dust-free, stainless Dhamma-eye' (whatever arises is subject to cessation) is the moment of stream-entry, and it follows a graduated talk (anupubbikatha), not a jhana attainment, in dozens of narratives.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: If the dhamma-cakkhu arises repeatedly during/after a talk (sitting listening), without any jhana-pericope, that is mass evidence stream-entry's stated proximate condition is wise attention + hearing, not absorption. The absence of jhana from the four sotapattiyanga is a structural argument. The verb 'samadhiyati' or 'ekaggata' may appear in the listening frame — that would be the 'access' the commentary names.
avenues.inference_path: If across the stream-entry narratives jhana never figures and the listed factors are cognitive/social (hear, attend wisely), then the canon makes the FIRST fruit require essentially no formal jhana — only enough composure to follow a graduated talk to its turning. That composure (cittekaggata in the listening moment) IS the phenomenon upacara/khanika-samadhi later names. So a near-absence of jhana in sotapatti contexts, plus a presence of ekaggata/pasanna-citta, jointly infers that 'access concentration' is the commentary's label for a real but un-named canonical state — vindicating the substance while questioning the term.

avenues.avenue: Concentration attached to listening / serene-faith moments (pasada, pasanna-citta, the composure of the hearer)
avenues.rationale: Before the dhamma-eye arises, the canon describes the hearer's mind becoming 'ready, malleable, free of hindrances, uplifted, serene' (kallacitta, muducitta, vinivaranacitta, udaggacitta, pasannacitta) — a stock phrase. This is a NAMED canonical state of suitable composure that is NOT a jhana, occurring at the threshold of stream-entry. It is the best candidate for what 'access concentration' actually denotes in the suttas.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The full stock phrase 'kallacitto muducitto vinivaranacitto udaggacitto pasannacitto' and its distribution. If it reliably precedes dhammacakkhu/abhisamaya, the canon has a named pre-realization composure-state. Co-occurrence with 'vinivarana' (hindrance-free) is key — hindrance-suppression is the commentary's defining mark of upacara-samadhi.
avenues.inference_path: If the canonical threshold state is 'hindrance-free, malleable, serene mind' (vinivarana-citta) rather than 'first jhana,' then the canon already describes hindrance-suppression as the relevant condition — and hindrance-suppression WITHOUT the jhana-factors being named is precisely the commentary's definition of access concentration. So this stock phrase is the strongest positive evidence that upacara-samadhi reifies a genuine sutta phenomenon. A finding here flips the question from 'invented term' to 'named-state-given-a-late-label.'

avenues.avenue: Hindrance-suppression as the operative threshold (nivarana absence) decoupled from jhana-attainment
avenues.rationale: The gradual-training sequence has 'purifies the mind from the hindrances' (nivaranehi cittam parisodheti) as a discrete step BEFORE the first jhana. So the canon explicitly recognizes a hindrance-purified mind that is not-yet-jhana. The commentary's upacara-samadhi is defined exactly as the zone where hindrances are suppressed but absorption not yet attained.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether 'nivaranehi cittam parisodheti' is a state-with-standing or a mere transition. If the canon ever describes insight or attainment from the hindrance-purified-but-pre-jhana position, that is canonical access-concentration in all but name. The commentarial term 'vikkhambhana-pahana' (suppression-abandonment) vs canonical 'parisodheti' bridges them.
avenues.inference_path: The gradual-training places mind-purification-from-hindrances as a NAMED stage between sense-restraint and first jhana. That stage IS the access zone. Its canonical existence (as a step, even if not a samadhi-grade) means the commentary did not invent the territory — it named a waypoint the canon already lists. Conversely if the canon NEVER treats that waypoint as a place one can stop and do insight (always immediately proceeds to jhana), then the commentary innovated by making it a stable plateau. Counting whether anything happens AT that step vs only THROUGH it is the decisive inference.

avenues.lens: Lens 6 — Access / momentary / preparatory concentration and its canonical near-equivalents. The task: locate where the commentarial vocabulary (upacara, khanika, parikamma, appana) enters the corpus, prove its zero-or-near-zero footprint in the mula canon, and — crucially — map the CANONICAL phenomena that the commentary later reifies as "access concentration." The canon grades concentration by GRADIENT (mattaso kari = partial-doer, the stream-enterer/once-returner; paripuri-kari = fulfiller, the non-returner) but never NAMES a sub-jhanic plateau.
