avenues.avenue: The seven-noble-persons typology (Kitagiri MN 70) — the canon's explicit cross of concentration-attainment against fruit
avenues.rationale: MN 70 Kitagiri and SN 48 / AN classify the seven persons partly BY whether they touch the formless 'peaceful liberations' (santa vimokkha) with the body. The kayasakkhi touches them and has SOME asavas destroyed; the paññavimutta does NOT touch them but is fully released. This is the canon directly correlating depth-of-concentration to type-of-attainer.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The exact text where high concentration (formless attainments) is decoupled from final release (paññavimutta needs none); where the lower attainers (dhammanusari/saddhanusari = the stream-entry-bound) are characterized — and whether ANY concentration depth is stipulated for them.
avenues.inference_path: The dhammanusari/saddhanusari (the two who are 'practising for the realization of the fruit of stream-entry') are defined by faith/wisdom faculties, NOT by a concentration attainment, in MN 70. That near-silence on samadhi for the stream-entry-bound is the single most direct canonical evidence that the lower path does NOT stipulate a jhana floor — the typology grades people by what they DON'T need (formless) and what they DO have (sufficient faith/wisdom + 'composed enough' mind). A hit confirming samadhi is unstipulated for the two 'anusari' types lets you infer the canon's lower-fruit concentration requirement is, by omission, only the generic 'samahita' floor — exactly the gap the commentary plugs with upacara.

avenues.avenue: samahito yathabhutam pajanati — the standard 'concentrated → sees as it is' causal formula and its un-quantified subject
avenues.rationale: SN 56.1 (Samadhi Sutta), SN 22.5, the transcendental-dependent-origination chain (SN 12.23 upanisa: samadhi→yathabhutañanadassana) all assert concentration is the proximate cause of seeing-as-it-is — but NONE quantify which/how-much concentration. This is THE causal hinge and THE silence.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Every instance of the samadhi→insight causal formula and whether the samadhi term is ever modified by a jhana-name or degree-word in these specific causal statements.
avenues.inference_path: If the causal formula invariantly says merely 'samahito' (composed) and never 'pathamajjhanam samapanno yathabhutam pajanati' (having attained first jhana, sees as it is), the canon causally requires only composure, not jhana, for insight. The commentary's move is to gloss this 'samahito' AS minimally upacara. The inference: the canon sets the cause at the GENUS level (composure) and is silent on the SPECIES (which depth); both the jhana camp and the dry camp are filling a real lexical gap, and the honest finding is that the canon underdetermines the answer — the measure is 'sufficient composure for yathabhuta to occur,' an outcome-defined floor.

avenues.avenue: Negative/threshold framings — hindrance-suppression (nivarana) and 'mind freed from the five' as the real operative minimum
avenues.rationale: The gradual-instruction success state — 'kallacitta muducitta vinivaranacitta udaggacitta pasannacitta' (ready, malleable, hindrance-free, uplifted, confident) — is the actual described mental condition at the moment the four truths are taught and the dhamma-eye opens (Upali, Yasa, the standard conversion pericope). This is hindrance-ABSENCE, not jhana-PRESENCE.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon's stated precondition for the stream-entry insight is jhana-attainment or merely hindrance-suppression + malleability; the full conversion-pericope vocabulary.
avenues.inference_path: This is possibly the strongest single avenue. If the dhamma-eye consistently opens to a 'hindrance-free, malleable, ready' mind described WITHOUT a jhana label, the canon's lower-fruit concentration requirement is functionally 'hindrances suspended' — which is EXACTLY what the commentary later names upacara-samadhi (access concentration is defined as hindrance-suppression short of absorption). The inference: upacara is not a fabrication but a NAMING of a state the canon already describes phenomenologically without a term. So the answer to 'how much concentration for stream-entry' is, canonically, 'enough to drop the five hindrances and become malleable' — sub-jhanic by the commentary's own definition, and textually grounded. This dissolves the polemic: the canon DOES set a floor, just not by the word jhana.

A near-absence is itself evidence: if the gradient vocabulary clusters on sila and panna but the samadhi axis stays binary (jhana attained / not), that asymmetry tells us the canon conceived concentration-for-stream-entry as a threshold, not a dial — or vice versa.
avenues.avenue: Orthogonal: degree-vocabulary in NON-meditative domains (generosity, virtue, gift-analysis) to calibrate where the canon DOES quantify
avenues.rationale: A control sample: the canon DOES quantify in gift-analysis (MN 142 Dakkhinavibhanga ranks gifts by recipient yielding graded fruit), in 'moderation in eating' (bhojane mattaññuta — knowing the measure), in virtue (silasampada/silavipatti). Seeing where measure-language IS deployed sharpens the significance of its absence on samadhi.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: That the canon is perfectly willing to grade fruit by measured input in some domains — so its refusal to grade insight-fruit by measured concentration is a CHOICE, not a general aversion to quantification.
avenues.inference_path: If gift/virtue/eating are quantified ('to this extent, this fruit') but concentration-for-awakening is not, the asymmetry is informative: it suggests the canon views concentration's contribution as not linearly dose-responsive toward awakening (you don't get 'more stream-entry' from more jhana) — consistent with a threshold-then-sufficient model rather than a dial. The orthogonal control thus strengthens the 'measure is functional/threshold, not quantified-dose' reading and explains WHY the canon leaves mattaso bare: more samadhi past sufficiency doesn't buy more of the lower fruit. (bhojane mattaññuta is also a lexical bridge: 'matta' as right-measure / knowing-the-amount — the same root as mattaso — showing 'measure' in the canon means appropriate-sufficiency, not a counted quantity.)

avenues.lens: LENS 8 — the vocabulary of degree, measure, and partiality applied to mind-states: the genus of "limited samadhi" (samadhismim mattaso kari). The canon grades mental cultivation as MORE vs LESS along several lexical axes — padesa/nippadesa (partial/complete), paritta/appamana/mahaggata (limited/measureless/exalted), olarika/sukhuma (gross/subtle), paripunna/aparipunna (fulfilled/unfulfilled), the "-mattaso kari / paripuri-kari" doer-fulfiller scale, the yavata/yavataka "to whatever extent" formulas, anupubba (graduated), appamattaka (trifling), and the comparative/superlative morphology (-tara/-tama, seyya/settha, hina/panita). The wager: the canon NEVER quantifies the measure of concentration for the lower fruits, so the answer must be triangulated from (a) the lexical scaffolding it DOES use to talk about quantity/degree of a mental quality, (b) where that scaffolding is and is NOT attached to samadhi/jhana, and (c) the commentarial layer (upacara, appana, parikamma) that retrofits a sub-jhanic floor onto a canon that grades the trainee only as 'partial'. A near-absence is itself evidence: if the gradient vocabulary clusters on sila and panna but the samadhi axis stays binary (jhana attained / not), that asymmetry tells us the canon conceived concentration-for-stream-entry as a threshold, not a dial — or vice versa.
avenues.avenue: Orthogonal: the 'as much as needed / yavadeva' instrumental-sufficiency formulas across the corpus
avenues.rationale: The satipatthana refrain 'patissati mattaya' (just enough mindfulness for knowing) and 'yavadeva ñanamattaya' (merely to the extent of knowledge) encode INSTRUMENTAL sufficiency — as much as the function needs, no more. The jhana-mastery formula 'nikamalabhi akicchalabhi akasiralabhi' (one who gains at will, without trouble, without difficulty) grades MASTERY of attainment separately from attainment itself.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The canon's idiom for 'as much as the task requires' (sufficiency-language) vs 'mastery/facility' language (nikamalabhi); whether the non-returner/arahant is marked by MASTERY (akicchalabhi) of jhana while the lower fruits are not.
avenues.inference_path: If jhana-MASTERY (nikamalabhi/akicchalabhi) is predicated of the higher attainers (cf. the non-returner as samadhi-paripuri) but the lower fruits carry only sufficiency-idioms (yavadeva, mattaya), then the canon distinguishes ENOUGH-to-see (lower) from MASTERED-as-abiding (higher) — and 'samadhismim mattaso kari' aligns with the sufficiency pole. Inference: the measure for stream-entry is 'as much concentration as the act of seeing requires' (yavadeva ñanamattaya applied to samadhi), and the non-returner's 'fulfillment' is the added MASTERY/abiding, not a different insight. This reconciles the gradient (AN 3.86) with the silence: the difference between mattaso and paripura is mastery-and-abiding, not the presence/absence of jhana per se.

avenues.avenue: Commentarial layer: the upacara/appana/parikamma/khanika scaffold and how it glosses the canon's degree-terms
avenues.rationale: The commentary (Visuddhimagga, the atthakathas/tikas in the corpus) is where upacara/appana/khanika are defined and where AN 3.86's 'mattaso' and 'padesa' get glossed. The question explicitly notes upacara = 0 in canon. Reading how the COMMENTARY fills the gap (and whether it equates mattaso WITH upacara) is essential to dating the floor-concept.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the commentary glosses 'samadhismim mattaso kari' as upacara-samadhi specifically; the commentarial definitions of the access/absorption/momentary tiers; whether khanika-samadhi is used to authorize dry insight.
avenues.inference_path: If the atthakatha on AN 3.86 (Manorathapurani) glosses mattaso-in-samadhi as upacara/access, that is the smoking-gun retrofit: the commentary names the canon's unnamed 'composed-enough' floor as access-concentration, converting a functional silence into a technical tier. Whether the commentary AUTHORIZES dry insight depends on khanika-samadhi's role — if Visuddhimagga/tika lets momentary concentration carry vipassana (the Mahasi reading) vs insists on at least upacara, you can date and locate the dry-path license precisely to a commentarial sub-stratum. The inference closes the loop: the canon grades (mattaso/paripura) without numbers; the commentary supplies the numbered tiers (parikamma<upacara<appana) and pins mattaso to upacara — so the 'access concentration' answer is real but is a commentarial GLOSS on a canonical functional floor (hindrance-free/malleable), not a canonical doctrine.

avenues.avenue: The padesa/nippadesa and 'partial vs complete' fulfillment in the bojjhanga/maggangani development
avenues.rationale: Padesa (partial/local) vs the complete (nippadesa/aparisesa/sakala) is the abstract degree-axis the question names first. Whether a path-factor or the path itself is held 'partially' (padesa) by lower attainers vs 'completely' (samannagata — fully endowed) by higher ones structures the lower/higher fruit distinction.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Where 'partial' is predicated of path-attainment or path-factors; whether the stream-enterer is 'samannagata' (fully endowed) with the eightfold path or holds it partially — and whether samma-samadhi specifically is the partially-held limb.
avenues.inference_path: Counter-evidence check: the stream-enterer is often called 'ariyena atthangikena maggena samannagato' (endowed with the noble eightfold path) — if FULLY endowed, that includes samma-samadhi=jhana, which would push AGAINST the low-samadhi reading and toward Brahmali (stream-enterers have right concentration = jhana, at least nascently). The resolution must reconcile 'samannagata with the eightfold path' (DN/SN stream-entry factors) WITH 'mattaso in samadhi' (AN 3.86). The likely inference: 'samannagata' = possesses the path-factor as RIGHT-DIRECTED (lokiya/mundane samma-samadhi, even momentary), while 'paripuri' = brings it to full (jhanic) development — i.e. one can be endowed-with yet not-fulfiller-of. This distinction (samannagata vs paripura) is itself the canon's degree-grammar for concentration and may be the single cleanest answer to the question. Search samannagata vs paripura co-occurrence to test it.

avenues.avenue: Abhidhamma / Puggalapannatti micro-analysis of the degree-terms (the 'doer to a measure' person-type defined formally)
avenues.rationale: Puggalapannatti formally DEFINES the person-types by these very degree-terms, and the Atthasalini/Vibhanga commentary on cittavisuddhi (purification of mind = upacara+appana in Vism's seven-purifications) is where 'how much concentration' gets its scholastic answer. The Abhidhamma's universal-ekaggata doctrine also bears directly.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The formal Abhidhamma definition of 'mattaso-kari in samadhi' and the seven-purifications placement of cittavisuddhi (does it require jhana or admit upacara?); whether Abhidhamma's every-citta-has-ekaggata licenses a minimal-concentration insight.
avenues.inference_path: Cittavisuddhi (mind-purification, the 2nd of the seven purifications, the concentration stage before insight-purifications begin) is defined in Vism as upacara + appana samadhi — explicitly admitting ACCESS concentration as a sufficient base to proceed to insight. If the corpus's Visuddhimagga/Atthasalini text confirms cittavisuddhi = upacara-OR-appana, then the SCHOLASTIC tradition itself sanctions sub-jhanic (access) concentration as the floor for insight — the dry-insight path is commentarially licensed at the cittavisuddhi definition, not invented by modern teachers. Inference: the modern 'dry insight' camp is recovering a Visuddhimagga-sanctioned option (upacara suffices for cittavisuddhi), while the canon underneath specifies only 'composed/hindrance-free' — so the historical answer is a three-layer stack: canon (functional floor, unnamed) → Abhidhamma/Vism (upacara named as sufficient for cittavisuddhi) → modern (khanika pushed even lower). Pinning each layer's term-set is the deliverable.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: The graded-training measure formula itself (sīla/samādhi/paññā paripūrakārī vs mattaso kārī) — find every locus and every grammatical variant of the partial-vs-full grading.
avenues.rationale: The whole question turns on this single graded formula (AN 3.86/3.87, AN 9.12). Enumerating every occurrence and every inflection of mattaso/paripura pins down exactly where the canon assigns the partial measure to which fruit, and whether the gradient is ever quantified. Compounds like padesa-kari / ekadesa may be commentarial paraphrases of mattaso.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Each hit locates a stratum (canon vs Abhidhamma vs commentary) where the partial-measure grading appears; clustering reveals whether the quantification is ever attempted or always left as a bare comparative.
avenues.inference_path: If mattaso/paripura appear ONLY in the AN/Puggalapaññatti graded-fruit pericope and never with a numeric or jhāna-count gloss in the canon, that near-absence is itself the finding: the canon gives a gradient without a floor. If the commentary (ṭīkā) reaches for padesa/ekadesa or a jhāna-count to gloss mattaso, the quantification is an exegetical import, dating the 'access concentration' move.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: The fruit-by-fruit attainer roster: which puggala gets which measure (stream-enterer & once-returner = partial samādhi; non-returner = full samādhi; arahant = full paññā).
avenues.rationale: AN 3.86/3.87 maps the partial samādhi specifically onto sotāpanna+sakadāgāmī and full samādhi onto anāgāmī. Finding all roster passages tests whether this mapping is stable or whether other texts grade the fruits by samādhi differently.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Co-occurrence of a fruit-term with mattaso vs paripura, or with a samādhi/jhāna term, fixes how much concentration each fruit is said to need.
avenues.inference_path: If sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī co-occur with samādhi only via the partial-measure formula and never with a jhāna attainment claim, the canon supports a low concentration floor for the lower fruits. If the non-returner is consistently the first 'fulfiller' in samādhi, that is the canon's own answer that full (jhānic) concentration is non-essential below non-return — the strongest textual datum for the dry-insight side regarding the LOWER fruits.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: The Susīma sutta (SN 12.70) and its paññāvimutta-without-jhāna core — and the cross-recensional asymmetry with SĀ 347.
avenues.rationale: Susīma is the linchpin: paññāvimutta arahants declare they do NOT attain the formless/eight liberations 'by the body', i.e. arahantship without the deepest samādhi attainments. The Chinese SĀ 347 parallel reportedly lacks the explicit jhāna-denial, making the Pāli phrase a possible local development.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The exact Pāli of the jhāna/vimokkha-denial clause, the dhammaṭṭhiti-ñāṇa-before-nibbāna-ñāṇa sequence, and whether the denial is canon-internal or commentarially softened.
avenues.inference_path: If the Pāli carries an explicit 'we do not attain the formless liberations' and the SĀ parallel does not, the asymmetry suggests the dry-insight (paññāvimutta-without-jhāna) reading is at least a recension-specific Theravāda articulation — cuts BOTH ways: it strengthens the canonical Pāli basis for a low floor while flagging it as possibly not pan-Buddhist. The dhammaṭṭhiti-then-nibbāna ordering (insight-knowledge precedes nibbāna-knowledge, no jhāna interposed) is independent canon-internal evidence that paññā can mature without the samādhi attainments.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: The paññāvimutta / ubhatobhāgavimutta / kāyasakkhī / diṭṭhippatta / saddhāvimutta seven-noble-persons typology (MN 70 Kīṭāgiri, AN 9.44, Puggalapaññatti) — the canon's own map of how much samādhi each liberated type carries.
avenues.rationale: This typology is the canon's explicit answer to 'who needs the formless attainments and who does not.' The ubhatobhāgavimutta touches the formless liberations 'with the body'; the paññāvimutta does not. Kāyasakkhī attains them but is not yet arahant. This grades samādhi against liberation directly.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The definitional clauses distinguishing types by whether they attain 'the peaceful formless liberations by the body', which is the canon's own concentration-floor differentiator.
avenues.inference_path: If the paññāvimutta is defined precisely by NOT attaining the formless liberations yet BEING arahant, the canon itself certifies arahantship without the highest samādhi — the lower fruits a fortiori need no more. The question of whether paññāvimutta still requires the four rūpa-jhānas (vs no jhāna at all) hinges on whether the definition denies only the ARŪPA liberations or jhāna generally; the exact scope of 'santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā' is the decisive clause.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Kathāvatthu controversies bearing directly on the concentration floor — momentary vs sustained concentration, whether an arahant can lack jhāna, sudden penetration, fruit-by-stages.
avenues.rationale: The Kathāvatthu records exactly the inter-school disputes that the modern jhāna-vs-dry-insight debate revives: whether comprehension (abhisamaya) is gradual or in-one-moment, whether the path-moment is momentary, what role jhāna plays. These are the earliest Abhidhamma-era articulations of the floor question.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Which schools held insight could be momentary/sudden (anupubbābhisamaya rejected) vs gradual — a proxy for whether sustained jhānic concentration was deemed necessary.
avenues.inference_path: If the Theravāda Kathāvatthu argues AGAINST anupubbābhisamaya (gradual penetration) and FOR ekābhisamaya (penetration of the four truths in one moment), that one-moment path-consciousness model is compatible with — even presupposes — a momentary/access concentration rather than sustained absorption, which is the Abhidhammic seed of upacāra-samādhi. Absence of any Kathāvatthu defense of 'arahant must have jhāna' would show the school did not treat full jhāna as a definitional requirement.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Paṭisambhidāmagga and the Abhidhamma-Piṭaka treatment of samādhi types and the path — the bridge text where canonical Abhidhamma may already gradate sub-jhānic concentration.
avenues.rationale: Paṭisambhidāmagga's Yuganaddhakathā is the canonical-adjacent text that systematizes the four ways samatha and vipassanā can be yoked (samatha-first, vipassanā-first, yoked, dhamma-uddhacca). It is the strongest near-canonical candidate for a vipassanā-led / minimal-samatha path.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether a vipassanā-pubbaṅgama (insight-first) mode is explicitly licensed, and what minimal concentration it presupposes; whether khaṇika-cittekaggatā / upacāra terms appear pre-commentarially.
avenues.inference_path: If Paṭisambhidāmagga's yuganaddha scheme licenses an insight-led path and is itself canonical (Khuddaka), the dry-insight option has a near-canonical footing predating Buddhaghosa — undercutting the claim that bare-insight is purely commentarial. Conversely, if even there every mode terminates in a samatha attainment, that bounds the floor upward. The first appearance of upacāra/appanā vocabulary in this stratum dates the access-concentration concept.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Direct hunt for the commentarial access/absorption vocabulary across strata to date the upacāra-samādhi construct and measure its canonical absence.
avenues.rationale: The CLAUDE.md notes upacāra-samādhi has 0 canon hits. Confirming the stratum-distribution of upacāra/appanā/parikamma/khaṇika-samādhi across canon vs aṭṭhakathā vs ṭīkā is the single most direct test of whether the access-concentration floor is exegetical.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The earliest stratum in which each term appears and its frequency curve from canon → Abhidhamma → aṭṭhakathā → ṭīkā.
avenues.inference_path: A clean zero in canon and Abhidhamma-piṭaka with a bloom in the aṭṭhakathā confirms upacāra-samādhi as a commentarial gap-filler — meaning the canon's 'measure' was quantified only later. If even khaṇika-samādhi (momentary concentration, the dry-insight vehicle in Visuddhimagga) is post-canonical, then BOTH the access-concentration AND the momentary-concentration floors are commentarial constructs, leaving the canon with only the bare mattaso gradient — the cleanest possible statement of the scholarly gap.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Jhāna as a path-factor: sammāsamādhi defined AS the four jhānas (SN 45.8, DN 22) versus passages where right concentration is defined otherwise — testing whether the canon hard-wires jhāna into the path.
avenues.rationale: If the canonical DEFINITION of the eighth path-factor is the four jhānas, that is the heavyweight argument (Brahmali/Sujato) that the path structurally requires jhāna. But variant definitions (e.g. sammāsamādhi as cittassekaggatā with the other seven factors as requisites, AN/MN passages) would loosen it.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Every formulation of sammāsamādhi and whether any defines it sub-jhānically (e.g. as mere one-pointedness, or as 'noble right concentration with its supports').
avenues.inference_path: If sammāsamādhi is ALWAYS the four-jhāna formula in the canon, the path-factor argument for a jhānic floor is strong — but note it would apply to the FULL path/arahantship, not necessarily to stream-entry, which the four-factors-of-stream-entry (sotāpattiyaṅga) define WITHOUT jhāna. The contrast between the jhāna-defined eightfold path and the jhāna-free sotāpattiyaṅga is itself the inference: the lower fruits are gated by faith/sīla/right-view, not by samādhi attainment.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Four factors of stream-entry (sotāpattiyaṅga) and the stream-entry causal sequence — what the canon actually requires to enter the stream, and whether samādhi/jhāna figures.
avenues.rationale: The canon gives an explicit recipe for stream-entry: associate with good persons, hear the true Dhamma, wise attention, practice in accordance. Jhāna is conspicuously absent. The four 'limbs of a stream-enterer' are faith + virtue, not concentration attainment.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether any stream-entry causal list includes jhāna/samādhi-attainment as a precondition, or whether it is governed by hearing + yoniso-manasikāra + right view.
avenues.inference_path: The NEAR-ABSENCE of jhāna in every sotāpattiyaṅga / stream-entry-causation list is a powerful inference for a low concentration floor at the lower fruits: stream-entry is repeatedly caused by hearing-and-wise-attention, an insight-and-faith event, not a samādhi attainment. This is orthogonal evidence (it never mentions the floor) that nonetheless bounds it: whatever samādhi is needed is the incidental concentration of attentive listening, consistent with the 'partial measure' grading.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Sudden-awakening-while-listening narratives — laypeople and others attaining stream-entry (even higher) on the spot during a discourse, with no prior jhāna training.
avenues.rationale: Dozens of conversion stories (Upāli, Yasa, Bāhiya, the graduated talk recipients) show stream-entry/once-return/even arahantship arising during or immediately after a talk. These are the canon's empirical data points on the minimum concentration for the lower fruits.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The mental-readiness vocabulary (kallacitta, muducitta, vinīvaraṇacitta — pliant, unhindered mind) that precedes the dhamma-eye, and the absence of a jhāna prerequisite in the narrative.
avenues.inference_path: If the dhamma-eye repeatedly opens after only the graduated discourse — i.e., after the mind is merely made 'pliant, malleable, free of hindrances' (vinīvaraṇacitta), a state short of jhāna — the canon's own conversion data set the lower-fruit floor at hindrance-suppression / momentary collectedness, NOT absorption. The phrase vinīvaraṇacitta is key: it is hindrance-FREE (the functional core of access concentration) but the texts do not call it jhāna, supporting the access-but-not-absorption reading for the lower fruits while denying the term itself is canonical.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: AN 9.12 Saupādisesa and the nine-fold grading of those who die with residue — the fullest canonical gradient that places mattaso explicitly.
avenues.rationale: AN 9.12 is the locus that simultaneously (a) lists nine grades of awakened persons dying with residue and (b) gives the sīla-full / samādhi-partial / paññā-partial grading. It is the densest single node for the question.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The exact phrasing tying each grade to the partial/full measures, and any internal gloss on what 'partial in concentration' means operationally.
avenues.inference_path: The structure 'full in sīla, partial in samādhi and paññā → still attains a fruit (up to non-return depending on type)' directly entails that the lower fruits do NOT require fulfilled (jhānic) concentration. The placement of the FIRST samādhi-fulfiller at the non-returner level is the canon's own quantification of the gradient — the closest the canon comes to a floor, and it sits ABOVE the lower fruits, certifying them as low-samādhi.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Vibhaṅga (Abhidhamma) Jhānavibhaṅga / Maggaṅgavibhaṅga / Sikkhāpadavibhaṅga treatment of samādhi and the path — does canonical Abhidhamma define right concentration sub-jhānically or always jhānically?
avenues.rationale: The Vibhaṅga's Suttanta-bhājanīya vs Abhidhamma-bhājanīya often differ: the suttanta half may keep the four-jhāna definition while the Abhidhamma half reduces right concentration to the cetasika ekaggatā present in any wholesome consciousness — including a single insight-moment.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the Abhidhamma-bhājanīya defines sammāsamādhi/samādhindriya as one-pointedness-cetasika (present sub-jhānically) rather than as the jhāna-attainment, and the bare synonyms (ṭhiti, avikkhepa, avisāhāra) it uses.
avenues.inference_path: If the Vibhaṅga's Abhidhamma-bhājanīya reduces right concentration to cittassekaggatā — a cetasika present in every path-moment regardless of jhāna depth — then canonical Abhidhamma itself supplies the technical basis for momentary/path-moment concentration as 'right concentration,' i.e. the seed of khaṇika-samādhi sufficing for the path. This is the Abhidhamma-internal route to a low floor, distinct from the upacāra route, and it is PRE-commentarial — a strong datum that the bare-insight position is not merely Buddhaghosa's invention.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Samatha/vipassanā pairing passages and the four explicit 'paths to arahantship' (AN 4.170 Yuganaddha) — the canon's typology of how the two yokes combine, including vipassanā-first.
avenues.rationale: AN 4.170 explicitly lists vipassanā-preceded-by-samatha, samatha-preceded-by-vipassanā, the two yoked, and the dhamma-excitement case. The vipassanā-first mode is the canonical hook for an insight-led path with concentration arising afterward.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon licenses developing insight BEFORE/with minimal samatha and whether the magga then arises — and what concentration that mode presupposes.
avenues.inference_path: The mere canonical existence of a vipassanā-pubbaṅgama mode shows the canon does not mandate samatha-first jhāna for the path. But note AN 4.170 says all four modes culminate in the path 'and the fetters are abandoned' — it does not say the vipassanā-first practitioner LACKS jhāna at the path-moment, only that samatha did not lead. So a careful reading bounds the inference: insight may LEAD, but the canon is silent on whether concentration is absent at the decisive moment — exactly the gap the commentary fills with khaṇika-samādhi. The silence here is the precise location of the unanswered floor.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Recension/Āgama-comparative loci beyond Susīma — Madhyama/Saṃyukta/Ekottarika parallels to the graded-training, the seven-persons typology, and the jhāna-as-path-factor texts.
avenues.rationale: Northern Abhidharma (Sarvāstivāda Abhidharmakośa, Yogācārabhūmi-Śrāvakabhūmi) developed its OWN sub-jhānic access concentration, anāgamya-samādhi, and the darśana-mārga (path of seeing) as momentary. These are independent cross-recensional developments answering the same gap.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the access-concentration / momentary-seeing-path constructs arose independently in the Northern traditions (anāgamya, dhyānāntara, kṣaṇika abhisamaya) — though these terms are Sanskrit, the corpus is CST Pali so English-field and transliteration hits are the access route.
avenues.inference_path: If the Sarvāstivāda independently posited anāgamya-samādhi (a sub-first-jhāna concentration sufficient for the path of seeing), the convergence of TWO traditions on a sub-jhānic floor for the breakthrough is strong evidence that the early shared substrate left the measure under-determined and each school filled it with an access-level construct. The Pāli corpus will carry these only as loanword/English-note hits, so a near-absence in Pali with presence in cross-references confirms the construct is a school-specific overlay on a common gap. The SĀ-347-lacks-the-denial datum generalizes: the jhāna-denial in Susīma is plausibly a Theravāda sharpening, so the Pāli is MORE explicit about dry-insight than the common stock — meaning the dry-insight reading is well-grounded in Pāli specifically even if not provably pre-sectarian.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Hindrance-suppression as the functional floor — nīvaraṇa removal language, which is the operational content of 'access' without the word.
avenues.rationale: The canon's recurring micro-sequence pāmojja→pīti→passaddhi→sukha→samādhi, and the 'having abandoned the five hindrances' clause, describe a collected, hindrance-free mind that is the functional substrate later named upacāra — without ever calling it jhāna.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: How often the breakthrough/insight is staged on hindrance-removal-and-gladdening rather than on a named jhāna; the strength of the pāmojja-to-samādhi cascade as the actual prerequisite.
avenues.inference_path: If insight/penetration is repeatedly preceded by hindrance-removal + the gladness cascade culminating in 'the concentrated mind sees things as they are' (samāhito yathābhūtaṃ pajānāti), the canon is describing the FUNCTION of access concentration (hindrance-free collectedness) as the floor, while declining to specify a jhāna count. This reconciles both camps: a real concentration floor exists (hindrances must be down) but it is not necessarily absorption — and it is exactly this functional, unnamed level that the commentary later reifies as upacāra. The samāhito-yathābhūtaṃ-pajānāti formula (AN 11.2 etc.) is the canon's own statement that concentration is causally necessary for liberating insight, which BOUNDS the dry-insight claim: zero concentration is canonically excluded even if jhāna is not mandated.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Puggalapaññatti's own definitions of the relevant persons (mattaso-kārī grades, the seven noble persons, the samādhi-bhāvanā fourfold) as the Abhidhamma-piṭaka's systematization.
avenues.rationale: Puggalapaññatti restates the AN graded-training persons in Abhidhamma register and also carries the fourfold samādhi-bhāvanā (AN 4.41). It is the canonical Abhidhamma checkpoint on whether the partial-measure grading is preserved verbatim or refined.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the Abhidhamma-piṭaka keeps the bare mattaso/paripura grading unquantified (matching the suttas) or adds any operational gloss, and whether it ties samādhi-bhāvanā purposes to the fruits.
avenues.inference_path: If Puggalapaññatti reproduces the partial-measure grading WITHOUT adding a jhāna-count or access-gloss, that confirms the canonical Abhidhamma layer ALSO leaves the measure unquantified — pushing the quantification entirely into the aṭṭhakathā. That stratigraphic result (canon silent, Abhidhamma-piṭaka silent, aṭṭhakathā specifies upacāra) is the decisive chronology for the whole debate: the 'access concentration' answer is demonstrably the latest layer.

avenues.lens: Lens 9 — cross-recensional (Āgama/SĀ parallels, esp. the Susīma jhāna-denial asymmetry) and Abhidhamma/exegetical-system angles (Puggalapaññatti, Vibhaṅga, Kathāvatthu, Paṭisambhidāmagga) on the trainee's concentration-measure (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) versus the fulfiller (paripūrakārī).
avenues.avenue: Indriya-balancing and the faculties as the canon's substitute axis for 'how much concentration' — samādhindriya among the five faculties, and the faith-vs-wisdom-led temperaments.
avenues.rationale: The five-faculties model lets concentration be ONE of five balanced powers, opening a temperament-relative answer: a wisdom-led (dhammānusārī) practitioner leans on paññindriya, a faith-led on saddhindriya, with samādhindriya correspondingly variable. This is the canon's own mechanism for variable concentration floors.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Whether the canon explicitly licenses different faculty-balances reaching the same fruit, implying no fixed samādhi quantum.
avenues.inference_path: If saddhānusārī/dhammānusārī (the two who ENTER the path, pre-stream-entry) are graded by faith or wisdom faculty rather than concentration, the canon is saying the entry to the path is gated by faith or wisdom, with concentration as a balancing requisite rather than a threshold. That makes 'how much concentration' a temperament-relative quantity the canon deliberately leaves variable — which is itself the answer: there is no single floor; there is a balanced-faculties condition. This orthogonal faculties-frame may resolve the debate better than the binary jhāna-yes/no it is usually posed in.

avenues.lens: LENS 10 — How the commentary (aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā) itself fills the canonical gap: the gloss targets where the post-canonical layer specifies what the trainee's "limited measure" of concentration (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) actually IS, and whether it supplies a sub-jhānic floor (upacāra/khaṇika/vipassanā/magga/phala samādhi) for the lower-path attainer. The whole evidential value here is that the canon gives a gradient but no quantum; the commentary is the layer that DOES name a quantum, so every commentarial gloss on the measure-phrase, on the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī's concentration, and on the four named sub-jhānic concentrations is direct evidence of how the tradition resolved the underdetermination — and a near-absence (e.g. if upacāra never co-occurs with the trainee-grading pericope) is itself a finding that the "access concentration" floor is a systematic-theology import rather than an exegesis of the specific canonical phrase.
avenues.avenue: Direct gloss of the measure-phrase 'samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī' / 'sīlasmiṃ mattaso kārī' in the AN 3.86-88 and AN 9.12 commentaries (Manorathapūraṇī) and the parallel Puggalapaññatti commentary
avenues.rationale: This is the single phrase the canon uses to grade the trainee's concentration short of fulfilment. The Manorathapūraṇī (AN-a) on 3.86/3.87/3.88 and on AN 9.12, plus the Puggalapaññatti-aṭṭhakathā, are the precise loci where the commentator must say what 'a measure' means. Whatever quantity the tradition assigns — a fraction of jhāna, only the lower jhānas, only access — appears here or nowhere.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A gloss that equates the trainee's 'measure' with the lower two jhānas (or first jhāna), or with upacāra, or with 'not the full eight attainments', directly answers how much concentration the lower fruits require in the tradition's own voice.
avenues.inference_path: If the gloss reads the measure as 'lower jhānas but not the higher / not the formless' (a jhāna-internal partition), that is evidence the commentary still kept the floor INSIDE jhāna for the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī and reserved fulness for the formless/non-returner — supporting the jhāna-required camp. If instead it explicitly names upacāra-samādhi here, that is the smoking gun that the sub-jhānic floor was read INTO this very phrase. A near-absence (the AN-a glosses the phrase only morphologically, 'mattaso = padesena', without naming any attainment) is itself a finding: the canon's silence was NOT filled at this locus, and the upacāra solution lives elsewhere in the system, imported.

avenues.lens: LENS 10 — How the commentary (aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā) itself fills the canonical gap: the gloss targets where the post-canonical layer specifies what the trainee's "limited measure" of concentration (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) actually IS, and whether it supplies a sub-jhānic floor (upacāra/khaṇika/vipassanā/magga/phala samādhi) for the lower-path attainer. The whole evidential value here is that the canon gives a gradient but no quantum; the commentary is the layer that DOES name a quantum, so every commentarial gloss on the measure-phrase, on the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī's concentration, and on the four named sub-jhānic concentrations is direct evidence of how the tradition resolved the underdetermination — and a near-absence (e.g. if upacāra never co-occurs with the trainee-grading pericope) is itself a finding that the "access concentration" floor is a systematic-theology import rather than an exegesis of the specific canonical phrase.
avenues.avenue: The commentarial definition and scope of upacāra-samādhi (access concentration) and its sibling appanā-samādhi (absorption) — where Buddhaghosa draws the line and whether the sotāpanna is placed below it
avenues.rationale: Upacāra is the commentary's flagship sub-jhānic category and has zero canonical attestation (per the question's own framing). Mapping exactly where Vism (and Vism-ṭīkā Paramatthamañjūsā) sets its lower and upper bounds — between parikamma and appanā, after nīvaraṇa-suppression but before the paṭibhāga-nimitta stabilises into jhāna — tells us the granularity of the floor the tradition could have assigned to the lower-path attainer.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The precise threshold conditions of upacāra (hindrances suppressed, counterpart-sign arisen, but absorption not yet attained) show what sub-jhānic state the commentary had available to assign as the minimum for insight.
avenues.inference_path: If upacāra is defined as already requiring full nīvaraṇa-suppression and a stabilised paṭibhāga-nimitta, then even the commentary's 'minimal' floor is substantial — arguing against a truly 'dry' bare-insight reading even within the commentarial system. If the texts that DISCUSS upacāra never tie it to sotāpatti-magga, then the access-floor is a meditation-manual construct that was never formally wired to the lower fruits, and the dry-insight派 must rest on khaṇika instead. The co-occurrence (or not) of 'upacara' with 'sotapatti'/'maggakkhana' in the same passages is the load-bearing test.

avenues.lens: LENS 10 — How the commentary (aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā) itself fills the canonical gap: the gloss targets where the post-canonical layer specifies what the trainee's "limited measure" of concentration (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) actually IS, and whether it supplies a sub-jhānic floor (upacāra/khaṇika/vipassanā/magga/phala samādhi) for the lower-path attainer. The whole evidential value here is that the canon gives a gradient but no quantum; the commentary is the layer that DOES name a quantum, so every commentarial gloss on the measure-phrase, on the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī's concentration, and on the four named sub-jhānic concentrations is direct evidence of how the tradition resolved the underdetermination — and a near-absence (e.g. if upacāra never co-occurs with the trainee-grading pericope) is itself a finding that the "access concentration" floor is a systematic-theology import rather than an exegesis of the specific canonical phrase.
avenues.avenue: Khaṇika-samādhi (momentary concentration) as the vipassanā-yānika's floor — the explicit commentarial device that lets insight proceed without jhāna
avenues.rationale: Khaṇika-samādhi is the commentary's name for the concentration that arises moment-to-moment on changing objects during bare insight. It is the textual hinge of the entire 'dry-insight' (sukkhavipassaka) reading. Whether and where the aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā actually use the term, and whether they grant it the power to suppress hindrances 'like upacāra', decides whether the canon's underdetermined measure was resolved in the dry direction at all.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: Any locus stating that khaṇika-samādhi can hold the hindrances at bay during vipassanā (the famous Vism/ṭīkā move that it 'does the work of upacāra') shows the tradition engineered a legitimate sub-upacāra floor for the lower path.
avenues.inference_path: If khaṇika-samādhi is well attested and explicitly equated in function to upacāra for hindrance-suppression, the commentary itself authorises a dry path — and the lower fruits' concentration-requirement, in the tradition's voice, can be momentary. If the term is RARE or LATE (clusters only in ṭīkā, not aṭṭhakathā), that dates the dry-insight resolution to the sub-commentarial stratum, meaning even the commentary proper still assumed jhāna/upacāra — a stratification finding directly relevant to the Cousins/Gombrich 'late innovation' thesis.

avenues.lens: LENS 10 — How the commentary (aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā) itself fills the canonical gap: the gloss targets where the post-canonical layer specifies what the trainee's "limited measure" of concentration (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) actually IS, and whether it supplies a sub-jhānic floor (upacāra/khaṇika/vipassanā/magga/phala samādhi) for the lower-path attainer. The whole evidential value here is that the canon gives a gradient but no quantum; the commentary is the layer that DOES name a quantum, so every commentarial gloss on the measure-phrase, on the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī's concentration, and on the four named sub-jhānic concentrations is direct evidence of how the tradition resolved the underdetermination — and a near-absence (e.g. if upacāra never co-occurs with the trainee-grading pericope) is itself a finding that the "access concentration" floor is a systematic-theology import rather than an exegesis of the specific canonical phrase.
avenues.avenue: Vipassanā-samādhi as a named category and its relation to the eightfold path's sammā-samādhi (which the canon defines AS the four jhānas)
avenues.rationale: The canon equates sammā-samādhi with the four jhānas (DN 22, SN 45.8). If the commentary needs a path-factor concentration for the jhāna-less insight-worker, it must either (a) call jhāna-less concentration 'vipassanā-samādhi' and still count it as sammā-samādhi, or (b) finesse the jhāna-definition. Where it does this is exactly where the gap is being papered over.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A commentarial statement that the path-factor sammā-samādhi is 'fulfilled' by vipassanā/khaṇika concentration for the dry worker shows the tradition re-reading the canon's jhāna-definition down to a sub-jhānic floor.
avenues.inference_path: If 'vipassanasamadhi' barely occurs but 'sammasamadhi = cattari jhanani' is restated verbatim in the commentaries, that shows the commentary did NOT lower the path-factor definition — the jhāna-floor was preserved at the doctrinal level even while khaṇika was allowed operationally. That tension (operational dry path vs. doctrinal jhāna path-factor) is itself the most important finding the lens can produce: the gap was filled inconsistently, which is why the modern debate exists.

avenues.lens: LENS 10 — How the commentary (aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā) itself fills the canonical gap: the gloss targets where the post-canonical layer specifies what the trainee's "limited measure" of concentration (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) actually IS, and whether it supplies a sub-jhānic floor (upacāra/khaṇika/vipassanā/magga/phala samādhi) for the lower-path attainer. The whole evidential value here is that the canon gives a gradient but no quantum; the commentary is the layer that DOES name a quantum, so every commentarial gloss on the measure-phrase, on the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī's concentration, and on the four named sub-jhānic concentrations is direct evidence of how the tradition resolved the underdetermination — and a near-absence (e.g. if upacāra never co-occurs with the trainee-grading pericope) is itself a finding that the "access concentration" floor is a systematic-theology import rather than an exegesis of the specific canonical phrase.
avenues.avenue: Magga-samādhi and phala-samādhi (path-moment and fruit-moment concentration) — the Abhidhamma/commentarial claim that the path-moment itself contains jhāna-strength concentration regardless of prior practice
avenues.rationale: A crucial commentarial move (rooted in Abhidhamma — Dhammasaṅgaṇī's jhāna-factor analysis of the supramundane path-citta, and Vism XXI-XXII) is that the supramundane magga-citta is always 'first-jhāna-strength or higher' by definition, even for a dry-insight worker. This means the commentary can grant that the sotāpanna had no prior mundane jhāna yet still insist his path-MOMENT carries jhānic concentration. This is the deepest place the gap is resolved.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A statement that every magga-citta (including sotāpatti-magga) is accompanied by lokuttara-jhāna at minimum first-jhāna strength shows the tradition's reconciliation: dry beforehand, jhānic at the instant of breakthrough.
avenues.inference_path: If magga-samādhi is universally first-jhāna-or-higher in the path-moment, then BOTH camps are partly right in the commentary's eyes — the path requires jhāna (at the moment) but not as a prerequisite discipline. This dissolves the modern binary and shows the canon's 'measure' refers to the PREPARATORY concentration only. Conversely, if the lokuttara-jhāna doctrine is confined to Abhidhamma/Vism and never reaches the AN-a gloss on the trainee, the reconciliation is a systematic add-on not anchored to the grading pericope — again a stratification finding.

avenues.lens: LENS 10 — How the commentary (aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā) itself fills the canonical gap: the gloss targets where the post-canonical layer specifies what the trainee's "limited measure" of concentration (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) actually IS, and whether it supplies a sub-jhānic floor (upacāra/khaṇika/vipassanā/magga/phala samādhi) for the lower-path attainer. The whole evidential value here is that the canon gives a gradient but no quantum; the commentary is the layer that DOES name a quantum, so every commentarial gloss on the measure-phrase, on the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī's concentration, and on the four named sub-jhānic concentrations is direct evidence of how the tradition resolved the underdetermination — and a near-absence (e.g. if upacāra never co-occurs with the trainee-grading pericope) is itself a finding that the "access concentration" floor is a systematic-theology import rather than an exegesis of the specific canonical phrase.
avenues.avenue: The sukkhavipassaka / suddhavipassanāyānika 'dry-insight practitioner' as a named commentarial person-type, and the paññāvimutta arahant 'without the formless attainments'
avenues.rationale: The commentary's recognition of a 'dry' practitioner type (and the canonical paññāvimutta who lacks the eight liberations, SN 12.70 Susīma) is the personnel-side of the concentration-floor question. If the tradition names a person who attains by insight alone, it has implicitly set the floor below jhāna for SOME attainers — and the commentary's gloss on whether that person can be a sotāpanna (vs. only an arahant) bounds the answer for the lower fruits.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: A commentarial classification stating that the sukkhavipassaka attains the lower paths without jhāna shows the floor was officially lowered for the lower fruits; one restricting dry attainment to arahantship-via-prior-paths would push the floor back up.
avenues.inference_path: If 'sukkhavipassaka' is attested but always glossed as still possessing khaṇika/upacāra, the 'dry' label is relative (dry of formless/absorption, not of all concentration) — narrowing the modern 'bare insight = no concentration' claim. The Susīmasutta commentary (SN-a on SN 12.70) is the canonical-bridge locus: how it glosses the paññāvimuttas who deny the formless attainments determines whether the canon's own dry-arahant is read as jhāna-less or merely formless-less. A near-absence of the term in the aṭṭhakathā but presence in ṭīkā again dates the construct.

avenues.lens: LENS 10 — How the commentary (aṭṭhakathā/ṭīkā) itself fills the canonical gap: the gloss targets where the post-canonical layer specifies what the trainee's "limited measure" of concentration (samādhismiṃ mattaso kārī) actually IS, and whether it supplies a sub-jhānic floor (upacāra/khaṇika/vipassanā/magga/phala samādhi) for the lower-path attainer. The whole evidential value here is that the canon gives a gradient but no quantum; the commentary is the layer that DOES name a quantum, so every commentarial gloss on the measure-phrase, on the sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī's concentration, and on the four named sub-jhānic concentrations is direct evidence of how the tradition resolved the underdetermination — and a near-absence (e.g. if upacāra never co-occurs with the trainee-grading pericope) is itself a finding that the "access concentration" floor is a systematic-theology import rather than an exegesis of the specific canonical phrase.
avenues.avenue: The samatha-pubbaṅgama / vipassanā-pubbaṅgama and the AN 4.170 (Yuganaddha) four-modes commentary — how the tradition handles the insight-led and yoked vehicles
avenues.rationale: AN 4.170 names four ways to arahantship including 'insight preceded by serenity' and 'serenity preceded by insight'. The Manorathapūraṇī on this sutta must explain what concentration the insight-first practitioner has when the path arises. This is the canon's own admission of multiple sequencings, and the commentary's gloss is where the dry-path concentration-floor for that mode is specified.
avenues.what_a_hit_shows: The AN-a gloss on 'vipassanā-pubbaṅgama' — whether it grants the practitioner upacāra/khaṇika at the path-moment, or back-fills jhāna — names the floor for the insight-led path.
avenues.inference_path: If the commentary says even the vipassanā-pubbaṅgama generates jhāna at the moment the path arises (tying back to magga-samādhi above), then the canon's four modes all terminate in jhānic concentration per the commentary — strong evidence the tradition never truly endorsed a jhāna-LESS attainment, only a jhāna-LATER one. If instead it allows the insight-led worker to reach the path on khaṇika alone, the floor is officially momentary. Either way the AN 4.170 gloss is decisive and rarely cited in the modern debate.
